{{神社| 名称=桜山八幡宮| 所在地=岐阜県高山市桜町178| 祭神=応神天皇| 社格=県社・別表神社| 例祭=[[10月9日]]・[[10月10日|10日]]（高山祭）| 神事=式年大祭}}
'''桜山八幡宮'''（さくらやまはちまんぐう）は、[[岐阜県]][[高山市]]にある[[神社]]である。秋の例祭は、春の日枝神社の例祭とともに[[高山祭]]として知られる。
[[応神天皇]]（[[八幡神]]）を主祭神とし、相殿に熱田大神・香椎大神を祀る。
==歴史== [[仁徳天皇]]65年、飛騨国に二つの顔を持つ宿儺（すくな）という賊が現れ、それを討伐するために派遣された難波根子武振熊命が戦勝祈願のために先代の応神天皇を祀ったのに始まると伝える。また、[[聖武天皇]]が諸国に設けた護国八幡だという説もある。
[[江戸時代]]に[[金森氏]]が当地の領主となったとき[[氏神]]として当社を保護し、[[神通川|宮川]]以北の住民を氏子とした。
==施設== 境内に高山屋台会館があり、高山祭の屋台が通年展示されている。
==外部リンク== *[http://www.hida-hachiman.org 櫻山八幡宮]（公式サイト）
==関連項目== {{神道 横}} [[Category:岐阜県の神社|ふくやまはちまんくう]] [[Category:府県社|ふくやまはちまんくう]] [[Category:別表神社|ふくやまはちまんくう]] [[Category:八幡宮|ふくやまはちまんくう]] {{shinto-stub}}

[[応神天皇]]（[[八幡神]]）を主祭神とし、相殿に熱田大神・香椎大神を祀る。
==歴史== [[仁徳天皇]]65年、飛騨国に二つの顔を持つ[[両面宿儺|宿儺]]（すくな）という賊が現れ、それを討伐するために派遣された難波根子武振熊命が戦勝祈願のために先代の応神天皇を祀ったのに始まると伝える。また、[[聖武天皇]]が諸国に設けた護国八幡だという説もある。
[[江戸時代]]に[[金森氏]]が当地の領主となったとき[[氏神]]として当社を保護し、[[神通川|宮川]]以北の住民を氏子とした。

==外部リンク== *[http://www.hida-hachiman.org 櫻山八幡宮]（公式サイト）

[[八幡神|八幡大神]]（[[応神天皇]]）を主祭神とし、相殿に熱田大神（[[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊命]]）・香椎大神（[[仲哀天皇]]・[[神功皇后]]）を祀る。

{{神社| 名称=櫻山八幡宮| 所在地=岐阜県高山市桜町178| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|8|53.56|N|137|15|39.27|E|scale:30000}}| 祭神=応神天皇| 社格=県社・別表神社| 例祭=[[10月9日]]・[[10月10日|10日]]（高山祭）| 神事=式年大祭| 画像=[[ファイル:Sakurayama-hachimangu Takayama Gifu pref04s3s3990.jpg|300px]]}}
'''桜山八幡宮'''（さくらやまはちまんぐう）（正字は'''櫻山八幡宮'''）は、[[岐阜県]][[高山市]]にある[[神社]]である。秋の例祭は、春の[[日枝神社 (高山市)|日枝神社]]の例祭とともに[[高山祭]]として知られる。
[[八幡神|八幡大神]]（[[応神天皇]]）を主祭神とし、相殿に熱田大神（[[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊命]]）・香椎大神（[[仲哀天皇]]・[[神功皇后]]）を祀る。
==歴史== [[仁徳天皇]]65年、飛騨国に二つの顔を持つ[[両面宿儺|宿儺]]（すくな）という賊が現れ、それを討伐するために派遣された難波根子武振熊命が戦勝祈願のために先代の応神天皇を祀ったのに始まると伝える。また、[[聖武天皇]]が諸国に設けた護国八幡だという説もある。
[[江戸時代]]に[[金森氏]]が当地の領主となったとき[[氏神]]として当社を保護し、[[神通川|宮川]]以北の住民を氏子とした。
明治時代に八幡神社に改称し、大正時代に県社になる。桜山八幡宮の名は戦後からという。
==施設== 境内に高山屋台会館があり、秋の高山祭に実際に曳き出される屋台が通年展示されている。近年、高山祭屋台会館と館名変更がなされている。 <gallery> ファイル:Sakurayama-hachimangu Takayama Gifu pref10s3s4020.jpg|神門前 ファイル:Sakurayama-hachimangu Takayama Gifu pref09n4110.jpg|琴平神社 ファイル:Sakurayama-hachimangu Takayama Gifu pref07n4140.jpg|稲荷神社 ファイル:Takayama torii.jpg|一の鳥居 ファイル:Sakurayama-hachiman_ninotorii.jpeg|二の鳥居 </gallery>
==外部リンク== {{Commonscat|Sakurayama-hachiman-gū}} *[http://www.hida-hachiman.org 櫻山八幡宮]（公式サイト）

{{神社| 名称=櫻山八幡宮| 所在地=岐阜県高山市桜町178| 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|8|53.56|N|137|15|39.27|E|scale:30000}}| 祭神=応神天皇| 社格=県社・別表神社| 例祭=[[10月9日]]・[[10月10日|10日]]（高山祭）| 神事=式年大祭| 画像=[[ファイル:Sakurayama-hachimangu Takayama Gifu pref04s3s3990.jpg|300px]]}}
'''桜山八幡宮'''（さくらやまはちまんぐう）（正字は'''櫻山八幡宮'''）は、[[岐阜県]][[高山市]]にある[[神社]]である。秋の例祭は、春の[[日枝神社 (高山市)|日枝神社]]の例祭とともに[[高山祭]]として知られ、国の重要無形民俗文化財に指定されている<ref name="gifuken bunkazai">{{Cite web |date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku-bunka-sports/bunka-geijutsu/bunkazai-zuroku/bunkazai-zuroku/mumin/takayansi/takayamamaturi.html |title=高山祭の屋台行事 |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2013-04-21}}</ref>。
[[八幡神|八幡大神]]（[[応神天皇]]）を主祭神とし、相殿に熱田大神（[[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊命]]）・香椎大神（[[仲哀天皇]]・[[神功皇后]]）を祀る。
==歴史== [[仁徳天皇]]65年、飛騨国に二つの顔を持つ[[両面宿儺|宿儺]]（すくな）という賊が現れ、それを討伐するために派遣された難波根子武振熊命が戦勝祈願のために先代の応神天皇を祀ったのに始まると伝える。また、[[聖武天皇]]が諸国に設けた護国八幡だという説もある。
[[江戸時代]]に[[金森氏]]が当地の領主となったとき[[氏神]]として当社を保護し、[[神通川|宮川]]以北の住民を氏子とした。
明治時代に八幡神社に改称し、昭和７年に県社になる。桜山八幡宮の名は戦後からという。
==施設== 境内に高山屋台会館があり、秋の高山祭に実際に曳き出される屋台が通年展示されている。近年、高山祭屋台会館と館名変更がなされている。 <gallery> ファイル:Sakurayama-hachimangu Takayama Gifu pref10s3s4020.jpg|神門前 ファイル:Sakurayama-hachimangu Takayama Gifu pref09n4110.jpg|琴平神社 ファイル:Sakurayama-hachimangu Takayama Gifu pref07n4140.jpg|稲荷神社 ファイル:Takayama torii.jpg|一の鳥居 ファイル:Sakurayama-hachiman_ninotorii.jpeg|二の鳥居 </gallery>

'''桜山八幡宮'''（さくらやまはちまんぐう）（正字は'''櫻山八幡宮'''）は、[[岐阜県]][[高山市]]にある[[神社]]である。秋の例祭は、春の[[日枝神社 (高山市)|日枝神社]]の例祭とともに[[高山祭]]として知られ、国の重要無形民俗文化財に指定されている<ref name="gifuken bunkazai">{{Cite web |date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazai/17768/mukei-minzoku/takayamamaturi.html |title=高山祭の屋台行事 |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2016-12-11}}</ref>。
[[八幡神|八幡大神]]（[[応神天皇]]）を主祭神とし、相殿に熱田大神（[[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊命]]）・香椎大神（[[仲哀天皇]]・[[神功皇后]]）を祀る。
==歴史== [[仁徳天皇]]65年、飛騨国に二つの顔を持つ[[両面宿儺|宿儺]]（すくな）という賊が現れ、それを討伐するために派遣された[[和珥氏]]の祖・[[武振熊命|難波根子武振熊命]]が戦勝祈願のために先代の応神天皇を祀ったのに始まると伝える。また、[[聖武天皇]]が諸国に設けた護国八幡だという説もある。
[[江戸時代]]に[[金森氏]]が当地の領主となったとき[[氏神]]として当社を保護し、[[宮川 (岐阜県)|宮川]]以北の住民を氏子とした。
明治時代に八幡神社に改称し、昭和７年に県社になる。桜山八幡宮の名は戦後からという。
==施設== 境内に高山屋台会館があり、秋の高山祭に実際に曳き出される屋台が通年展示されている。近年、高山祭屋台会館と館名変更がなされている。 <gallery> ファイル:Sakurayama-hachimangu Takayama Gifu pref10s3s4020.jpg|神門前 ファイル:Sakurayama-hachimangu Takayama Gifu pref09n4110.jpg|琴平神社 ファイル:Sakurayama-hachimangu Takayama Gifu pref07n4140.jpg|稲荷神社 ファイル:Takayama torii.jpg|一の鳥居 ファイル:Sakurayama-hachiman_ninotorii.jpeg|二の鳥居 </gallery>
== 境内社 == *秋葉神社 *:祭神・[[火産霊神|火結神]] *天満神社 *:祭神・[[菅原道真公]] *稲荷神社 *:祭神・[[倉稲魂命|倉稲霊神]]、[[猿田彦神]]、[[松尾大社|松尾神]] *琴平神社 *:祭神・[[大物主神]]、[[崇徳天皇]]、[[大己貴神]]、[[少彦名命|少名彦名神]] *照前神社 *:祭神・[[武振熊命|浪速根子武振熊命]]
== 文化財 == *高山祭の屋台行事（国の[[重要無形民俗文化財]]）<ref name="gifuken bunkazai"/> *八幡宮御輿及び御輿庫（市指定有形民俗文化財）<ref>{{Cite web |date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/kurashi/1000021/1000119/1000847/1000915/1004125.html|title=民俗文化財 |publisher=高山市 |accessdate=2016-12-11}}</ref>

== 境内社 == *秋葉神社 *:祭神・[[火産霊神|火結神]] *天満神社 *:祭神・[[菅原道真公]] *稲荷神社 *:祭神・[[倉稲魂命|倉稲霊神]]、[[猿田彦神]]、[[松尾大社|松尾神]] *琴平神社 *:祭神・[[大物主神]]、[[崇徳天皇]]、[[大己貴神]]、[[少彦名命|少名彦名神]] *照前神社 *:祭神・[[武振熊命|浪速根子武振熊命]]
== 文化財 == *高山祭の屋台行事（国の[[重要無形民俗文化財]]）<ref name="gifuken bunkazai"/> *八幡宮御輿及び御輿庫（市指定有形民俗文化財）<ref>{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/kurashi/1000021/1000119/1000847/1000915/1004125.html|title=民俗文化財 |publisher=高山市 |accessdate=2016-12-11}}</ref>

{{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さくらやまはちまんくう}} [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:高山市の歴史]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧県社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:八幡宮]] [[Category:区市町村指定有形民俗文化財]] [[Category:高山市の建築物]]

{{神社 | 名称=櫻山八幡宮 | 所在地=岐阜県高山市桜町178 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|8|53.56|N|137|15|39.27|E|scale:30000}} | 祭神=応神天皇 | 社格=県社・別表神社・[[金幣社]] | 例祭=[[10月9日]]・[[10月10日|10日]]（高山祭） | 神事=式年大祭 | 画像=[[ファイル:Sakurayama-hachimangu Takayama Gifu pref04s3s3990.jpg|300px]] }}
'''桜山八幡宮'''（さくらやまはちまんぐう）（正字は'''櫻山八幡宮'''）は、[[岐阜県]][[高山市]]にある[[神社]]である。秋の例祭は、春の[[日枝神社 (高山市)|日枝神社]]の例祭とともに[[高山祭]]として知られ、国の重要無形民俗文化財に指定されている<ref name="gifuken bunkazai">{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazai/17768/mukei-minzoku/takayamamaturi.html |title=高山祭の屋台行事 |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2016-12-11}}</ref>。
[[八幡神|八幡大神]]（[[応神天皇]]）を主祭神とし、相殿に熱田大神（[[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊命]]）・香椎大神（[[仲哀天皇]]・[[神功皇后]]）を祀る。
==歴史== [[仁徳天皇]]65年、飛騨国に二つの顔を持つ[[両面宿儺|宿儺]]（すくな）という賊が現れ、それを討伐するために派遣された[[和珥氏]]の祖・[[武振熊命|難波根子武振熊命]]が戦勝祈願のために先代の応神天皇を祀ったのに始まると伝える。また、[[聖武天皇]]が諸国に設けた護国八幡だという説もある。
[[江戸時代]]に[[金森氏]]が当地の領主となったとき[[氏神]]として当社を保護し、[[宮川 (岐阜県)|宮川]]以北の住民を氏子とした。
明治時代に八幡神社に改称し、昭和７年に県社になる。桜山八幡宮の名は戦後からという。
==施設== 境内に[[高山祭屋台会館]]があり、秋の高山祭に実際に曳き出される屋台が通年展示されている。 <gallery> ファイル:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref10s3s4020.jpg|神門前 ファイル:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref09n4110.jpg|琴平神社 ファイル:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref07n4140.jpg|稲荷神社 ファイル:Takayama_torii.jpg|大鳥居 ファイル:Sakurayama-hachiman_ninotorii.jpeg|二の鳥居 </gallery>
== 境内社 == *秋葉神社 *:祭神・[[火産霊神|火結神]] *天満神社 *:祭神・[[菅原道真公]] *稲荷神社 *:祭神・[[倉稲魂命|倉稲霊神]]、[[猿田彦神]]、[[松尾大社|松尾神]] *琴平神社 *:祭神・[[大物主神]]、[[崇徳天皇]]、[[大己貴神]]、[[少彦名命|少名彦名神]] *照前神社 *:祭神・[[武振熊命|浪速根子武振熊命]]
== 文化財 == *高山祭の屋台行事（国の[[重要無形民俗文化財]]）<ref name="gifuken bunkazai"/> *八幡宮御輿及び御輿庫（市指定有形民俗文化財）<ref>{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/kurashi/1000021/1000119/1000847/1000915/1004125.html|title=民俗文化財 |publisher=高山市 |accessdate=2016-12-11}}</ref>
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
==外部リンク== {{Commonscat|Sakurayama-hachiman-gū}} *[http://www.sakurayamahachiman.jimdo.com/ 櫻山八幡宮]

[[八幡神|八幡大神]]（[[応神天皇]]）を主祭神とし、相殿に熱田大神（[[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊命]]）・香椎大神（[[仲哀天皇]]・[[神功皇后]]）を祀る。 ==歴史== [[仁徳天皇]]65年、飛騨国に二つの顔を持つ[[両面宿儺|宿儺]]（すくな）という賊が現れ、それを討伐するために派遣された[[和珥氏]]の祖・[[武振熊命|難波根子武振熊命]]が戦勝祈願のために先代の応神天皇を祀ったのに始まると伝える。また、[[聖武天皇]]が諸国に設けた護国八幡だという説もある。 [[江戸時代]]に[[金森氏]]が当地の領主となったとき[[氏神]]として当社を保護し、[[宮川 (岐阜県)|宮川]]以北の住民を氏子とした。 明治時代に八幡神社に改称し、昭和７年に県社になる。桜山八幡宮の名は戦後からという。 ==施設== 境内に[[高山祭屋台会館]]があり、秋の高山祭に実際に曳き出される屋台が通年展示されている。 ファイル:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref10s3s4020.jpg|神門前 ファイル:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref09n4110.jpg|琴平神社 ファイル:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref07n4140.jpg|稲荷神社 ファイル:Takayama_torii.jpg|大鳥居 ファイル:Sakurayama-hachiman_ninotorii.jpeg|二の鳥居 == 境内社 == *秋葉神社 *:祭神・[[火産霊神|火結神]] *天満神社 *:祭神・[[菅原道真公]] *稲荷神社 *:祭神・[[倉稲魂命|倉稲霊神]]、[[猿田彦神]]、[[松尾大社|松尾神]] *琴平神社 *:祭神・[[大物主神]]、[[崇徳天皇]]、[[大己貴神]]、[[少彦名命|少名彦名神]] *照前神社 *:祭神・[[武振熊命|浪速根子武振熊命]] == 文化財 == revision.text:
{{Shrine | name = Sakurayama Hachimangu | image = [[File:Sakurayama-hachimangu Takayama Gifu pref04s3s3990.jpg|250px]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E}}}} | location = [[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], [[Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], Sakuramachi 178 | coordinates = {{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E|scale:30000}} | deities = [[Emperor Ōjin]] | rank = Former Prefectural Shrine, [[Bekkaku Shrine|Bekkaku Shrine]], Kanpei-sha | annual_festival = {{ill|October 9|ja|10月9日}}, {{ill|October 10|ja|10月10日}} (Takayama Festival) | ceremonies = Shikinen Taisai }} '''Sakurayama Hachimangu''' (さくらやまはちまんぐう) (officially '''櫻山八幡宮''') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], [[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], Japan. The autumn festival, along with the spring festival of {{ill|Hie Shrine (Takayama)|ja|日枝神社 (高山市)}}, is known as the {{ill|Takayama Festival|ja|高山祭}}, which is designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazai/17768/mukei-minzoku/takayamamaturi.html |title=高山祭の屋台行事 |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2016-12-11}}. The main deity is the Hachiman deity, {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, and the enshrined deities include Atsuta Okami ({{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊命}}) and Kashi Okami ({{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}). == History == It is said that in the 65th year of the reign of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}, a bandit with two faces named {{ill|Ryomen Sukuna|ja|両面宿儺}} appeared in Hida Province. He was defeated by {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|武振熊命}}, the ancestor of the Wani clan, who prayed for victory to the former Emperor Ōjin. Another theory is that it was established as a protective Hachiman shrine by {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}} in various provinces. In the [[Edo period]], the Kanamori clan, the local lords, protected the shrine as their {{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神}} and made the residents north of the {{ill|Miyagawa (Gifu)|ja|宮川}} their parishioners. In the Meiji period, it was renamed Hachiman Shrine and became a Prefectural Shrine in 1932. The name Sakurayama Hachimangu has been used since after World War II. == Facilities == The {{ill|Takayama Festival Yatai Kaikan|ja|高山祭屋台会館}} is located on the shrine grounds, where the festival floats used in the autumn Takayama Festival are displayed throughout the year. File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref10s3s4020.jpg|Shrine gate File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref09n4110.jpg|Kotohira Shrine File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref07n4140.jpg|Inari Shrine File:Takayama_torii.jpg|Great Torii File:Sakurayama-hachiman_ninotorii.jpeg|Second Torii == Shrines within the Grounds == * Akiba Shrine ** Deity: {{ill|Kagutsuchi|ja|火産霊神}} * Tenman Shrine ** Deity: {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} * Inari Shrine ** Deities: {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲霊神}}, {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦神}}, {{ill|Matsunoo Shrine|ja|松尾神}} * Kotohira Shrine ** Deities: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴神}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少名彦名神}} * Terumae Shrine ** Deity: {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|浪速根子武振熊命}} == Cultural Properties == * Takayama Festival Floats Event (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * Mikoshi and Mikoshi Storehouse of Hachiman Shrine (Municipal Tangible Folk Cultural Property){{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/kurashi/1000021/1000119/1000847/1000915/1004125.html|title=民俗文化財 |publisher=高山市 |accessdate=2016-12-11}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Sakurayama-hachiman-gū}} * [http://www.sakurayamahachiman.jimdo.com/ Sakurayama Hachimangu] {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さくらやまはちまんくう}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Takayama, Gifu]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Bekkaku shrines]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Municipal tangible folk cultural properties]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Takayama, Gifu]]

{{Shrine | name = Sakurayama Hachimangu | image = [[File:Sakurayama-hachimangu Takayama Gifu pref04s3s3990.jpg|250px]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E}}}} | location = [[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], [[Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], Sakuramachi 178 | coordinates = {{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E|scale:30000}} | deities = [[Emperor Ōjin]] | rank = Former Prefectural Shrine, [[Bekkaku Shrine|Bekkaku Shrine]], Kanpei-sha | annual_festival = {{ill|October 9|ja|10月9日}}, {{ill|October 10|ja|10月10日}} (Takayama Festival) | ceremonies = Shikinen Taisai }} '''Sakurayama Hachimangu''' (さくらやまはちまんぐう) (officially '''櫻山八幡宮''') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], [[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], Japan. The autumn festival, along with the spring festival of {{ill|Hie Shrine (Takayama)|ja|日枝神社 (高山市)}}, is known as the {{ill|Takayama Festival|ja|高山祭}}, which is designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazai/17768/mukei-minzoku/takayamamaturi.html |title=高山祭の屋台行事 |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2016-12-11}}. The main deity is the Hachiman deity, {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, and the enshrined deities include Atsuta Okami ({{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊命}}) and Kashi Okami ({{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}). == History == It is said that in the 65th year of the reign of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}, a bandit with two faces named {{ill|Ryomen Sukuna|ja|両面宿儺}} appeared in Hida Province. He was defeated by {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|武振熊命}}, the ancestor of the Wani clan, who prayed for victory to the former Emperor Ōjin. Another theory is that it was established as a protective Hachiman shrine by {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}} in various provinces. In the [[Edo period]], the Kanamori clan, the local lords, protected the shrine as their {{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神}} and made the residents north of the {{ill|Miyagawa (Gifu)|ja|宮川}} their parishioners. In the Meiji period, it was renamed Hachiman Shrine and became a Prefectural Shrine in 1932. The name Sakurayama Hachimangu has been used since after World War II. == Facilities == The {{ill|Takayama Festival Yatai Kaikan|ja|高山祭屋台会館}} is located on the shrine grounds, where the festival floats used in the autumn Takayama Festival are displayed throughout the year. File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref10s3s4020.jpg|Shrine gate File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref09n4110.jpg|Kotohira Shrine File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref07n4140.jpg|Inari Shrine File:Takayama_torii.jpg|Great Torii File:Sakurayama-hachiman_ninotorii.jpeg|Second Torii == Shrines within the Grounds == * Akiba Shrine ** Deity: {{ill|Kagutsuchi|ja|火産霊神}} * Tenman Shrine ** Deity: {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} * Inari Shrine ** Deities: {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲霊神}}, {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦神}}, {{ill|Matsunoo Shrine|ja|松尾神}} * Kotohira Shrine ** Deities: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴神}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少名彦名神}} * Terumae Shrine ** Deity: {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|浪速根子武振熊命}} == Cultural Properties == * Takayama Festival Floats Event (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * Mikoshi and Mikoshi Storehouse of Hachiman Shrine (Municipal Tangible Folk Cultural Property){{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/kurashi/1000021/1000119/1000847/1000915/1004125.html|title=民俗文化財 |publisher=高山市 |accessdate=2016-12-11}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Sakurayama-hachiman-gū}} * [http://www.sakurayamahachiman.jimdo.com/ Sakurayama Hachimangu] {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さくらやまはちまんくう}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Takayama, Gifu]] [[Category:Former prefectural shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Municipal tangible folk cultural properties]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Takayama, Gifu]]

{{Shrine | name = Sakurayama Hachimangu | image = [[File:Sakurayama-hachimangu Takayama Gifu pref04s3s3990.jpg|250px]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E}}}} | location = [[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], [[Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], Sakuramachi 178 | coordinates = {{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E|scale:30000}} | deities = [[Emperor Ōjin]] | rank = Former Prefectural Shrine, [[Bekkaku Shrine|Bekkaku Shrine]], Kanpei-sha | annual_festival = {{ill|October 9|ja|10月9日}}, {{ill|October 10|ja|10月10日}} (Takayama Festival) | ceremonies = Shikinen Taisai }} '''Sakurayama Hachimangu''' (さくらやまはちまんぐう) (officially '''櫻山八幡宮''') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], [[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], Japan. The autumn festival, along with the spring festival of {{ill|Hie Shrine (Takayama)|ja|日枝神社 (高山市)}}, is known as the {{ill|Takayama Festival|ja|高山祭}}, which is designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazai/17768/mukei-minzoku/takayamamaturi.html |title=高山祭の屋台行事 |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2016-12-11}}. The main deity is the Hachiman deity, {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, and the enshrined deities include Atsuta Okami ({{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊命}}) and Kashi Okami ({{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}). == History == It is said that in the 65th year of the reign of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}, a bandit with two faces named {{ill|Ryomen Sukuna|ja|両面宿儺}} appeared in Hida Province. He was defeated by {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|武振熊命}}, the ancestor of the Wani clan, who prayed for victory to the former Emperor Ōjin. Another theory is that it was established as a protective Hachiman shrine by {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}} in various provinces. In the [[Edo period]], the Kanamori clan, the local lords, protected the shrine as their {{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神}} and made the residents north of the {{ill|Miyagawa (Gifu)|ja|宮川}} their parishioners. In the Meiji period, it was renamed Hachiman Shrine and became a Prefectural Shrine in 1932. The name Sakurayama Hachimangu has been used since after World War II. == Facilities == The {{ill|Takayama Festival Yatai Kaikan|ja|高山祭屋台会館}} is located on the shrine grounds, where the festival floats used in the autumn Takayama Festival are displayed throughout the year. File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref10s3s4020.jpg|Shrine gate File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref09n4110.jpg|Kotohira Shrine File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref07n4140.jpg|Inari Shrine File:Takayama_torii.jpg|Great Torii File:Sakurayama-hachiman_ninotorii.jpeg|Second Torii == Shrines within the Grounds == * Akiba Shrine ** Deity: {{ill|Kagutsuchi|ja|火産霊神}} * Tenman Shrine ** Deity: {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} * Inari Shrine ** Deities: {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲霊神}}, {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦神}}, {{ill|Matsunoo Shrine|ja|松尾神}} * Kotohira Shrine ** Deities: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴神}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少名彦名神}} * Terumae Shrine ** Deity: {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|浪速根子武振熊命}} == Cultural Properties == * Takayama Festival Floats Event (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * Mikoshi and Mikoshi Storehouse of Hachiman Shrine (Municipal Tangible Folk Cultural Property){{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/kurashi/1000021/1000119/1000847/1000915/1004125.html|title=民俗文化財 |publisher=高山市 |accessdate=2016-12-11}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Sakurayama-hachiman-gū}} * [http://www.sakurayamahachiman.jimdo.com/ Sakurayama Hachimangu] {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さくらやまはちまんくう}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Takayama, Gifu]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Municipal tangible folk cultural properties]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Takayama, Gifu]]

{{Shrine | name = Sakurayama Hachimangu | image = [[File:Sakurayama-hachimangu Takayama Gifu pref04s3s3990.jpg|250px]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E}}}} | location = [[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], [[Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], Sakuramachi 178 | coordinates = {{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E|scale:30000}} | deities = [[Emperor Ōjin]] | rank = Former Prefectural Shrine, [[Bekkaku Shrine|Bekkaku Shrine]], Kanpei-sha | annual_festival = {{ill|October 9|ja|10月9日}}, {{ill|October 10|ja|10月10日}} (Takayama Festival) | ceremonies = Shikinen Taisai }} '''Sakurayama Hachimangu''' (さくらやまはちまんぐう) (officially '''櫻山八幡宮''') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], [[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], Japan. The main deity is the Hachiman deity, {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, and the enshrined deities include Atsuta Okami ({{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊命}}) and Kashi Okami ({{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}). == History == It is said that in the 65th year of the reign of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}, a bandit with two faces named {{ill|Ryomen Sukuna|ja|両面宿儺}} appeared in Hida Province. He was defeated by {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|武振熊命}}, the ancestor of the Wani clan, who prayed for victory to the former Emperor Ōjin. Another theory is that it was established as a protective Hachiman shrine by {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}} in various provinces. In the [[Edo period]], the Kanamori clan, the local lords, protected the shrine as their {{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神}} and made the residents north of the {{ill|Miyagawa (Gifu)|ja|宮川}} their parishioners. In the Meiji period, it was renamed Hachiman Shrine and became a Prefectural Shrine in 1932. The name Sakurayama Hachimangu has been used since after World War II. == Facilities == The {{ill|Takayama Festival Yatai Kaikan|ja|高山祭屋台会館}} is located on the shrine grounds, where the festival floats used in the autumn Takayama Festival are displayed throughout the year. File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref10s3s4020.jpg|Shrine gate File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref09n4110.jpg|Kotohira Shrine File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref07n4140.jpg|Inari Shrine File:Takayama_torii.jpg|Great Torii File:Sakurayama-hachiman_ninotorii.jpeg|Second Torii == Shrines within the Grounds == * Akiba Shrine ** Deity: {{ill|Kagutsuchi|ja|火産霊神}} * Tenman Shrine ** Deity: {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} * Inari Shrine ** Deities: {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲霊神}}, {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦神}}, {{ill|Matsunoo Shrine|ja|松尾神}} * Kotohira Shrine ** Deities: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴神}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少名彦名神}} * Terumae Shrine ** Deity: {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|浪速根子武振熊命}} == Cultural Properties == * Takayama Festival Floats Event (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * Mikoshi and Mikoshi Storehouse of Hachiman Shrine (Municipal Tangible Folk Cultural Property){{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/kurashi/1000021/1000119/1000847/1000915/1004125.html|title=民俗文化財 |publisher=高山市 |accessdate=2016-12-11}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Sakurayama-hachiman-gū}} * [http://www.sakurayamahachiman.jimdo.com/ Sakurayama Hachimangu] {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さくらやまはちまんくう}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Takayama, Gifu]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Municipal tangible folk cultural properties]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Takayama, Gifu]] [[Category:Original pages]]

{{神社 | 名称=櫻山八幡宮 | 画像=[[ファイル:Sakurayama-hachimangu Takayama Gifu pref04s3s3990.jpg|250px]]
拝殿 | 所在地=[[岐阜県]][[高山市]]桜町178 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E|scale:30000}} | 祭神=[[応神天皇]] | 社格=旧[[県社]]・[[別表神社]]・[[金幣社]] | 例祭=[[10月9日]]・[[10月10日|10日]]（高山祭） | 神事=式年大祭 | 地図={{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=櫻山八幡宮|coordinates= {{coord2|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E}}}} }} '''桜山八幡宮'''（さくらやまはちまんぐう）（正字は'''櫻山八幡宮'''）は、[[岐阜県]][[高山市]]にある[[神社]]である。秋の例祭は、春の[[日枝神社 (高山市)|日枝神社]]の例祭とともに[[高山祭]]として知られ、国の重要無形民俗文化財に指定されている{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazai/17768/mukei-minzoku/takayamamaturi.html |title=高山祭の屋台行事 |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2016-12-11}}。 [[八幡神|八幡大神]]（[[応神天皇]]）を主祭神とし、相殿に熱田大神（[[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊命]]）・香椎大神（[[仲哀天皇]]・[[神功皇后]]）を祀る。 ==歴史== [[仁徳天皇]]65年、飛騨国に二つの顔を持つ[[両面宿儺|宿儺]]（すくな）という賊が現れ、それを討伐するために派遣された[[和珥氏]]の祖・[[武振熊命|難波根子武振熊命]]が戦勝祈願のために先代の応神天皇を祀ったのに始まると伝える。また、[[聖武天皇]]が諸国に設けた護国八幡だという説もある。 [[江戸時代]]に[[金森氏]]が当地の領主となったとき[[氏神]]として当社を保護し、[[宮川 (岐阜県)|宮川]]以北の住民を氏子とした。 明治時代に八幡神社に改称し、昭和７年に県社になる。桜山八幡宮の名は戦後からという。 ==施設== 境内に[[高山祭屋台会館]]があり、秋の高山祭に実際に曳き出される屋台が通年展示されている。 ファイル:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref10s3s4020.jpg|神門前 ファイル:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref09n4110.jpg|琴平神社 ファイル:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref07n4140.jpg|稲荷神社 ファイル:Takayama_torii.jpg|大鳥居 ファイル:Sakurayama-hachiman_ninotorii.jpeg|二の鳥居 == 境内社 == *秋葉神社 *:祭神・[[火産霊神|火結神]] *天満神社 *:祭神・[[菅原道真公]] *稲荷神社 *:祭神・[[倉稲魂命|倉稲霊神]]、[[猿田彦神]]、[[松尾大社|松尾神]] *琴平神社 *:祭神・[[大物主神]]、[[崇徳天皇]]、[[大己貴神]]、[[少彦名命|少名彦名神]] *照前神社 *:祭神・[[武振熊命|浪速根子武振熊命]] == 文化財 == *高山祭の屋台行事（国の[[重要無形民俗文化財]]） *八幡宮御輿及び御輿庫（市指定有形民俗文化財）{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/kurashi/1000021/1000119/1000847/1000915/1004125.html|title=民俗文化財 |publisher=高山市 |accessdate=2016-12-11}} == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} ==外部リンク== {{Commonscat|Sakurayama-hachiman-gū}} *[http://www.sakurayamahachiman.jimdo.com/ 櫻山八幡宮] {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さくらやまはちまんくう}} [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:高山市の歴史]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧県社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:八幡宮]] [[Category:区市町村指定有形民俗文化財]] [[Category:高山市の建築物]]

revision.comment: 鳥居の説明が間違っていました。

{{Shrine | name = Sakurayama Hachimangu | image = [[File:Sakurayama-hachimangu Takayama Gifu pref04s3s3990.jpg|250px]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E}}}} | location = [[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], [[Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], Sakuramachi 178 | coordinates = {{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E|scale:30000}} | deities = [[Emperor Ōjin]] | rank = Former Prefectural Shrine, [[Bekkaku Shrine|Bekkaku Shrine]], Kanpei-sha | annual_festival = {{ill|October 9|ja|10月9日}}, {{ill|October 10|ja|10月10日}} (Takayama Festival) | ceremonies = Shikinen Taisai }} '''Sakurayama Hachimangu''' (さくらやまはちまんぐう) (officially '''櫻山八幡宮''') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], [[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], Japan. The autumn festival, along with the spring festival of {{ill|Hie Shrine (Takayama)|ja|日枝神社 (高山市)}}, is known as the {{ill|Takayama Festival|ja|高山祭}}, which is designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazai/17768/mukei-minzoku/takayamamaturi.html |title=高山祭の屋台行事 |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2016-12-11}}. The main deity is the Hachiman deity, {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, and the enshrined deities include Atsuta Okami ({{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊命}}) and Kashi Okami ({{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}). == History == It is said that in the 65th year of the reign of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}, a bandit with two faces named {{ill|Ryomen Sukuna|ja|両面宿儺}} appeared in Hida Province. He was defeated by {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|武振熊命}}, the ancestor of the Wani clan, who prayed for victory to the former Emperor Ōjin. Another theory is that it was established as a protective Hachiman shrine by {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}} in various provinces. In the [[Edo period]], the Kanamori clan, the local lords, protected the shrine as their {{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神}} and made the residents north of the {{ill|Miyagawa (Gifu)|ja|宮川}} their parishioners. In the Meiji period, it was renamed Hachiman Shrine and became a Prefectural Shrine in 1932. The name Sakurayama Hachimangu has been used since after World War II. == Facilities == The {{ill|Takayama Festival Yatai Kaikan|ja|高山祭屋台会館}} is located on the shrine grounds, where the festival floats used in the autumn Takayama Festival are displayed throughout the year. File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref10s3s4020.jpg|Shrine gate File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref09n4110.jpg|Kotohira Shrine File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref07n4140.jpg|Inari Shrine File:Takayama_torii.jpg|Great Torii File:Sakurayama-hachiman_ninotorii.jpeg|Second Torii == Shrines within the Grounds == * Akiba Shrine ** Deity: {{ill|Kagutsuchi|ja|火産霊神}} * Tenman Shrine ** Deity: {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} * Inari Shrine ** Deities: {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲霊神}}, {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦神}}, {{ill|Matsunoo Shrine|ja|松尾神}} * Kotohira Shrine ** Deities: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴神}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少名彦名神}} * Terumae Shrine ** Deity: {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|浪速根子武振熊命}} == Cultural Properties == * Takayama Festival Floats Event (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * Mikoshi and Mikoshi Storehouse of Hachiman Shrine (Municipal Tangible Folk Cultural Property){{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/kurashi/1000021/1000119/1000847/1000915/1004125.html|title=民俗文化財 |publisher=高山市 |accessdate=2016-12-11}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Sakurayama-hachiman-gū}} * [http://www.sakurayamahachiman.jimdo.com/ Sakurayama Hachimangu] {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さくらやまはちまんくう}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Takayama, Gifu]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Uncategorized_Shinto_shrines]][[en:Sakurayama Hachimangu]][[Category:Uncategorized_Shinto_shrines]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Municipal tangible folk cultural properties]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Takayama, Gifu]] [[Category:Original pages]]

{{Shrine | name = Sakurayama Hachimangu | image = [[File:Sakurayama-hachimangu Takayama Gifu pref04s3s3990.jpg|250px]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E}}}} | location = [[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], [[Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], Sakuramachi 178 | coordinates = {{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E|scale:30000}} | deities = [[Emperor Ōjin]] | rank = Former Prefectural Shrine, [[Bekkaku Shrine|Bekkaku Shrine]], Kanpei-sha | annual_festival = {{ill|October 9|ja|10月9日}}, {{ill|October 10|ja|10月10日}} (Takayama Festival) | ceremonies = Shikinen Taisai }} '''Sakurayama Hachimangu''' (さくらやまはちまんぐう) (officially '''櫻山八幡宮''') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], [[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], Japan. The autumn festival, along with the spring festival of {{ill|Hie Shrine (Takayama)|ja|日枝神社 (高山市)}}, is known as the {{ill|Takayama Festival|ja|高山祭}}, which is designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazai/17768/mukei-minzoku/takayamamaturi.html |title=高山祭の屋台行事 |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2016-12-11}}. The main deity is the Hachiman deity, {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, and the enshrined deities include Atsuta Okami ({{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊命}}) and Kashi Okami ({{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}). == History == It is said that in the 65th year of the reign of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}, a bandit with two faces named {{ill|Ryomen Sukuna|ja|両面宿儺}} appeared in Hida Province. He was defeated by {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|武振熊命}}, the ancestor of the Wani clan, who prayed for victory to the former Emperor Ōjin. Another theory is that it was established as a protective Hachiman shrine by {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}} in various provinces. In the [[Edo period]], the Kanamori clan, the local lords, protected the shrine as their {{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神}} and made the residents north of the {{ill|Miyagawa (Gifu)|ja|宮川}} their parishioners. In the Meiji period, it was renamed Hachiman Shrine and became a Prefectural Shrine in 1932. The name Sakurayama Hachimangu has been used since after World War II. == Facilities == The {{ill|Takayama Festival Yatai Kaikan|ja|高山祭屋台会館}} is located on the shrine grounds, where the festival floats used in the autumn Takayama Festival are displayed throughout the year. {{Shrine | name = Sakurayama Hachimangu | image = [[File:Sakurayama-hachimangu Takayama Gifu pref04s3s3990.jpg|250px]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E}}}} | location = [[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], [[Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], Sakuramachi 178 | coordinates = {{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E|scale:30000}} | deity = [[Emperor Ōjin]] | rank = Former Prefectural Shrine, [[Bekkaku Shrine|Bekkaku Shrine]], Kanpei-sha | annual_festival = {{ill|October 9|ja|10月9日}}, {{ill|October 10|ja|10月10日}} (Takayama festival) | ceremonies = Shikinen Taisai }} '''Sakurayama Hachimangu''' (さくらやまはちまんぐう) (officially '''櫻山八幡宮''') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], [[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], Japan. The autumn festival, along with the spring festival of {{ill|Hie Shrine (Takayama)|ja|日枝神社 (高山市)}}, is known as the {{ill|Takayama festival|ja|高山祭}}, which is designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazai/17768/mukei-minzoku/takayamamaturi.html |title=高山祭の屋台行事 |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2016-12-11}}. The main deity is the Hachiman deity, {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, and the enshrined deities include Atsuta Okami ({{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊命}}) and Kashi Okami ({{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}). == History == It is said that in the 65th year of the reign of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}, a bandit with two faces named {{ill|Ryomen Sukuna|ja|両面宿儺}} appeared in Hida Province. He was defeated by {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|武振熊命}}, the ancestor of the Wani clan, who prayed for victory to the former Emperor Ōjin. Another theory is that it was established as a protective Hachiman shrine by {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}} in various provinces. In the [[Edo period]], the Kanamori clan, the local lords, protected the shrine as their {{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神}} and made the residents north of the {{ill|Miyagawa (Gifu)|ja|宮川}} their parishioners. In the Meiji period, it was renamed Hachiman Shrine and became a Prefectural Shrine in 1932. The name Sakurayama Hachimangu has been used since after World War II. == Facilities == The {{ill|Takayama festival Yatai Kaikan|ja|高山祭屋台会館}} is located on the shrine grounds, where the festival floats used in the autumn Takayama festival are displayed throughout the year. File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref10s3s4020.jpg|Shrine gate File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref09n4110.jpg|Kotohira Shrine File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref07n4140.jpg|Inari Shrine File:Takayama_torii.jpg|Great Torii File:Sakurayama-hachiman_ninotorii.jpeg|Second Torii == Shrines within the Grounds == * Akiba Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Kagutsuchi|ja|火産霊神}} * Tenman Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} * Inari Shrine ** Deities: {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲霊神}}, {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦神}}, {{ill|Matsunoo Shrine|ja|松尾神}} * Kotohira Shrine ** Deities: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴神}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少名彦名神}} * Terumae Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|浪速根子武振熊命}} == Cultural Properties == * Takayama festival Floats Event (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * Mikoshi and Mikoshi Storehouse of Hachiman Shrine (Municipal Tangible Folk Cultural Property){{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/kurashi/1000021/1000119/1000847/1000915/1004125.html|title=民俗文化財 |publisher=高山市 |accessdate=2016-12-11}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Sakurayama-hachiman-gū}} * [http://www.sakurayamahachiman.jimdo.com/ Sakurayama Hachimangu] {{shinto-stub}} revision.comment: adding religious affiliation to the ones with a broken religious affiliation section
revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sakurayama Hachimangu | image = [[File:Sakurayama-hachimangu Takayama Gifu pref04s3s3990.jpg|250px]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E}}}} | location = [[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], [[Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], Sakuramachi 178 | coordinates = {{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E|scale:30000}} | deity = [[Emperor Ōjin]] | rank = Former Prefectural Shrine, [[Bekkaku Shrine|Bekkaku Shrine]], Kanpei-sha | annual_festival = {{ill|October 9|ja|10月9日}}, {{ill|October 10|ja|10月10日}} (Takayama festival) | ceremonies = Shikinen Taisai }} '''Sakurayama Hachimangu''' (さくらやまはちまんぐう) (officially '''櫻山八幡宮''') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], [[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], Japan. The autumn festival, along with the spring festival of {{ill|Hie Shrine (Takayama)|ja|日枝神社 (高山市)}}, is known as the {{ill|Takayama festival|ja|高山祭}}, which is designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazai/17768/mukei-minzoku/takayamamaturi.html |title=高山祭の屋台行事 |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2016-12-11}}. The main deity is the Hachiman deity, {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, and the enshrined deities include Atsuta Okami ({{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊命}}) and Kashi Okami ({{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}). == History == It is said that in the 65th year of the reign of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}, a bandit with two faces named {{ill|Ryomen Sukuna|ja|両面宿儺}} appeared in Hida Province. He was defeated by {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|武振熊命}}, the ancestor of the Wani clan, who prayed for victory to the former Emperor Ōjin. Another theory is that it was established as a protective Hachiman shrine by {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}} in various provinces. In the [[Edo period]], the Kanamori clan, the local lords, protected the shrine as their {{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神}} and made the residents north of the {{ill|Miyagawa (Gifu)|ja|宮川}} their parishioners. In the Meiji period, it was renamed Hachiman Shrine and became a Prefectural Shrine in 1932. The name Sakurayama Hachimangu has been used since after World War II. == Facilities == The {{ill|Takayama festival Yatai Kaikan|ja|高山祭屋台会館}} is located on the shrine grounds, where the festival floats used in the autumn Takayama festival are displayed throughout the year. File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref10s3s4020.jpg|Shrine gate File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref09n4110.jpg|Kotohira Shrine File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref07n4140.jpg|Inari Shrine File:Takayama_torii.jpg|Great Torii File:Sakurayama-hachiman_ninotorii.jpeg|Second Torii == Shrines within the Grounds == * Akiba Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Kagutsuchi|ja|火産霊神}} * Tenman Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} * Inari Shrine ** Deities: {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲霊神}}, {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦神}}, {{ill|Matsunoo Shrine|ja|松尾神}} * Kotohira Shrine ** Deities: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴神}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少名彦名神}} * Terumae Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|浪速根子武振熊命}} == Cultural Properties == * Takayama festival Floats Event (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * Mikoshi and Mikoshi Storehouse of Hachiman Shrine (Municipal Tangible Folk Cultural Property){{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/kurashi/1000021/1000119/1000847/1000915/1004125.html|title=民俗文化財 |publisher=高山市 |accessdate=2016-12-11}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Sakurayama-hachiman-gū}} * [http://www.sakurayamahachiman.jimdo.com/ Sakurayama Hachimangu] {{shinto-stub}} revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sakurayama Hachimangu | image = [[File:Sakurayama-hachimangu Takayama Gifu pref04s3s3990.jpg|250px]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E}}}} | location = [[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], [[Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], Sakuramachi 178 | coordinates = {{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E|scale:30000}} | deity = [[Emperor Ōjin]] | type = Former Prefectural Shrine, [[Bekkaku Shrine|Bekkaku Shrine]], Kanpei-sha | annual_festival = {{ill|October 9|ja|10月9日}}, {{ill|October 10|ja|10月10日}} (Takayama festival) | ceremonies = Shikinen Taisai }} '''Sakurayama Hachimangu''' (さくらやまはちまんぐう) (officially '''櫻山八幡宮''') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], [[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], Japan. The autumn festival, along with the spring festival of {{ill|Hie Shrine (Takayama)|ja|日枝神社 (高山市)}}, is known as the {{ill|Takayama festival|ja|高山祭}}, which is designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazai/17768/mukei-minzoku/takayamamaturi.html |title=高山祭の屋台行事 |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2016-12-11}}. The main deity is the Hachiman deity, {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, and the enshrined deities include Atsuta Okami ({{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊命}}) and Kashi Okami ({{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}). == History == It is said that in the 65th year of the reign of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}, a bandit with two faces named {{ill|Ryomen Sukuna|ja|両面宿儺}} appeared in Hida Province. He was defeated by {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|武振熊命}}, the ancestor of the Wani clan, who prayed for victory to the former Emperor Ōjin. Another theory is that it was established as a protective Hachiman shrine by {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}} in various provinces. In the [[Edo period]], the Kanamori clan, the local lords, protected the shrine as their {{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神}} and made the residents north of the {{ill|Miyagawa (Gifu)|ja|宮川}} their parishioners. In the Meiji period, it was renamed Hachiman Shrine and became a Prefectural Shrine in 1932. The name Sakurayama Hachimangu has been used since after World War II. == Facilities == The {{ill|Takayama festival Yatai Kaikan|ja|高山祭屋台会館}} is located on the shrine grounds, where the festival floats used in the autumn Takayama festival are displayed throughout the year. File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref10s3s4020.jpg|Shrine gate File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref09n4110.jpg|Kotohira Shrine File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref07n4140.jpg|Inari Shrine File:Takayama_torii.jpg|Great Torii File:Sakurayama-hachiman_ninotorii.jpeg|Second Torii == Shrines within the Grounds == * Akiba Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Kagutsuchi|ja|火産霊神}} * Tenman Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} * Inari Shrine ** Deities: {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲霊神}}, {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦神}}, {{ill|Matsunoo Shrine|ja|松尾神}} * Kotohira Shrine ** Deities: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴神}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少名彦名神}} * Terumae Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|浪速根子武振熊命}} == Cultural Properties == * Takayama festival Floats Event (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * Mikoshi and Mikoshi Storehouse of Hachiman Shrine (Municipal Tangible Folk Cultural Property){{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/kurashi/1000021/1000119/1000847/1000915/1004125.html|title=民俗文化財 |publisher=高山市 |accessdate=2016-12-11}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Sakurayama-hachiman-gū}} * [http://www.sakurayamahachiman.jimdo.com/ Sakurayama Hachimangu] {{shinto-stub}} In the Meiji period, it was renamed Hachiman Shrine and became a Prefectural Shrine in 1932. {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sakurayama Hachimangu | image = [[File:Sakurayama-hachimangu Takayama Gifu pref04s3s3990.jpg|250px]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E}}}} | location = [[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], [[Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], Sakuramachi 178 | coordinates = {{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E|scale:30000}} | deity = [[Emperor Ōjin]] | type = Former Prefectural Shrine, [[Bekkaku Shrine|Bekkaku Shrine]], Kanpei-sha | annual_festival = {{ill|October 9|ja|10月9日}}, {{ill|October 10|ja|10月10日}} (Takayama festival) | ceremonies = Shikinen Taisai }} '''Sakurayama Hachimangu''' (さくらやまはちまんぐう) (officially '''櫻山八幡宮''') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], [[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], Japan. The autumn festival, along with the spring festival of {{ill|Hie Shrine (Takayama)|ja|日枝神社 (高山市)}}, is known as the {{ill|Takayama festival|ja|高山祭}}, which is designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazai/17768/mukei-minzoku/takayamamaturi.html |title=高山祭の屋台行事 |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2016-12-11}}. The main deity is the Hachiman deity, {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, and the enshrined deities include Atsuta Okami ({{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊命}}) and Kashi Okami ({{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}). == History == It is said that in the 65th year of the reign of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}, a bandit with two faces named {{ill|Ryomen Sukuna|ja|両面宿儺}} appeared in Hida Province. He was defeated by {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|武振熊命}}, the ancestor of the Wani clan, who prayed for victory to the former Emperor Ōjin. Another theory is that it was established as a protective Hachiman shrine by {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}} in various provinces. In the [[Edo period]], the Kanamori clan, the local lords, protected the shrine as their {{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神}} and made the residents north of the {{ill|Miyagawa (Gifu)|ja|宮川}} their parishioners. In the Meiji period, it was renamed Hachiman Shrine and became a Prefectural Shrine in 1932. The name Sakurayama Hachimangu has been used since after World War II. == Facilities == The {{ill|Takayama festival Yatai Kaikan|ja|高山祭屋台会館}} is located on the shrine grounds, where the festival floats used in the autumn Takayama festival are displayed throughout the year. {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sakurayama Hachimangu | image = [[File:Sakurayama-hachimangu Takayama Gifu pref04s3s3990.jpg|250px]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E}}}} | location = [[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], [[Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], Sakuramachi 178 | coordinates = {{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E|scale:30000}} | deity = [[Emperor Ōjin]] | type = Former Prefectural Shrine, [[Bekkaku Shrine|Bekkaku Shrine]], Kanpei-sha | annual_festival = {{ill|October 9|ja|10月9日}}, {{ill|October 10|ja|10月10日}} (Takayama festival) | ceremonies = Shikinen Taisai }} '''Sakurayama Hachimangu''' (さくらやまはちまんぐう) (officially '''櫻山八幡宮''') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], [[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], Japan. The autumn festival, along with the spring festival of {{ill|Hie Shrine (Takayama)|ja|日枝神社 (高山市)}}, is known as the {{ill|Takayama festival|ja|高山祭}}, which is designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazai/17768/mukei-minzoku/takayamamaturi.html |title=高山祭の屋台行事 |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2016-12-11}}. The main deity is the Hachiman deity, {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, and the enshrined deities include Atsuta Okami ({{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊命}}) and Kashi Okami ({{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}). == History == It is said that in the 65th year of the reign of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}, a bandit with two faces named {{ill|Ryomen Sukuna|ja|両面宿儺}} appeared in Hida Province. He was defeated by {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|武振熊命}}, the ancestor of the Wani clan, who prayed for victory to the former Emperor Ōjin. Another theory is that it was established as a protective Hachiman shrine by {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}} in various provinces. In the [[Edo period]], the Kanamori clan, the local lords, protected the shrine as their {{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神}} and made the residents north of the {{ill|Miyagawa (Gifu)|ja|宮川}} their parishioners. In the Meiji period, it was renamed Hachiman Shrine and became a Prefectural Shrine in 1932. The name Sakurayama Hachimangu has been used since after World War II. == Facilities == The {{ill|Takayama festival Yatai Kaikan|ja|高山祭屋台会館}} is located on the shrine grounds, where the festival floats used in the autumn Takayama festival are displayed throughout the year. File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref10s3s4020.jpg|Shrine gate File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref09n4110.jpg|Kotohira Shrine File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref07n4140.jpg|Inari Shrine File:Takayama_torii.jpg|Great Torii File:Sakurayama-hachiman_ninotorii.jpeg|Second Torii == Shrines within the Grounds == * Akiba {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sakurayama Hachimangu | image = [[File:Sakurayama-hachimangu Takayama Gifu pref04s3s3990.jpg|250px]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E}}}} | location = [[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], [[Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], Sakuramachi 178 | coordinates = {{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E|scale:30000}} | deity = [[Emperor Ōjin]] | type = Former Prefectural Shrine, [[Bekkaku Shrine|Bekkaku Shrine]], Kanpei-sha | annual_festival = {{ill|October 9|ja|10月9日}}, {{ill|October 10|ja|10月10日}} (Takayama festival) | ceremonies = Shikinen Taisai }} '''Sakurayama Hachimangu''' (さくらやまはちまんぐう) (officially '''櫻山八幡宮''') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], [[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], Japan. The autumn festival, along with the spring festival of {{ill|Hie Shrine (Takayama)|ja|日枝神社 (高山市)}}, is known as the {{ill|Takayama festival|ja|高山祭}}, which is designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazai/17768/mukei-minzoku/takayamamaturi.html |title=高山祭の屋台行事 |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2016-12-11}}. The main deity is the Hachiman deity, {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, and the enshrined deities include Atsuta Okami ({{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊命}}) and Kashi Okami ({{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}). == History == It is said that in the 65th year of the reign of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}, a bandit with two faces named {{ill|Ryomen Sukuna|ja|両面宿儺}} appeared in Hida Province. He was defeated by {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|武振熊命}}, the ancestor of the Wani clan, who prayed for victory to the former Emperor Ōjin. Another theory is that it was established as a protective Hachiman shrine by {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}} in various provinces. In the [[Edo period]], the Kanamori clan, the local lords, protected the shrine as their {{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神}} and made the residents north of the {{ill|Miyagawa (Gifu)|ja|宮川}} their parishioners. In the Meiji period, it was renamed Hachiman Shrine and became a Prefectural Shrine in 1932. The name Sakurayama Hachimangu has been used since after World War II. == Facilities == The {{ill|Takayama festival Yatai Kaikan|ja|高山祭屋台会館}} is located on the shrine grounds, where the festival floats used in the autumn Takayama festival are displayed throughout the year. {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sakurayama Hachimangu | image = [[File:Sakurayama-hachimangu Takayama Gifu pref04s3s3990.jpg|250px]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E}}}} | location = [[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], [[Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], Sakuramachi 178 | coordinates = {{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E|scale:30000}} | deity = [[Emperor Ōjin]] | type = Former Prefectural Shrine, [[Bekkaku Shrine|Bekkaku Shrine]], Kanpei-sha | annual_festival = {{ill|October 9|ja|10月9日}}, {{ill|October 10|ja|10月10日}} (Takayama festival) | ceremonies = Shikinen Taisai }} '''Sakurayama Hachimangu''' (さくらやまはちまんぐう) (officially '''櫻山八幡宮''') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], [[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], Japan. The autumn festival, along with the spring festival of {{ill|Hie Shrine (Takayama)|ja|日枝神社 (高山市)}}, is known as the {{ill|Takayama festival|ja|高山祭}}, which is designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazai/17768/mukei-minzoku/takayamamaturi.html |title=高山祭の屋台行事 |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2016-12-11}}. The main deity is the Hachiman deity, {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, and the enshrined deities include Atsuta Okami ({{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊命}}) and Kashi Okami ({{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}). == History == It is said that in the 65th year of the reign of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}, a bandit with two faces named {{ill|Ryomen Sukuna|ja|両面宿儺}} appeared in Hida Province. He was defeated by {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|武振熊命}}, the ancestor of the Wani clan, who prayed for victory to the former Emperor Ōjin. Another theory is that it was established as a protective Hachiman shrine by {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}} in various provinces. In the [[Edo period]], the Kanamori clan, the local lords, protected the shrine as their {{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神}} and made the residents north of the {{ill|Miyagawa (Gifu)|ja|宮川}} their parishioners. In the Meiji period, it was renamed Hachiman Shrine and became a Prefectural Shrine in 1932. The name Sakurayama Hachimangu has been used since after World War II. == Facilities == The {{ill|Takayama festival Yatai Kaikan|ja|高山祭屋台会館}} is located on the shrine grounds, where the festival floats used in the autumn Takayama festival are displayed throughout the year. Another theory is that it was established as a protective Hachiman shrine by {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}} in various provinces. In the [[Edo period]], the Kanamori clan, the local lords, protected the shrine as their {{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神}} and made the residents north of the {{ill|Miyagawa (Gifu)|ja|宮川}} their parishioners. In the Meiji period, it was renamed Hachiman Shrine and became a Prefectural Shrine in 1932. The name Sakurayama Hachimangu has been used since after World War II. == Facilities == The {{ill|Takayama festival Yatai Kaikan|ja|高山祭屋台会館}} is located on the shrine grounds, where the festival floats used in the autumn Takayama festival are displayed throughout the year. File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref10s3s4020.jpg|Shrine gate File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref09n4110.jpg|Kotohira Shrine The main deity is the Hachiman deity, {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, and the enshrined deities include Atsuta Okami ({{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊命}}) and Kashi Okami ({{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}). == History == It is said that in the 65th year of the reign of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}, a bandit with two faces named {{ill|Ryomen Sukuna|ja|両面宿儺}} appeared in Hida Province. Another theory is that it was established as a protective Hachiman shrine by {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}} in various provinces. In the [[Edo period]], the Kanamori clan, the local lords, protected the shrine as their {{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神}} and made the residents north of the {{ill|Miyagawa (Gifu)|ja|宮川}} their parishioners. In the Meiji period, it was renamed Hachiman Shrine and became a Prefectural Shrine in 1932. The autumn festival, along with the spring festival of {{ill|Hie Shrine (Takayama)|ja|日枝神社 (高山市)}}, is known as the {{ill|Takayama festival|ja|高山祭}}, which is designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazai/17768/mukei-minzoku/takayamamaturi.html |title=高山祭の屋台行事 |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2016-12-11}}. The main deity is the Hachiman deity, {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, and the enshrined deities include Atsuta Okami ({{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊命}}) and Kashi Okami ({{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}). == History == It is said that in the 65th year of the reign of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}, a bandit with two faces named {{ill|Ryomen Sukuna|ja|両面宿儺}} appeared in Hida Province. He was defeated by {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|武振熊命}}, the ancestor of the Wani clan, who prayed for victory to the former Emperor Ōjin. Another theory is that it was established as a protective Hachiman shrine by {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}} in various provinces. In the [[Edo period]], the Kanamori clan, the local lords, protected the shrine as their {{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神}} and made the residents north of the {{ill|Miyagawa (Gifu)|ja|宮川}} their parishioners. In the Meiji period, it was renamed Hachiman Shrine and became a Prefectural Shrine in 1932. The name Sakurayama Hachimangu has been used since after World War II. == Facilities == The {{ill|Takayama festival Yatai Kaikan|ja|高山祭屋台会館}} is located on the shrine grounds, where the festival floats used in the autumn Takayama festival are displayed throughout the year. File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref10s3s4020.jpg|Shrine gate File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref09n4110.jpg|Kotohira Shrine File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref07n4140.jpg|Inari Shrine File:Takayama_torii.jpg|Great Torii File:Sakurayama-hachiman_ninotorii.jpeg|Second Torii == Shrines within the Grounds == * Akiba Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Kagutsuchi|ja|火産霊神}} * Tenman Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} * Inari Shrine ** Deities: {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲霊神}}, {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦神}}, deity: {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|浪速根子武振熊命}} == Cultural Properties == * Takayama festival Floats Event (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * Mikoshi and Mikoshi Storehouse of Hachiman Shrine (Municipal Tangible Folk Cultural Property){{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/kurashi/1000021/1000119/1000847/1000915/1004125.html|title=民俗文化財 |publisher=高山市 revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sakurayama Hachimangu | image = [[File:Sakurayama-hachimangu Takayama Gifu pref04s3s3990.jpg|250px]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E}}}} | location = [[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], [[Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], Sakuramachi 178 | coordinates = {{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E|scale:30000}} | deity = [[Emperor Ōjin]] | type = Former Prefectural Shrine, [[Bekkaku Shrine|Bekkaku Shrine]], Kanpei-sha | annual_festival = {{ill|October 9|ja|10月9日}}, {{ill|October 10|ja|10月10日}} (Takayama festival) | ceremonies = Shikinen Taisai }} '''Sakurayama Hachimangu''' (さくらやまはちまんぐう) (officially '''櫻山八幡宮''') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], [[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], Japan. The autumn festival, along with the spring festival of {{ill|Hie Shrine (Takayama)|ja|日枝神社 (高山市)}}, is known as the {{ill|Takayama festival|ja|高山祭}}, which is designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazai/17768/mukei-minzoku/takayamamaturi.html |title=高山祭の屋台行事 |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2016-12-11}}. The main deity is the Hachiman deity, {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, and the enshrined deities include Atsuta Okami ({{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊命}}) and Kashi Okami ({{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}). == History == It is said that in the 65th year of the reign of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}, a bandit with two faces named {{ill|Ryomen Sukuna|ja|両面宿儺}} appeared in Hida Province. He was defeated by {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|武振熊命}}, the ancestor of the Wani clan, who prayed for victory to the former Emperor Ōjin. Another theory is that it was established as a protective Hachiman shrine by {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}} in various provinces. In the [[Edo period]], the Kanamori clan, the local lords, protected the shrine as their {{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神}} and made the residents north of the {{ill|Miyagawa (Gifu)|ja|宮川}} their parishioners. In the Meiji period, it was renamed Hachiman Shrine and became a Prefectural Shrine in 1932. The name Sakurayama Hachimangu has been used since after World War II. == Facilities == The {{ill|Takayama festival Yatai Kaikan|ja|高山祭屋台会館}} is located on the shrine grounds, where the festival floats used in the autumn Takayama festival are displayed throughout the year. File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref10s3s4020.jpg|Shrine gate File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref09n4110.jpg|Kotohira Shrine File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref07n4140.jpg|Inari Shrine File:Takayama_torii.jpg|Great Torii File:Sakurayama-hachiman_ninotorii.jpeg|Second Torii == Shrines within the Grounds == * Akiba Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Kagutsuchi|ja|火産霊神}} * Tenman Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} * Inari Shrine ** Deities: {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲霊神}}, {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦神}}, {{ill|Matsunoo Shrine|ja|松尾神}} * Kotohira Shrine ** Deities: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴神}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少名彦名神}} * Terumae Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|浪速根子武振熊命}} == Cultural Properties == * Takayama festival Floats Event (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * Mikoshi and Mikoshi Storehouse of Hachiman Shrine (Municipal Tangible Folk Cultural Property){{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/kurashi/1000021/1000119/1000847/1000915/1004125.html|title=民俗文化財 |publisher=高山市 |accessdate=2016-12-11}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Sakurayama-hachiman-gū}} * [http://www.sakurayamahachiman.jimdo.com/ Sakurayama Hachimangu] {{shinto-stub}} revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sakurayama Hachimangu | image = [[File:Sakurayama-hachimangu Takayama Gifu pref04s3s3990.jpg|250px]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E}}}} | location = [[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], [[Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], Sakuramachi 178 | coordinates = {{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E|scale:30000}} | deity = [[Emperor Ōjin]] | type = Former Prefectural Shrine, [[Bekkaku Shrine|Bekkaku Shrine]], Kanpei-sha | annual_festival = {{ill|October 9|ja|10月9日}}, {{ill|October 10|ja|10月10日}} (Takayama festival) | ceremonies = Shikinen Taisai }} '''Sakurayama Hachimangu''' (さくらやまはちまんぐう) (officially '''櫻山八幡宮''') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], [[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], Japan. The autumn festival, along with the spring festival of {{ill|Hie Shrine (Takayama)|ja|日枝神社 (高山市)}}, is known as the {{ill|Takayama festival|ja|高山祭}}, which is designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazai/17768/mukei-minzoku/takayamamaturi.html |title=高山祭の屋台行事 |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2016-12-11}}. The main deity is the Hachiman deity, {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, and the enshrined deities include Atsuta Okami ({{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊命}}) and Kashi Okami ({{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}). == History == It is said that in the 65th year of the reign of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}, a bandit with two faces named {{ill|Ryomen Sukuna|ja|両面宿儺}} appeared in Hida Province. He was defeated by {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|武振熊命}}, the ancestor of the Wani clan, who prayed for victory to the former Emperor Ōjin. Another theory is that it was established as a protective Hachiman shrine by {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}} in various provinces. In the [[Edo period]], the Kanamori clan, the local lords, protected the shrine as their {{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神}} and made the residents north of the {{ill|Miyagawa (Gifu)|ja|宮川}} their parishioners. In the Meiji period, it was renamed Hachiman Shrine and became a Prefectural Shrine in 1932. The name Sakurayama Hachimangu has been used since after World War II. == Facilities == The {{ill|Takayama festival Yatai Kaikan|ja|高山祭屋台会館}} is located on the shrine grounds, where the festival floats used in the autumn Takayama festival are displayed throughout the year. File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref10s3s4020.jpg|Shrine gate File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref09n4110.jpg|Kotohira Shrine File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref07n4140.jpg|Inari Shrine File:Takayama_torii.jpg|Great Torii File:Sakurayama-hachiman_ninotorii.jpeg|Second Torii == Shrines within the Grounds == * Akiba Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Kagutsuchi|ja|火産霊神}} * Tenman Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} * Inari Shrine ** Deities: {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲霊神}}, {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦神}}, {{ill|Matsunoo Shrine|ja|松尾神}} * Kotohira Shrine ** Deities: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴神}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少名彦名神}} * Terumae Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|浪速根子武振熊命}} == Cultural Properties == * Takayama festival Floats Event (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * Mikoshi and Mikoshi Storehouse of Hachiman Shrine (Municipal Tangible Folk Cultural Property){{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/kurashi/1000021/1000119/1000847/1000915/1004125.html|title=民俗文化財 |publisher=高山市 |accessdate=2016-12-11}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Sakurayama-hachiman-gū}} * [http://www.sakurayamahachiman.jimdo.com/ Sakurayama Hachimangu] {{shinto-stub}} In the Meiji period, it was renamed Hachiman Shrine and became a Prefectural Shrine in 1932. The name Sakurayama Hachimangu has been used since after World War II. == Facilities == The {{ill|Takayama festival Yatai Kaikan|ja|高山祭屋台会館}} is located on the shrine grounds, where the festival floats used in the autumn Takayama festival are displayed throughout the year. {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sakurayama Hachimangu | image = [[File:Sakurayama-hachimangu Takayama Gifu pref04s3s3990.jpg|250px]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E}}}} | location = [[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], [[Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], Sakuramachi 178 | coordinates = {{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E|scale:30000}} | deity = [[Emperor Ōjin]] | type = Former Prefectural Shrine, [[Beppyo Shrine|Beppyo Shrine]], Kanpei-sha | annual_festival = {{ill|October 9|ja|10月9日}}, {{ill|October 10|ja|10月10日}} (Takayama festival) | ceremonies = Shikinen Taisai }} '''Sakurayama Hachimangu''' (さくらやまはちまんぐう) (officially '''櫻山八幡宮''') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], [[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], Japan. The autumn festival, along with the spring festival of {{ill|Hie Shrine (Takayama)|ja|日枝神社 (高山市)}}, is known as the {{ill|Takayama festival|ja|高山祭}}, which is designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazai/17768/mukei-minzoku/takayamamaturi.html |title=高山祭の屋台行事 |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2016-12-11}}. The main deity is the Hachiman deity, {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, and the enshrined deities include Atsuta Okami ({{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊命}}) and Kashi Okami ({{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}). == History == It is said that in the 65th year of the reign of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}, a bandit with two faces named {{ill|Ryomen Sukuna|ja|両面宿儺}} appeared in Hida Province. He was defeated by {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|武振熊命}}, the ancestor of the Wani clan, who prayed for victory to the former Emperor Ōjin. Another theory is that it was established as a protective Hachiman shrine by {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}} in various provinces. In the [[Edo period]], the Kanamori clan, the local lords, protected the shrine as their {{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神}} and made the residents north of the {{ill|Miyagawa (Gifu)|ja|宮川}} their parishioners. In the Meiji period, it was renamed Hachiman Shrine and became a Prefectural Shrine in 1932. The name Sakurayama Hachimangu has been used since after World War II. == Facilities == The {{ill|Takayama festival Yatai Kaikan|ja|高山祭屋台会館}} is located on the shrine grounds, where the festival floats used in the autumn Takayama festival are displayed throughout the year. File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref10s3s4020.jpg|Shrine gate File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref09n4110.jpg|Kotohira Shrine File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref07n4140.jpg|Inari Shrine File:Takayama_torii.jpg|Great Torii File:Sakurayama-hachiman_ninotorii.jpeg|Second Torii == Shrines within the Grounds == * Akiba {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sakurayama Hachimangu | image = [[File:Sakurayama-hachimangu Takayama Gifu pref04s3s3990.jpg|250px]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E}}}} | location = [[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], [[Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], Sakuramachi 178 | coordinates = {{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E|scale:30000}} | deity = [[Emperor Ōjin]] | type = Former Prefectural Shrine, [[Beppyo Shrine|Beppyo Shrine]], Kanpei-sha | annual_festival = {{ill|October 9|ja|10月9日}}, {{ill|October 10|ja|10月10日}} (Takayama festival) | ceremonies = Shikinen Taisai }} '''Sakurayama Hachimangu''' (さくらやまはちまんぐう) (officially '''櫻山八幡宮''') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], [[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], Japan. The autumn festival, along with the spring festival of {{ill|Hie Shrine (Takayama)|ja|日枝神社 (高山市)}}, is known as the {{ill|Takayama festival|ja|高山祭}}, which is designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazai/17768/mukei-minzoku/takayamamaturi.html |title=高山祭の屋台行事 |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2016-12-11}}. The main deity is the Hachiman deity, {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, and the enshrined deities include Atsuta Okami ({{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊命}}) and Kashi Okami ({{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}). == History == It is said that in the 65th year of the reign of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}, a bandit with two faces named {{ill|Ryomen Sukuna|ja|両面宿儺}} appeared in Hida Province. He was defeated by {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|武振熊命}}, the ancestor of the Wani clan, who prayed for victory to the former Emperor Ōjin. Another theory is that it was established as a protective Hachiman shrine by {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}} in various provinces. In the [[Edo period]], the Kanamori clan, the local lords, protected the shrine as their {{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神}} and made the residents north of the {{ill|Miyagawa (Gifu)|ja|宮川}} their parishioners. In the Meiji period, it was renamed Hachiman Shrine and became a Prefectural Shrine in 1932. The name Sakurayama Hachimangu has been used since after World War II. == Facilities == The {{ill|Takayama festival Yatai Kaikan|ja|高山祭屋台会館}} is located on the shrine grounds, where the festival floats used in the autumn Takayama festival are displayed throughout the year. File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref10s3s4020.jpg|Shrine gate File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref09n4110.jpg|Kotohira Shrine File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref07n4140.jpg|Inari Shrine File:Takayama_torii.jpg|Great Torii File:Sakurayama-hachiman_ninotorii.jpeg|Second Torii == Shrines within the Grounds == * Akiba Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Kagutsuchi|ja|火産霊神}} * Tenman Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} * Inari Shrine ** Deities: {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲霊神}}, {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦神}}, {{ill|Matsunoo Shrine|ja|松尾神}} * Kotohira Shrine ** Deities: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴神}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少名彦名神}} * Terumae Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|浪速根子武振熊命}} == Cultural Properties == * Takayama festival Floats Event (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * Mikoshi and Mikoshi Storehouse of Hachiman Shrine (Municipal Tangible Folk Cultural Property){{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/kurashi/1000021/1000119/1000847/1000915/1004125.html|title=民俗文化財 |publisher=高山市 |accessdate=2016-12-11}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Sakurayama-hachiman-gū}} * [http://www.sakurayamahachiman.jimdo.com/ Sakurayama Hachimangu] {{shinto-stub}} The autumn festival, along with the spring festival of {{ill|Hie Shrine (Takayama)|ja|日枝神社 (高山市)}}, is known as the {{ill|Takayama festival|ja|高山祭}}, which is designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazai/17768/mukei-minzoku/takayamamaturi.html |title=高山祭の屋台行事 |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2016-12-11}}. The main deity is the Hachiman deity, {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, and the enshrined deities include Atsuta Okami ({{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊命}}) and Kashi Okami ({{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}). == History == It is said that in the 65th year of the reign of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}, a bandit with two faces named {{ill|Ryomen Sukuna|ja|両面宿儺}} appeared in Hida Province. Another theory is that it was established as a protective Hachiman shrine by {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}} in various provinces. In the [[Edo period]], the Kanamori clan, the local lords, protected the shrine as their {{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神}} and made the residents north of the {{ill|Miyagawa (Gifu)|ja|宮川}} their parishioners.
