{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sakurayama Hachimangu | image = [[File:Sakurayama-hachimangu Takayama Gifu pref04s3s3990.jpg|250px]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E}}}} | location = [[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], [[Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], Sakuramachi 178 | coordinates = {{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E|scale:30000}} | deity = [[Emperor Ōjin]] | type = Former Prefectural Shrine, [[Beppyo Shrine|Beppyo Shrine]], Kanpei-sha | annual_festival = {{ill|October 9|ja|10月9日}}, {{ill|October 10|ja|10月10日}} (Takayama festival) | ceremonies = Shikinen Taisai }} '''Sakurayama Hachimangu''' (さくらやまはちまんぐう) (officially '''櫻山八幡宮''') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], [[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], Japan. The autumn festival, along with the spring festival of {{ill|Hie Shrine (Takayama)|ja|日枝神社 (高山市)}}, is known as the {{ill|Takayama festival|ja|高山祭}}, which is designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazai/17768/mukei-minzoku/takayamamaturi.html |title=高山祭の屋台行事 |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2016-12-11}}. The main deity is the Hachiman deity, {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, and the enshrined deities include Atsuta Okami ({{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊命}}) and Kashi Okami ({{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}). == History == It is said that in the 65th year of the reign of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}, a bandit with two faces named {{ill|Ryomen Sukuna|ja|両面宿儺}} appeared in Hida Province. He was defeated by {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|武振熊命}}, the ancestor of the Wani clan, who prayed for victory to the former Emperor Ōjin. Another theory is that it was established as a protective Hachiman shrine by {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}} in various provinces. In the [[Edo period]], the Kanamori clan, the local lords, protected the shrine as their {{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神}} and made the residents north of the {{ill|Miyagawa (Gifu)|ja|宮川}} their parishioners. In the Meiji period, it was renamed Hachiman Shrine and became a Prefectural Shrine in 1932. The name Sakurayama Hachimangu has been used since after World War II. == Facilities == The {{ill|Takayama festival Yatai Kaikan|ja|高山祭屋台会館}} is located on the shrine grounds, where the festival floats used in the autumn Takayama festival are displayed throughout the year. File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref10s3s4020.jpg|Shrine gate File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref09n4110.jpg|Kotohira Shrine File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref07n4140.jpg|Inari Shrine File:Takayama_torii.jpg|Great Torii File:Sakurayama-hachiman_ninotorii.jpeg|Second Torii == Shrines within the Grounds == * Akiba Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Kagutsuchi|ja|火産霊神}} * Tenman Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} * Inari Shrine ** Deities: {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲霊神}}, {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦神}}, {{ill|Matsunoo Shrine|ja|松尾神}} * Kotohira Shrine ** Deities: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴神}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少名彦名神}} * Terumae Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|浪速根子武振熊命}} == Cultural Properties == * Takayama festival Floats Event (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * Mikoshi and Mikoshi Storehouse of Hachiman Shrine (Municipal Tangible Folk Cultural Property){{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/kurashi/1000021/1000119/1000847/1000915/1004125.html|title=民俗文化財 |publisher=高山市 {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sakurayama Hachimangu | image = [[File:Sakurayama-hachimangu Takayama Gifu pref04s3s3990.jpg|250px]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E}}}} | location = [[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], [[Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], Sakuramachi 178 | coordinates = {{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E|scale:30000}} | deity = [[Emperor Ōjin]] | type = Former Prefectural Shrine, [[Beppyo Shrine|Beppyo Shrine]], Kanpei-sha | annual_festival = {{ill|October 9|ja|10月9日}}, {{ill|October 10|ja|10月10日}} (Takayama festival) | ceremonies = Shikinen Taisai }} '''Sakurayama Hachimangu''' (さくらやまはちまんぐう) (officially '''櫻山八幡宮''') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], [[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], Japan. The autumn festival, along with the spring festival of {{ill|Hie Shrine (Takayama)|ja|日枝神社 (高山市)}}, is known as the {{ill|Takayama festival|ja|高山祭}}, which is designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazai/17768/mukei-minzoku/takayamamaturi.html |title=高山祭の屋台行事 |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2016-12-11}}. The main deity is the Hachiman deity, {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, and the enshrined deities include Atsuta Okami ({{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊命}}) and Kashi Okami ({{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}). == History == It is said that in the 65th year of the reign of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}, a bandit with two faces named {{ill|Ryomen Sukuna|ja|両面宿儺}} appeared in Hida Province. He was defeated by {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|武振熊命}}, the ancestor of the Wani clan, who prayed for victory to the former Emperor Ōjin. Another theory is that it was established as a protective Hachiman shrine by {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}} in various provinces. In the [[Edo period]], the Kanamori clan, the local lords, protected the shrine as their {{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神}} and made the residents north of the {{ill|Miyagawa (Gifu)|ja|宮川}} their parishioners. In the Meiji period, it was renamed Hachiman Shrine and became a Prefectural Shrine in 1932. The name Sakurayama Hachimangu has been used since after World War II. == Facilities == The {{ill|Takayama festival Yatai Kaikan|ja|高山祭屋台会館}} is located on the shrine grounds, where the festival floats used in the autumn Takayama festival are displayed throughout the year. File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref10s3s4020.jpg|Shrine gate File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref09n4110.jpg|Kotohira Shrine File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref07n4140.jpg|Inari Shrine File:Takayama_torii.jpg|Great Torii File:Sakurayama-hachiman_ninotorii.jpeg|Second Torii == Shrines within the Grounds == * Akiba Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Kagutsuchi|ja|火産霊神}} * Tenman Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} * Inari Shrine ** Deities: {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲霊神}}, {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦神}}, {{ill|Matsunoo Shrine|ja|松尾神}} * Kotohira Shrine ** Deities: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴神}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少名彦名神}} * Terumae Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|浪速根子武振熊命}} == Cultural Properties == * Takayama festival Floats Event (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * Mikoshi and Mikoshi Storehouse of Hachiman Shrine (Municipal Tangible Folk Cultural Property){{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/kurashi/1000021/1000119/1000847/1000915/1004125.html|title=民俗文化財 |publisher=高山市 |accessdate=2016-12-11}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Sakurayama-hachiman-gū}} * [http://www.sakurayamahachiman.jimdo.com/ Sakurayama Hachimangu] {{shinto-stub}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sakurayama Hachimangu | image = [[File:Sakurayama-hachimangu Takayama Gifu pref04s3s3990.jpg|250px]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E}}}} | location = [[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], [[Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], Sakuramachi 178 | coordinates = {{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E|scale:30000}} | deity = [[Emperor Ōjin]] | type = Former Prefectural Shrine, [[Beppyo Shrine|Beppyo Shrine]], Kanpei-sha | annual_festival = {{ill|October 9|ja|10月9日}}, {{ill|October 10|ja|10月10日}} (Takayama festival) | ceremonies = Shikinen Taisai }} '''Sakurayama Hachimangu''' (さくらやまはちまんぐう) (officially '''櫻山八幡宮''') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], [[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], Japan. The autumn festival, along with the spring festival of {{ill|Hie Shrine (Takayama)|ja|日枝神社 (高山市)}}, is known as the {{ill|Takayama festival|ja|高山祭}}, which is designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazai/17768/mukei-minzoku/takayamamaturi.html |title=高山祭の屋台行事 |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2016-12-11}}. The main deity is the Hachiman deity, {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, and the enshrined deities include Atsuta Okami ({{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊命}}) and Kashi Okami ({{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}). == History == It is said that in the 65th year of the reign of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}, a bandit with two faces named {{ill|Ryomen Sukuna|ja|両面宿儺}} appeared in Hida Province. He was defeated by {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|武振熊命}}, the ancestor of the Wani clan, who prayed for victory to the former Emperor Ōjin. Another theory is that it was established as a protective Hachiman shrine by {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}} in various provinces. In the [[Edo period]], the Kanamori clan, the local lords, protected the shrine as their {{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神}} and made the residents north of the {{ill|Miyagawa (Gifu)|ja|宮川}} their parishioners. In the Meiji period, it was renamed Hachiman Shrine and became a Prefectural Shrine in 1932. The name Sakurayama Hachimangu has been used since after World War II. == Facilities == The {{ill|Takayama festival Yatai Kaikan|ja|高山祭屋台会館}} is located on the shrine grounds, where the festival floats used in the autumn Takayama festival are displayed throughout the year. File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref10s3s4020.jpg|Shrine gate File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref09n4110.jpg|Kotohira Shrine File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref07n4140.jpg|Inari Shrine File:Takayama_torii.jpg|Great Torii File:Sakurayama-hachiman_ninotorii.jpeg|Second Torii == Shrines within the Grounds == * Akiba Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Kagutsuchi|ja|火産霊神}} * Tenman Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} * Inari Shrine ** Deities: {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲霊神}}, {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦神}}, {{ill|Matsunoo Shrine|ja|松尾神}} * Kotohira Shrine ** Deities: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴神}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少名彦名神}} * Terumae Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|浪速根子武振熊命}} == Cultural Properties == * Takayama festival Floats Event (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * Mikoshi and Mikoshi Storehouse of Hachiman Shrine (Municipal Tangible Folk Cultural Property){{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/kurashi/1000021/1000119/1000847/1000915/1004125.html|title=民俗文化財 |publisher=高山市 |accessdate=2016-12-11}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Sakurayama-hachiman-gū}} * [http://www.sakurayamahachiman.jimdo.com/ Sakurayama Hachimangu] {{shinto-stub}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sakurayama Hachimangu | image = [[File:Sakurayama-hachimangu Takayama Gifu pref04s3s3990.jpg|250px]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E}}}} | location = [[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], [[Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], Sakuramachi 178 | coordinates = {{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E|scale:30000}} | deity = [[Emperor Ōjin]] | type = Former Prefectural Shrine, [[Beppyo Shrine|Beppyo Shrine]], Kanpei-sha | annual_festival = {{ill|October 9|ja|10月9日}}, {{ill|October 10|ja|10月10日}} (Takayama festival) | ceremonies = Shikinen Taisai }} '''Sakurayama Hachimangu''' (さくらやまはちまんぐう) (officially '''櫻山八幡宮''') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], [[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], Japan. The autumn festival, along with the spring festival of {{ill|Hie Shrine (Takayama)|ja|日枝神社 (高山市)}}, is known as the {{ill|Takayama festival|ja|高山祭}}, which is designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazai/17768/mukei-minzoku/takayamamaturi.html |title=高山祭の屋台行事 |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2016-12-11}}. The main deity is the Hachiman deity, {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, and the enshrined deities include Atsuta Okami ({{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊命}}) and Kashi Okami ({{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}). == History == It is said that in the 65th year of the reign of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}, a bandit with two faces named {{ill|Ryomen Sukuna|ja|両面宿儺}} appeared in Hida Province. He was defeated by {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|武振熊命}}, the ancestor of the Wani clan, who prayed for victory to the former Emperor Ōjin. Another theory is that it was established as a protective Hachiman shrine by {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}} in various provinces. In the [[Edo period]], the Kanamori clan, the local lords, protected the shrine as their {{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神}} and made the residents north of the {{ill|Miyagawa (Gifu)|ja|宮川}} their parishioners. In the Meiji period, it was renamed Hachiman Shrine and became a Prefectural Shrine in 1932. The name Sakurayama Hachimangu has been used since after World War II. == Facilities == The {{ill|Takayama festival Yatai Kaikan|ja|高山祭屋台会館}} is located on the shrine grounds, where the festival floats used in the autumn Takayama festival are displayed throughout the year. File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref10s3s4020.jpg|Shrine gate File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref09n4110.jpg|Kotohira Shrine File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref07n4140.jpg|Inari Shrine File:Takayama_torii.jpg|Great Torii File:Sakurayama-hachiman_ninotorii.jpeg|Second Torii == Shrines within the Grounds == * Akiba Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Kagutsuchi|ja|火産霊神}} * Tenman Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} * Inari Shrine ** Deities: {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲霊神}}, {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦神}}, {{ill|Matsunoo Shrine|ja|松尾神}} * Kotohira Shrine ** Deities: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴神}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少名彦名神}} * Terumae Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|浪速根子武振熊命}} == Cultural Properties == * Takayama festival Floats Event (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * Mikoshi and Mikoshi Storehouse of Hachiman Shrine (Municipal Tangible Folk Cultural Property){{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/kurashi/1000021/1000119/1000847/1000915/1004125.html|title=民俗文化財 |publisher=高山市 |accessdate=2016-12-11}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Sakurayama-hachiman-gū}} * [http://www.sakurayamahachiman.jimdo.com/ Sakurayama Hachimangu] {{shinto-stub}} The autumn festival, along with the spring festival of {{ill|Hie Shrine (Takayama)|ja|日枝神社 (高山市)}}, is known as the {{ill|Takayama festival|ja|高山祭}}, which is designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazai/17768/mukei-minzoku/takayamamaturi.html |title=高山祭の屋台行事 |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2016-12-11}}. The main deity is the Hachiman deity, {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, and the enshrined deities include Atsuta Okami ({{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊命}}) and Kashi Okami ({{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}). == History == It is said that in the 65th year of the reign of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}, a bandit with two faces named {{ill|Ryomen Sukuna|ja|両面宿儺}} appeared in Hida Province. He was defeated by {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|武振熊命}}, the ancestor of the Wani clan, who prayed for victory to the former Emperor Ōjin. Another theory is that it was established as a protective Hachiman shrine by {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}} in various provinces. In the [[Edo period]], the Kanamori clan, the local lords, protected the shrine as their {{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神}} and made the residents north of the {{ill|Miyagawa (Gifu)|ja|宮川}} their parishioners. In the Meiji period, it was renamed Hachiman Shrine and became a Prefectural Shrine in 1932. The name Sakurayama Hachimangu has been used since after World War II. == Facilities == The {{ill|Takayama festival Yatai Kaikan|ja|高山祭屋台会館}} is located on the shrine grounds, where the festival floats used in the autumn Takayama festival are displayed throughout the year. File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref10s3s4020.jpg|Shrine gate File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref09n4110.jpg|Kotohira Shrine Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Kagutsuchi|ja|火産霊神}} * Tenman Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} * Inari Shrine ** Deities: {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲霊神}}, {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦神}}, He was defeated by {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|武振熊命}}, the ancestor of the Wani clan, who prayed for victory to the former Emperor Ōjin. Another theory is that it was established as a protective Hachiman shrine by {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}} in various provinces. In the [[Edo period]], the Kanamori clan, the local lords, protected the shrine as their {{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神}} and made the residents north of the {{ill|Miyagawa (Gifu)|ja|宮川}} their parishioners. In the Meiji period, it was renamed Hachiman Shrine and became a Prefectural Shrine in 1932. The name Sakurayama Hachimangu has been used since after World War II. == Facilities == The {{ill|Takayama festival Yatai Kaikan|ja|高山祭屋台会館}} is located on the shrine grounds, where the festival floats used in the autumn Takayama festival are displayed throughout the year. {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sakurayama Hachimangu | image = [[File:Sakurayama-hachimangu Takayama Gifu pref04s3s3990.jpg|250px]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E}}}} | location = [[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], [[Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], Sakuramachi 178 | coordinates = {{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E|scale:30000}} | deity = [[Emperor Ōjin]] | type = Former Prefectural Shrine, [[Beppyo Shrine|Beppyo Shrine]], Kanpei-sha | annual_festival = {{ill|October 9|ja|10月9日}}, {{ill|October 10|ja|10月10日}} (Takayama festival) | ceremonies = Shikinen Taisai }} '''Sakurayama Hachimangu''' (桜山八幡宮, officially '''櫻山八幡宮''') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], [[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], Japan. The autumn festival, along with the spring festival of {{ill|Hie Shrine (Takayama)|ja|日枝神社 (高山市)}}, is known as the {{ill|Takayama festival|ja|高山祭}}, which is designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazai/17768/mukei-minzoku/takayamamaturi.html |title=高山祭の屋台行事 |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2016-12-11}}. The main deity is the Hachiman deity, {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, and the enshrined deities include Atsuta Okami ({{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊命}}) and Kashi Okami ({{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}). == History == It is said that in the 65th year of the reign of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}, a bandit with two faces named {{ill|Ryomen Sukuna|ja|両面宿儺}} appeared in Hida Province. He was defeated by {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|武振熊命}}, the ancestor of the Wani clan, who prayed for victory to the former Emperor Ōjin. Another theory is that it was established as a protective Hachiman shrine by {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}} in various provinces. In the [[Edo period]], the Kanamori clan, the local lords, protected the shrine as their {{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神}} and made the residents north of the {{ill|Miyagawa (Gifu)|ja|宮川}} their parishioners. In the Meiji period, it was renamed Hachiman Shrine and became a Prefectural Shrine in 1932. The name Sakurayama Hachimangu has been used since after World War II. == Facilities == The {{ill|Takayama festival Yatai Kaikan|ja|高山祭屋台会館}} is located on the shrine grounds, where the festival floats used in the autumn Takayama festival are displayed throughout the year. File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref10s3s4020.jpg|Shrine gate File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref09n4110.jpg|Kotohira Shrine File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref07n4140.jpg|Inari Shrine File:Takayama_torii.jpg|Great Torii File:Sakurayama-hachiman_ninotorii.jpeg|Second Torii == Shrines within the Grounds == * Akiba Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Kagutsuchi|ja|火産霊神}} * Tenman Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} * Inari Shrine ** Deities: {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲霊神}}, {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦神}}, deity: {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|浪速根子武振熊命}} == Cultural Properties == * Takayama festival Floats Event (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * Mikoshi and Mikoshi Storehouse of Hachiman Shrine (Municipal Tangible Folk Cultural Property){{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/kurashi/1000021/1000119/1000847/1000915/1004125.html|title=民俗文化財 |publisher=高山市 |accessdate=2016-12-11}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Sakurayama-hachiman-gū}} * [http://www.sakurayamahachiman.jimdo.com/ Sakurayama Hachimangu] {{shinto-stub}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sakurayama Hachimangu | image = [[File:Sakurayama-hachimangu Takayama Gifu pref04s3s3990.jpg|250px]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E}}}} | location = [[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], [[Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], Sakuramachi 178 | coordinates = {{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E|scale:30000}} | deity = [[Emperor Ōjin]] | type = Former Prefectural Shrine, [[Beppyo Shrine|Beppyo Shrine]], Kanpei-sha | annual_festival = {{ill|October 9|ja|10月9日}}, {{ill|October 10|ja|10月10日}} (Takayama festival) | ceremonies = Shikinen Taisai }} '''Sakurayama Hachimangu''' (桜山八幡宮, officially '''櫻山八幡宮''') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], [[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], Japan. The autumn festival, along with the spring festival of {{ill|Hie Shrine (Takayama)|ja|日枝神社 (高山市)}}, is known as the {{ill|Takayama festival|ja|高山祭}}, which is designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazai/17768/mukei-minzoku/takayamamaturi.html |title=高山祭の屋台行事 |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2016-12-11}}. The main deity is the Hachiman deity, {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, and the enshrined deities include Atsuta Okami ({{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊命}}) and Kashi Okami ({{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}). == History == It is said that in the 65th year of the reign of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}, a bandit with two faces named {{ill|Ryomen Sukuna|ja|両面宿儺}} appeared in Hida Province. He was defeated by {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|武振熊命}}, the ancestor of the Wani clan, who prayed for victory to the former Emperor Ōjin. Another theory is that it was established as a protective Hachiman shrine by {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}} in various provinces. In the [[Edo period]], the Kanamori clan, the local lords, protected the shrine as their {{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神}} and made the residents north of the {{ill|Miyagawa (Gifu)|ja|宮川}} their parishioners. In the Meiji period, it was renamed Hachiman Shrine and became a Prefectural Shrine in 1932. The name Sakurayama Hachimangu has been used since after World War II. == Facilities == The {{ill|Takayama festival Yatai Kaikan|ja|高山祭屋台会館}} is located on the shrine grounds, where the festival floats used in the autumn Takayama festival are displayed throughout the year. File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref10s3s4020.jpg|Shrine gate File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref09n4110.jpg|Kotohira Shrine File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref07n4140.jpg|Inari Shrine File:Takayama_torii.jpg|Great Torii File:Sakurayama-hachiman_ninotorii.jpeg|Second Torii == Shrines within the Grounds == * Akiba Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Kagutsuchi|ja|火産霊神}} * Tenman Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} * Inari Shrine ** Deities: {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲霊神}}, {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦神}}, {{ill|Matsunoo Shrine|ja|松尾神}} * Kotohira Shrine ** Deities: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴神}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少名彦名神}} * Terumae Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|浪速根子武振熊命}} == Cultural Properties == * Takayama festival Floats Event (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * Mikoshi and Mikoshi Storehouse of Hachiman Shrine (Municipal Tangible Folk Cultural Property){{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/kurashi/1000021/1000119/1000847/1000915/1004125.html|title=民俗文化財 |publisher=高山市 |accessdate=2016-12-11}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Sakurayama-hachiman-gū}} * [http://www.sakurayamahachiman.jimdo.com/ Sakurayama Hachimangu] {{shinto-stub}} revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sakurayama Hachimangu | image = [[File:Sakurayama-hachimangu Takayama Gifu pref04s3s3990.jpg|250px]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E}}}} | location = [[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], [[Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], Sakuramachi 178 | coordinates = {{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E|scale:30000}} | deity = [[Emperor Ōjin]] | type = Former Prefectural Shrine, [[Beppyo Shrine|Beppyo Shrine]], Kanpei-sha | annual_festival = {{ill|October 9|ja|10月9日}}, {{ill|October 10|ja|10月10日}} (Takayama festival) | ceremonies = Shikinen Taisai }} '''Sakurayama Hachimangu''' (桜山八幡宮, officially '''櫻山八幡宮''') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], [[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], Japan. The autumn festival, along with the spring festival of {{ill|Hie Shrine (Takayama)|ja|日枝神社 (高山市)}}, is known as the {{ill|Takayama festival|ja|高山祭}}, which is designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazai/17768/mukei-minzoku/takayamamaturi.html |title=高山祭の屋台行事 |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2016-12-11}}. The main deity is the Hachiman deity, {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, and the enshrined deities include Atsuta Okami ({{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊命}}) and Kashi Okami ({{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}). == History == It is said that in the 65th year of the reign of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}, a bandit with two faces named {{ill|Ryomen Sukuna|ja|両面宿儺}} appeared in Hida Province. He was defeated by {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|武振熊命}}, the ancestor of the Wani clan, who prayed for victory to the former Emperor Ōjin. Another theory is that it was established as a protective Hachiman shrine by {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}} in various provinces. In the [[Edo period]], the Kanamori clan, the local lords, protected the shrine as their {{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神}} and made the residents north of the {{ill|Miyagawa (Gifu)|ja|宮川}} their parishioners. In the Meiji period, it was renamed Hachiman Shrine and became a Prefectural Shrine in 1932. The name Sakurayama Hachimangu has been used since after World War II. == Facilities == The {{ill|Takayama festival Yatai Kaikan|ja|高山祭屋台会館}} is located on the shrine grounds, where the festival floats used in the autumn Takayama festival are displayed throughout the year. File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref10s3s4020.jpg|Shrine gate File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref09n4110.jpg|Kotohira Shrine File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref07n4140.jpg|Inari Shrine File:Takayama_torii.jpg|Great Torii File:Sakurayama-hachiman_ninotorii.jpeg|Second Torii == Shrines within the Grounds == * Akiba Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Kagutsuchi|ja|火産霊神}} * Tenman Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} * Inari Shrine ** Deities: {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲霊神}}, {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦神}}, {{ill|Matsunoo Shrine|ja|松尾神}} * Kotohira Shrine ** Deities: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴神}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少名彦名神}} * Terumae Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|浪速根子武振熊命}} == Cultural Properties == * Takayama festival Floats Event (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * Mikoshi and Mikoshi Storehouse of Hachiman Shrine (Municipal Tangible Folk Cultural Property){{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/kurashi/1000021/1000119/1000847/1000915/1004125.html|title=民俗文化財 |publisher=高山市 |accessdate=2016-12-11}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Sakurayama-hachiman-gū}} * [http://www.sakurayamahachiman.jimdo.com/ Sakurayama Hachimangu] {{shinto-stub}} revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sakurayama Hachimangu | image = [[File:Sakurayama-hachimangu Takayama Gifu pref04s3s3990.jpg|250px]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E}}}} | location = [[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], [[Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], Sakuramachi 178 | coordinates = {{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E|scale:30000}} | deity = [[Emperor Ōjin]] | type = Former Prefectural Shrine, [[Beppyo Shrine|Beppyo Shrine]], Kanpei-sha | annual_festival = {{ill|October 9|ja|10月9日}}, {{ill|October 10|ja|10月10日}} (Takayama festival) | ceremonies = Shikinen Taisai }} '''Sakurayama Hachimangu''' (桜山八幡宮, officially '''櫻山八幡宮''') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], [[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], Japan. The autumn festival, along with the spring festival of {{ill|Hie Shrine (Takayama)|ja|日枝神社 (高山市)}}, is known as the {{ill|Takayama festival|ja|高山祭}}, which is designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazai/17768/mukei-minzoku/takayamamaturi.html |title=高山祭の屋台行事 |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2016-12-11}}. The main deity is the Hachiman deity, {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, and the enshrined deities include Atsuta Okami ({{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊命}}) and Kashi Okami ({{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}). == History == It is said that in the 65th year of the reign of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}, a bandit with two faces named {{ill|Ryomen Sukuna|ja|両面宿儺}} appeared in Hida Province. He was defeated by {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|武振熊命}}, the ancestor of the Wani clan, who prayed for victory to the former Emperor Ōjin. Another theory is that it was established as a protective Hachiman shrine by {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}} in various provinces. In the [[Edo period]], the Kanamori clan, the local lords, protected the shrine as their {{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神}} and made the residents north of the {{ill|Miyagawa (Gifu)|ja|宮川}} their parishioners. In the Meiji period, it was renamed Hachiman Shrine and became a Prefectural Shrine in 1932. The name Sakurayama Hachimangu has been used since after World War II. == Facilities == The {{ill|Takayama festival Yatai Kaikan|ja|高山祭屋台会館}} is located on the shrine grounds, where the festival floats used in the autumn Takayama festival are displayed throughout the year. File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref10s3s4020.jpg|Shrine gate File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref09n4110.jpg|Kotohira Shrine File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref07n4140.jpg|Inari Shrine File:Takayama_torii.jpg|Great Torii File:Sakurayama-hachiman_ninotorii.jpeg|Second Torii == Shrines within the Grounds == * Akiba Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Kagutsuchi|ja|火産霊神}} * Tenman Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} * Inari Shrine ** Deities: {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲霊神}}, {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦神}}, {{ill|Matsunoo Shrine|ja|松尾神}} * Kotohira Shrine ** Deities: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴神}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少名彦名神}} * Terumae Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|浪速根子武振熊命}} == Cultural Properties == * Takayama festival Floats Event (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * Mikoshi and Mikoshi Storehouse of Hachiman Shrine (Municipal Tangible Folk Cultural Property){{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/kurashi/1000021/1000119/1000847/1000915/1004125.html|title=民俗文化財 |publisher=高山市 |accessdate=2016-12-11}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Sakurayama-hachiman-gū}} * [http://www.sakurayamahachiman.jimdo.com/ Sakurayama Hachimangu] {{shinto-stub}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sakurayama Hachimangu | image = [[File:Sakurayama-hachimangu Takayama Gifu pref04s3s3990.jpg|250px]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E}}}} | location = [[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], [[Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], Sakuramachi 178 | coordinates = {{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E|scale:30000}} | deity = [[Emperor Ōjin]] | type = Former Prefectural Shrine, [[Beppyo Shrine|Beppyo Shrine]], Kanpei-sha | annual_festival = {{ill|October 9|ja|10月9日}}, {{ill|October 10|ja|10月10日}} (Takayama festival) | ceremonies = Shikinen Taisai }} '''Sakurayama Hachimangu''' (桜山八幡宮, officially '''櫻山八幡宮''') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], [[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], Japan. The autumn festival, along with the spring festival of {{ill|Hie Shrine (Takayama)|ja|日枝神社 (高山市)}}, is known as the {{ill|Takayama festival|ja|高山祭}}, which is designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazai/17768/mukei-minzoku/takayamamaturi.html |title=高山祭の屋台行事 |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2016-12-11}}. The main deity is the Hachiman deity, {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, and the enshrined deities include Atsuta Okami ({{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊命}}) and Kashi Okami ({{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}). == History == It is said that in the 65th year of the reign of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}, a bandit with two faces named {{ill|Ryomen Sukuna|ja|両面宿儺}} appeared in Hida Province. He was defeated by {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|武振熊命}}, the ancestor of the Wani clan, who prayed for victory to the former Emperor Ōjin. Another theory is that it was established as a protective Hachiman shrine by {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}} in various provinces. In the [[Edo period]], the Kanamori clan, the local lords, protected the shrine as their {{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神}} and made the residents north of the {{ill|Miyagawa (Gifu)|ja|宮川}} their parishioners. In the Meiji period, it was renamed Hachiman Shrine and became a Prefectural Shrine in 1932. The name Sakurayama Hachimangu has been used since after World War II. == Facilities == The {{ill|Takayama festival Yatai Kaikan|ja|高山祭屋台会館}} is located on the shrine grounds, where the festival floats used in the autumn Takayama festival are displayed throughout the year. File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref10s3s4020.jpg|Shrine gate File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref09n4110.jpg|Kotohira Shrine File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref07n4140.jpg|Inari Shrine File:Takayama_torii.jpg|Great Torii File:Sakurayama-hachiman_ninotorii.jpeg|Second Torii == Shrines within the Grounds == * Akiba Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Kagutsuchi|ja|火産霊神}} * Tenman Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} * Inari Shrine ** Deities: {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲霊神}}, {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦神}}, {{ill|Matsunoo Shrine|ja|松尾神}} * Kotohira Shrine ** Deities: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴神}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少名彦名神}} * Terumae Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|浪速根子武振熊命}} == Cultural Properties == * Takayama festival Floats Event (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * Mikoshi and Mikoshi Storehouse of Hachiman Shrine (Municipal Tangible Folk Cultural Property){{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/kurashi/1000021/1000119/1000847/1000915/1004125.html|title=民俗文化財 |publisher=高山市 |accessdate=2016-12-11}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Sakurayama-hachiman-gū}} * [http://www.sakurayamahachiman.jimdo.com/ Sakurayama Hachimangu] {{shinto-stub}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sakurayama Hachimangu | image = [[File:Sakurayama-hachimangu Takayama Gifu pref04s3s3990.jpg|250px]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E}}}} | location = [[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], [[Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], Sakuramachi 178 | coordinates = {{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E|scale:30000}} | deity = [[Emperor Ōjin]] | type = Former Prefectural Shrine, [[Beppyo Shrine|Beppyo Shrine]], Kanpei-sha | annual_festival = {{ill|October 9|ja|10月9日}}, {{ill|October 10|ja|10月10日}} (Takayama festival) | ceremonies = Shikinen Taisai }} '''Sakurayama Hachimangu''' (桜山八幡宮, officially '''櫻山八幡宮''') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], [[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], Japan. The autumn festival, along with the spring festival of {{ill|Hie Shrine (Takayama)|ja|日枝神社 (高山市)}}, is known as the {{ill|Takayama festival|ja|高山祭}}, which is designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazai/17768/mukei-minzoku/takayamamaturi.html |title=高山祭の屋台行事 |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2016-12-11}}. The main deity is the Hachiman deity, {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, and the enshrined deities include Atsuta Okami ({{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊命}}) and Kashi Okami ({{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}). == History == It is said that in the 65th year of the reign of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}, a bandit with two faces named {{ill|Ryomen Sukuna|ja|両面宿儺}} appeared in Hida Province. He was defeated by {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|武振熊命}}, the ancestor of the Wani clan, who prayed for victory to the former Emperor Ōjin. Another theory is that it was established as a protective Hachiman shrine by {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}} in various provinces. In the [[Edo period]], the Kanamori clan, the local lords, protected the shrine as their {{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神}} and made the residents north of the {{ill|Miyagawa (Gifu)|ja|宮川}} their parishioners. In the Meiji period, it was renamed Hachiman Shrine and became a Prefectural Shrine in 1932. The name Sakurayama Hachimangu has been used since after World War II. == Facilities == The {{ill|Takayama festival Yatai Kaikan|ja|高山祭屋台会館}} is located on the shrine grounds, where the festival floats used in the autumn Takayama festival are displayed throughout the year. File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref10s3s4020.jpg|Shrine gate File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref09n4110.jpg|Kotohira Shrine File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref07n4140.jpg|Inari Shrine File:Takayama_torii.jpg|Great Torii File:Sakurayama-hachiman_ninotorii.jpeg|Second Torii == Shrines within the Grounds == * Akiba Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Kagutsuchi|ja|火産霊神}} * Tenman Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} * Inari Shrine ** Deities: {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲霊神}}, {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦神}}, {{ill|Matsunoo Shrine|ja|松尾神}} * Kotohira Shrine ** Deities: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴神}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少名彦名神}} * Terumae Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|浪速根子武振熊命}} == Cultural Properties == * Takayama festival Floats Event (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * Mikoshi and Mikoshi Storehouse of Hachiman Shrine (Municipal Tangible Folk Cultural Property){{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/kurashi/1000021/1000119/1000847/1000915/1004125.html|title=民俗文化財 |publisher=高山市 |accessdate=2016-12-11}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Sakurayama-hachiman-gū}} * [http://www.sakurayamahachiman.jimdo.com/ Sakurayama Hachimangu] {{shinto-stub}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sakurayama Hachimangu | image = [[File:Sakurayama-hachimangu Takayama Gifu pref04s3s3990.jpg|250px]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E}}}} | location = [[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], [[Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], Sakuramachi 178 | coordinates = {{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E|scale:30000}} | deity = [[Emperor Ōjin]] | type = Former Prefectural Shrine, [[Beppyo Shrine|Beppyo Shrine]], Kanpei-sha | annual_festival = {{ill|October 9|ja|10月9日}}, {{ill|October 10|ja|10月10日}} (Takayama festival) | ceremonies = Shikinen Taisai }} '''Sakurayama Hachimangu''' (桜山八幡宮, officially '''櫻山八幡宮''') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], [[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], Japan. The autumn festival, along with the spring festival of {{ill|Hie Shrine (Takayama)|ja|日枝神社 (高山市)}}, is known as the {{ill|Takayama festival|ja|高山祭}}, which is designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazai/17768/mukei-minzoku/takayamamaturi.html |title=高山祭の屋台行事 |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2016-12-11}}. The main deity is the Hachiman deity, {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, and the enshrined deities include Atsuta Okami ({{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊命}}) and Kashi Okami ({{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}). == History == It is said that in the 65th year of the reign of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}, a bandit with two faces named {{ill|Ryomen Sukuna|ja|両面宿儺}} appeared in Hida Province. He was defeated by {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|武振熊命}}, the ancestor of the Wani clan, who prayed for victory to the former Emperor Ōjin. Another theory is that it was established as a protective Hachiman shrine by {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}} in various provinces. In the [[Edo period]], the Kanamori clan, the local lords, protected the shrine as their {{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神}} and made the residents north of the {{ill|Miyagawa (Gifu)|ja|宮川}} their parishioners. In the Meiji period, it was renamed Hachiman Shrine and became a Prefectural Shrine in 1932. The name Sakurayama Hachimangu has been used since after World War II. == Facilities == The {{ill|Takayama festival Yatai Kaikan|ja|高山祭屋台会館}} is located on the shrine grounds, where the festival floats used in the autumn Takayama festival are displayed throughout the year. File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref10s3s4020.jpg|Shrine gate File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref09n4110.jpg|Kotohira Shrine File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref07n4140.jpg|Inari Shrine File:Takayama_torii.jpg|Great Torii File:Sakurayama-hachiman_ninotorii.jpeg|Second Torii == Shrines within the Grounds == * Akiba Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Kagutsuchi|ja|火産霊神}} * Tenman Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} * Inari Shrine ** Deities: {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲霊神}}, {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦神}}, {{ill|Matsunoo Shrine|ja|松尾神}} * Kotohira Shrine ** Deities: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴神}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少名彦名神}} * Terumae Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|浪速根子武振熊命}} == Cultural Properties == * Takayama festival Floats Event (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * Mikoshi and Mikoshi Storehouse of Hachiman Shrine (Municipal Tangible Folk Cultural Property){{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/kurashi/1000021/1000119/1000847/1000915/1004125.html|title=民俗文化財 |publisher=高山市 |accessdate=2016-12-11}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Sakurayama-hachiman-gū}} * [http://www.sakurayamahachiman.jimdo.com/ Sakurayama Hachimangu] {{shinto-stub}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sakurayama Hachimangu | image = [[File:Sakurayama-hachimangu Takayama Gifu pref04s3s3990.jpg|250px]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E}}}} | location = [[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], [[Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], Sakuramachi 178 | coordinates = {{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E|scale:30000}} | deity = [[Emperor Ōjin]] | type = Former Prefectural Shrine, [[Beppyo Shrine|Beppyo Shrine]], Kanpei-sha | annual_festival = {{ill|October 9|ja|10月9日}}, {{ill|October 10|ja|10月10日}} (Takayama festival) | ceremonies = Shikinen Taisai }} '''Sakurayama Hachimangu''' (桜山八幡宮, officially '''櫻山八幡宮''') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], [[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], Japan. The autumn festival, along with the spring festival of {{ill|Hie Shrine (Takayama)|ja|日枝神社 (高山市)}}, is known as the {{ill|Takayama festival|ja|高山祭}}, which is designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazai/17768/mukei-minzoku/takayamamaturi.html |title=高山祭の屋台行事 |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2016-12-11}}. The main deity is the Hachiman deity, {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, and the enshrined deities include Atsuta Okami ({{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊命}}) and Kashi Okami ({{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}). == History == It is said that in the 65th year of the reign of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}, a bandit with two faces named {{ill|Ryomen Sukuna|ja|両面宿儺}} appeared in Hida Province. He was defeated by {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|武振熊命}}, the ancestor of the Wani clan, who prayed for victory to the former Emperor Ōjin. Another theory is that it was established as a protective Hachiman shrine by {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}} in various provinces. In the [[Edo period]], the Kanamori clan, the local lords, protected the shrine as their {{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神}} and made the residents north of the {{ill|Miyagawa (Gifu)|ja|宮川}} their parishioners. In the Meiji period, it was renamed Hachiman Shrine and became a Prefectural Shrine in 1932. The name Sakurayama Hachimangu has been used since after World War II. == Facilities == The {{ill|Takayama festival Yatai Kaikan|ja|高山祭屋台会館}} is located on the shrine grounds, where the festival floats used in the autumn Takayama festival are displayed throughout the year. File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref10s3s4020.jpg|Shrine gate File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref09n4110.jpg|Kotohira Shrine File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref07n4140.jpg|Inari Shrine File:Takayama_torii.jpg|Great Torii File:Sakurayama-hachiman_ninotorii.jpeg|Second Torii == Shrines within the Grounds == * Akiba Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Kagutsuchi|ja|火産霊神}} * Tenman Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} * Inari Shrine ** Deities: {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲霊神}}, {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦神}}, {{ill|Matsunoo Shrine|ja|松尾神}} * Kotohira Shrine ** Deities: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴神}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少名彦名神}} * Terumae Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|浪速根子武振熊命}} == Cultural Properties == * Takayama festival Floats Event (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * Mikoshi and Mikoshi Storehouse of Hachiman Shrine (Municipal Tangible Folk Cultural Property){{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/kurashi/1000021/1000119/1000847/1000915/1004125.html|title=民俗文化財 |publisher=高山市 |accessdate=2016-12-11}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Sakurayama-hachiman-gū}} * [http://www.sakurayamahachiman.jimdo.com/ Sakurayama Hachimangu] {{shinto-stub}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sakurayama Hachimangu | image = [[File:Sakurayama-hachimangu Takayama Gifu pref04s3s3990.jpg|250px]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E}}}} | location = [[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], [[Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], Sakuramachi 178 | coordinates = {{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E|scale:30000}} | deity = [[Emperor Ōjin]] | type = Former Prefectural Shrine, [[Beppyo Shrine|Beppyo Shrine]], Kanpei-sha | annual_festival = {{ill|October 9|ja|10月9日}}, {{ill|October 10|ja|10月10日}} (Takayama festival) | ceremonies = Shikinen Taisai }} '''Sakurayama Hachimangu''' (桜山八幡宮, officially '''櫻山八幡宮''') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], [[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], Japan. The autumn festival, along with the spring festival of {{ill|Hie Shrine (Takayama)|ja|日枝神社 (高山市)}}, is known as the {{ill|Takayama festival|ja|高山祭}}, which is designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazai/17768/mukei-minzoku/takayamamaturi.html |title=高山祭の屋台行事 |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2016-12-11}}. The main deity is the Hachiman deity, {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, and the enshrined deities include Atsuta Okami ({{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊命}}) and Kashi Okami ({{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}). == History == It is said that in the 65th year of the reign of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}, a bandit with two faces named {{ill|Ryomen Sukuna|ja|両面宿儺}} appeared in Hida Province. He was defeated by {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|武振熊命}}, the ancestor of the Wani clan, who prayed for victory to the former Emperor Ōjin. Another theory is that it was established as a protective Hachiman shrine by {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}} in various provinces. In the [[Edo period]], the Kanamori clan, the local lords, protected the shrine as their {{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神}} and made the residents north of the {{ill|Miyagawa (Gifu)|ja|宮川}} their parishioners. In the Meiji period, it was renamed Hachiman Shrine and became a Prefectural Shrine in 1932. The name Sakurayama Hachimangu has been used since after World War II. == Facilities == The {{ill|Takayama festival Yatai Kaikan|ja|高山祭屋台会館}} is located on the shrine grounds, where the festival floats used in the autumn Takayama festival are displayed throughout the year. File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref10s3s4020.jpg|Shrine gate File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref09n4110.jpg|Kotohira Shrine File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref07n4140.jpg|Inari Shrine File:Takayama_torii.jpg|Great Torii File:Sakurayama-hachiman_ninotorii.jpeg|Second Torii == Shrines within the Grounds == * Akiba Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Kagutsuchi|ja|火産霊神}} * Tenman Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|菅原道真}} * Inari Shrine ** Deities: {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲霊神}}, {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦神}}, {{ill|Matsunoo Shrine|ja|松尾神}} * Kotohira Shrine ** Deities: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴神}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少名彦名神}} * Terumae Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|浪速根子武振熊命}} == Cultural Properties == * Takayama festival Floats Event (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * Mikoshi and Mikoshi Storehouse of Hachiman Shrine (Municipal Tangible Folk Cultural Property){{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/kurashi/1000021/1000119/1000847/1000915/1004125.html|title=民俗文化財 |publisher=高山市 |accessdate=2016-12-11}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Sakurayama-hachiman-gū}} * [http://www.sakurayamahachiman.jimdo.com/ Sakurayama Hachimangu] {{shinto-stub}} revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sakurayama Hachimangu | image = [[File:Sakurayama-hachimangu Takayama Gifu pref04s3s3990.jpg|250px]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E}}}} | location = [[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], [[Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], Sakuramachi 178 | coordinates = {{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E|scale:30000}} | deity = [[Emperor Ōjin]] | type = Former Prefectural Shrine, [[Beppyo Shrine|Beppyo Shrine]], Kanpei-sha | annual_festival = {{ill|October 9|ja|10月9日}}, {{ill|October 10|ja|10月10日}} (Takayama festival) | ceremonies = Shikinen Taisai }} '''Sakurayama Hachimangu''' (桜山八幡宮, officially '''櫻山八幡宮''') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], [[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], Japan. The autumn festival, along with the spring festival of {{ill|Hie Shrine (Takayama)|ja|日枝神社 (高山市)}}, is known as the {{ill|Takayama festival|ja|高山祭}}, which is designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazai/17768/mukei-minzoku/takayamamaturi.html |title=高山祭の屋台行事 |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2016-12-11}}. The main deity is the Hachiman deity, {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, and the enshrined deities include Atsuta Okami ({{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊命}}) and Kashi Okami ({{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}). == History == It is said that in the 65th year of the reign of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}, a bandit with two faces named {{ill|Ryomen Sukuna|ja|両面宿儺}} appeared in Hida Province. He was defeated by {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|武振熊命}}, the ancestor of the Wani clan, who prayed for victory to the former Emperor Ōjin. Another theory is that it was established as a protective Hachiman shrine by {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}} in various provinces. In the [[Edo period]], the Kanamori clan, the local lords, protected the shrine as their {{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神}} and made the residents north of the {{ill|Miyagawa (Gifu)|ja|宮川}} their parishioners. In the Meiji period, it was renamed Hachiman Shrine and became a Prefectural Shrine in 1932. The name Sakurayama Hachimangu has been used since after World War II. == Facilities == The {{ill|Takayama festival Yatai Kaikan|ja|高山祭屋台会館}} is located on the shrine grounds, where the festival floats used in the autumn Takayama festival are displayed throughout the year. File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref10s3s4020.jpg|Shrine gate File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref09n4110.jpg|Kotohira Shrine File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref07n4140.jpg|Inari Shrine File:Takayama_torii.jpg|Great Torii File:Sakurayama-hachiman_ninotorii.jpeg|Second Torii == Shrines within the Grounds == * Akiba Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Kagutsuchi|ja|火産霊神}} * Tenman Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} * Inari Shrine ** Deities: {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲霊神}}, {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦神}}, {{ill|Matsunoo Shrine|ja|松尾神}} * Kotohira Shrine ** Deities: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴神}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少名彦名神}} * Terumae Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|浪速根子武振熊命}} == Cultural Properties == * Takayama festival Floats Event (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * Mikoshi and Mikoshi Storehouse of Hachiman Shrine (Municipal Tangible Folk Cultural Property){{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/kurashi/1000021/1000119/1000847/1000915/1004125.html|title=民俗文化財 |publisher=高山市 |accessdate=2016-12-11}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sakurayama Hachimangu | image = [[File:Sakurayama-hachimangu Takayama Gifu pref04s3s3990.jpg|250px]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E}}}} | location = [[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], [[Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], Sakuramachi 178 | coordinates = {{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E|scale:30000}} | deity = [[Emperor Ōjin]] | type = Former Prefectural Shrine, [[Beppyo Shrine|Beppyo Shrine]], Kanpei-sha | annual_festival = {{ill|October 9|ja|10月9日}}, {{ill|October 10|ja|10月10日}} (Takayama festival) | ceremonies = Shikinen Taisai }} '''Sakurayama Hachimangu''' (桜山八幡宮, officially '''櫻山八幡宮''') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], [[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], Japan. The autumn festival, along with the spring festival of {{ill|Hie Shrine (Takayama)|ja|日枝神社 (高山市)}}, is known as the {{ill|Takayama festival|ja|高山祭}}, which is designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazai/17768/mukei-minzoku/takayamamaturi.html |title=高山祭の屋台行事 |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2016-12-11}}. The main deity is the Hachiman deity, {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, and the enshrined deities include Atsuta Okami ({{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊命}}) and Kashi Okami ({{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}). == History == It is said that in the 65th year of the reign of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}, a bandit with two faces named {{ill|Ryomen Sukuna|ja|両面宿儺}} appeared in Hida Province. He was defeated by {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|武振熊命}}, the ancestor of the Wani clan, who prayed for victory to the former Emperor Ōjin. Another theory is that it was established as a protective Hachiman shrine by {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}} in various provinces. In the [[Edo period]], the Kanamori clan, the local lords, protected the shrine as their {{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神}} and made the residents north of the {{ill|Miyagawa (Gifu)|ja|宮川}} their parishioners. In the Meiji period, it was renamed Hachiman Shrine and became a Prefectural Shrine in 1932. The name Sakurayama Hachimangu has been used since after World War II. == Facilities == The {{ill|Takayama festival Yatai Kaikan|ja|高山祭屋台会館}} is located on the shrine grounds, where the festival floats used in the autumn Takayama festival are displayed throughout the year. File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref10s3s4020.jpg|Shrine gate File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref09n4110.jpg|Kotohira Shrine File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref07n4140.jpg|Inari Shrine File:Takayama_torii.jpg|Great Torii File:Sakurayama-hachiman_ninotorii.jpeg|Second Torii == Shrines within the Grounds == * Akiba Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Kagutsuchi|ja|火産霊神}} * Tenman Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} * Inari Shrine ** Deities: {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲霊神}}, {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦神}}, {{ill|Matsunoo Shrine|ja|松尾神}} * Kotohira Shrine ** Deities: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴神}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少名彦名神}} * Terumae Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|浪速根子武振熊命}} == Cultural Properties == * Takayama festival Floats Event (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * Mikoshi and Mikoshi Storehouse of Hachiman Shrine (Municipal Tangible Folk Cultural Property){{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/kurashi/1000021/1000119/1000847/1000915/1004125.html|title=民俗文化財 |publisher=高山市 |accessdate=2016-12-11}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sakurayama Hachimangu | image = [[File:Sakurayama-hachimangu Takayama Gifu pref04s3s3990.jpg|250px]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E}}}} | location = [[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], [[Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], Sakuramachi 178 | coordinates = {{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E|scale:30000}} | deity = [[Emperor Ōjin]] | type = Former Prefectural Shrine, [[Beppyo Shrine|Beppyo Shrine]], Kanpei-sha | annual_festival = {{ill|October 9|ja|10月9日}}, {{ill|October 10|ja|10月10日}} (Takayama festival) | ceremonies = Shikinen Taisai }} '''Sakurayama Hachimangu''' (桜山八幡宮, officially '''櫻山八幡宮''') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], [[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], Japan. The autumn festival, along with the spring festival of {{ill|Hie Shrine (Takayama)|ja|日枝神社 (高山市)}}, is known as the {{ill|Takayama festival|ja|高山祭}}, which is designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazai/17768/mukei-minzoku/takayamamaturi.html |title=高山祭の屋台行事 |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2016-12-11}}. The main deity is the Hachiman deity, {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, and the enshrined deities include Atsuta Okami ({{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊命}}) and Kashi Okami ({{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}). == History == It is said that in the 65th year of the reign of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}, a bandit with two faces named {{ill|Ryomen Sukuna|ja|両面宿儺}} appeared in Hida Province. He was defeated by {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|武振熊命}}, the ancestor of the Wani clan, who prayed for victory to the former Emperor Ōjin. Another theory is that it was established as a protective Hachiman shrine by {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}} in various provinces. In the [[Edo period]], the Kanamori clan, the local lords, protected the shrine as their {{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神}} and made the residents north of the {{ill|Miyagawa (Gifu)|ja|宮川}} their parishioners. In the Meiji period, it was renamed Hachiman Shrine and became a Prefectural Shrine in 1932. The name Sakurayama Hachimangu has been used since after World War II. == Facilities == The {{ill|Takayama festival Yatai Kaikan|ja|高山祭屋台会館}} is located on the shrine grounds, where the festival floats used in the autumn Takayama festival are displayed throughout the year. File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref10s3s4020.jpg|Shrine gate File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref09n4110.jpg|Kotohira Shrine File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref07n4140.jpg|Inari Shrine File:Takayama_torii.jpg|Great Torii File:Sakurayama-hachiman_ninotorii.jpeg|Second Torii == Shrines within the Grounds == * Akiba Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Kagutsuchi|ja|火産霊神}} * Tenman Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} * Inari Shrine ** Deities: {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲霊神}}, {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦神}}, {{ill|Matsunoo Shrine|ja|松尾神}} * Kotohira Shrine ** Deities: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴神}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少名彦名神}} * Terumae Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|浪速根子武振熊命}} == Cultural Properties == * Takayama festival Floats Event (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * Mikoshi and Mikoshi Storehouse of Hachiman Shrine (Municipal Tangible Folk Cultural Property){{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/kurashi/1000021/1000119/1000847/1000915/1004125.html|title=民俗文化財 |publisher=高山市 |accessdate=2016-12-11}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sakurayama Hachimangu | image = [[File:Sakurayama-hachimangu Takayama Gifu pref04s3s3990.jpg|250px]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E}}}} | location = [[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], [[Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], Sakuramachi 178 | coordinates = {{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E|scale:30000}} | deity = [[Emperor Ōjin]] | type = Former Prefectural Shrine, [[Beppyo Shrine|Beppyo Shrine]], Kanpei-sha | annual_festival = {{ill|October 9|ja|10月9日}}, {{ill|October 10|ja|10月10日}} (Takayama festival) | ceremonies = Shikinen Taisai }} '''Sakurayama Hachimangu''' (桜山八幡宮, officially '''櫻山八幡宮''') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], [[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], Japan. The autumn festival, along with the spring festival of {{ill|Hie Shrine (Takayama)|ja|日枝神社 (高山市)}}, is known as the {{ill|Takayama festival|ja|高山祭}}, which is designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazai/17768/mukei-minzoku/takayamamaturi.html |title=高山祭の屋台行事 |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2016-12-11}}. The main deity is the Hachiman deity, {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, and the enshrined deities include Atsuta Okami ({{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊命}}) and Kashi Okami ({{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}). == History == It is said that in the 65th year of the reign of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}, a bandit with two faces named {{ill|Ryomen Sukuna|ja|両面宿儺}} appeared in Hida Province. He was defeated by {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|武振熊命}}, the ancestor of the Wani clan, who prayed for victory to the former Emperor Ōjin. Another theory is that it was established as a protective Hachiman shrine by {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}} in various provinces. In the [[Edo period]], the Kanamori clan, the local lords, protected the shrine as their {{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神}} and made the residents north of the {{ill|Miyagawa (Gifu)|ja|宮川}} their parishioners. In the Meiji period, it was renamed Hachiman Shrine and became a Prefectural Shrine in 1932. The name Sakurayama Hachimangu has been used since after World War II. == Facilities == The {{ill|Takayama festival Yatai Kaikan|ja|高山祭屋台会館}} is located on the shrine grounds, where the festival floats used in the autumn Takayama festival are displayed throughout the year. File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref10s3s4020.jpg|Shrine gate File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref09n4110.jpg|Kotohira Shrine File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref07n4140.jpg|Inari Shrine File:Takayama_torii.jpg|Great Torii File:Sakurayama-hachiman_ninotorii.jpeg|Second Torii == Shrines within the Grounds == * Akiba Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Kagutsuchi|ja|火産霊神}} * Tenman Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} * Inari Shrine ** Deities: {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲霊神}}, {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦神}}, {{ill|Matsunoo Shrine|ja|松尾神}} * Kotohira Shrine ** Deities: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴神}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少名彦名神}} * Terumae Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|浪速根子武振熊命}} == Cultural Properties == * Takayama festival Floats Event (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * Mikoshi and Mikoshi Storehouse of Hachiman Shrine (Municipal Tangible Folk Cultural Property){{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/kurashi/1000021/1000119/1000847/1000915/1004125.html|title=民俗文化財 |publisher=高山市 |accessdate=2016-12-11}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sakurayama Hachimangu | image = [[File:Sakurayama-hachimangu Takayama Gifu pref04s3s3990.jpg|250px]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E}}}} | location = [[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], [[Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], Sakuramachi 178 | coordinates = {{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E|scale:30000}} | deity = [[Emperor Ōjin]] | type = Former Prefectural Shrine, [[Beppyo Shrine|Beppyo Shrine]], Kanpei-sha | annual_festival = {{ill|October 9|ja|10月9日}}, {{ill|October 10|ja|10月10日}} (Takayama festival) | ceremonies = Shikinen Taisai }} '''Sakurayama Hachimangu''' (桜山八幡宮, officially '''櫻山八幡宮''') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], [[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], Japan. The autumn festival, along with the spring festival of {{ill|Hie Shrine (Takayama)|ja|日枝神社 (高山市)}}, is known as the {{ill|Takayama festival|ja|高山祭}}, which is designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazai/17768/mukei-minzoku/takayamamaturi.html |title=高山祭の屋台行事 |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2016-12-11}}. The main deity is the Hachiman deity, {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, and the enshrined deities include Atsuta Okami ({{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊命}}) and Kashi Okami ({{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}). == History == It is said that in the 65th year of the reign of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}, a bandit with two faces named {{ill|Ryomen Sukuna|ja|両面宿儺}} appeared in Hida Province. He was defeated by {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|武振熊命}}, the ancestor of the Wani clan, who prayed for victory to the former Emperor Ōjin. Another theory is that it was established as a protective Hachiman shrine by {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}} in various provinces. In the [[Edo period]], the Kanamori clan, the local lords, protected the shrine as their {{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神}} and made the residents north of the {{ill|Miyagawa (Gifu)|ja|宮川}} their parishioners. In the Meiji period, it was renamed Hachiman Shrine and became a Prefectural Shrine in 1932. The name Sakurayama Hachimangu has been used since after World War II. == Facilities == The {{ill|Takayama festival Yatai Kaikan|ja|高山祭屋台会館}} is located on the shrine grounds, where the festival floats used in the autumn Takayama festival are displayed throughout the year. File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref10s3s4020.jpg|Shrine gate File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref09n4110.jpg|Kotohira Shrine File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref07n4140.jpg|Inari Shrine File:Takayama_torii.jpg|Great Torii File:Sakurayama-hachiman_ninotorii.jpeg|Second Torii == Shrines within the Grounds == * Akiba Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Kagutsuchi|ja|火産霊神}} * Tenman Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} * Inari Shrine ** Deities: {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲霊神}}, {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦神}}, {{ill|Matsunoo Shrine|ja|松尾神}} * Kotohira Shrine ** Deities: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴神}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少名彦名神}} * Terumae Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|浪速根子武振熊命}} == Cultural Properties == * Takayama festival Floats Event (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * Mikoshi and Mikoshi Storehouse of Hachiman Shrine (Municipal Tangible Folk Cultural Property){{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/kurashi/1000021/1000119/1000847/1000915/1004125.html|title=民俗文化財 |publisher=高山市 |accessdate=2016-12-11}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sakurayama Hachimangu | image = [[File:Sakurayama-hachimangu Takayama Gifu pref04s3s3990.jpg|250px]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E}}}} | location = [[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], [[Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], Sakuramachi 178 | coordinates = {{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E|scale:30000}} | deity = [[Emperor Ōjin]] | type = Former Prefectural Shrine, [[Beppyo Shrine|Beppyo Shrine]], Kanpei-sha | annual_festival = {{ill|October 9|ja|10月9日}}, {{ill|October 10|ja|10月10日}} (Takayama festival) | ceremonies = Shikinen Taisai }} '''Sakurayama Hachimangu''' (桜山八幡宮, officially '''櫻山八幡宮''') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], [[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], Japan. The autumn festival, along with the spring festival of {{ill|Hie Shrine (Takayama)|ja|日枝神社 (高山市)}}, is known as the {{ill|Takayama festival|ja|高山祭}}, which is designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazai/17768/mukei-minzoku/takayamamaturi.html |title=高山祭の屋台行事 |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2016-12-11}}. The main deity is the Hachiman deity, {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, and the enshrined deities include Atsuta Okami ({{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊命}}) and Kashi Okami ({{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}). == History == It is said that in the 65th year of the reign of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}, a bandit with two faces named {{ill|Ryomen Sukuna|ja|両面宿儺}} appeared in Hida Province. He was defeated by {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|武振熊命}}, the ancestor of the Wani clan, who prayed for victory to the former Emperor Ōjin. Another theory is that it was established as a protective Hachiman shrine by {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}} in various provinces. In the [[Edo period]], the Kanamori clan, the local lords, protected the shrine as their {{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神}} and made the residents north of the {{ill|Miyagawa (Gifu)|ja|宮川}} their parishioners. In the Meiji period, it was renamed Hachiman Shrine and became a Prefectural Shrine in 1932. The name Sakurayama Hachimangu has been used since after World War II. == Facilities == The {{ill|Takayama festival Yatai Kaikan|ja|高山祭屋台会館}} is located on the shrine grounds, where the festival floats used in the autumn Takayama festival are displayed throughout the year. File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref10s3s4020.jpg|Shrine gate File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref09n4110.jpg|Kotohira Shrine File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref07n4140.jpg|Inari Shrine File:Takayama_torii.jpg|Great Torii File:Sakurayama-hachiman_ninotorii.jpeg|Second Torii == Shrines within the Grounds == * Akiba Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Kagutsuchi|ja|火産霊神}} * Tenman Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} * Inari Shrine ** Deities: {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲霊神}}, {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦神}}, {{ill|Matsunoo Shrine|ja|松尾神}} * Kotohira Shrine ** Deities: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴神}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少名彦名神}} * Terumae Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|浪速根子武振熊命}} == Cultural Properties == * Takayama festival Floats Event (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * Mikoshi and Mikoshi Storehouse of Hachiman Shrine (Municipal Tangible Folk Cultural Property){{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/kurashi/1000021/1000119/1000847/1000915/1004125.html|title=民俗文化財 |publisher=高山市 |accessdate=2016-12-11}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links ==
