revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sakurayama Hachimangu | image = [[File:Sakurayama-hachimangu Takayama Gifu pref04s3s3990.jpg|250px]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E}}}} | location = [[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], [[Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], Sakuramachi 178 | coordinates = {{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E|scale:30000}} | deity = [[Emperor Ōjin]] | type = Former Prefectural Shrine, [[Beppyo Shrine|Beppyo Shrine]], Kanpei-sha | annual_festival = {{ill|October 9|ja|10月9日}}, {{ill|October 10|ja|10月10日}} (Takayama festival) | ceremonies = Shikinen Taisai }} '''Sakurayama Hachimangu''' (桜山八幡宮, officially '''櫻山八幡宮''') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], [[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], Japan. The autumn festival, along with the spring festival of {{ill|Hie Shrine (Takayama)|ja|日枝神社 (高山市)}}, is known as the {{ill|Takayama festival|ja|高山祭}}, which is designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazai/17768/mukei-minzoku/takayamamaturi.html |title=高山祭の屋台行事 |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2016-12-11}}. The main deity is the Hachiman deity, {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇}}, and the enshrined deities include Atsuta Okami ({{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊命}}) and Kashi Okami ({{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇}} and {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后}}). == History == It is said that in the 65th year of the reign of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇}}, a bandit with two faces named {{ill|Ryomen Sukuna|ja|両面宿儺}} appeared in Hida Province. He was defeated by {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|武振熊命}}, the ancestor of the Wani clan, who prayed for victory to the former Emperor Ōjin. Another theory is that it was established as a protective Hachiman shrine by {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇}} in various provinces. In the [[Edo period]], the Kanamori clan, the local lords, protected the shrine as their {{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神}} and made the residents north of the {{ill|Miyagawa (Gifu)|ja|宮川}} their parishioners. In the Meiji period, it was renamed Hachiman Shrine and became a Prefectural Shrine in 1932. The name Sakurayama Hachimangu has been used since after World War II. == Facilities == The {{ill|Takayama festival Yatai Kaikan|ja|高山祭屋台会館}} is located on the shrine grounds, where the festival floats used in the autumn Takayama festival are displayed throughout the year. File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref10s3s4020.jpg|Shrine gate File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref09n4110.jpg|Kotohira Shrine File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref07n4140.jpg|Inari Shrine File:Takayama_torii.jpg|Great Torii File:Sakurayama-hachiman_ninotorii.jpeg|Second Torii == Shrines within the Grounds == * Akiba Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Kagutsuchi|ja|火産霊神}} * Tenman Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane}} * Inari Shrine ** Deities: {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲霊神}}, {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦神}}, {{ill|Matsunoo Shrine|ja|松尾神}} * Kotohira Shrine ** Deities: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神}}, {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴神}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少名彦名神}} * Terumae Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|浪速根子武振熊命}} == Cultural Properties == * Takayama festival Floats Event (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * Mikoshi and Mikoshi Storehouse of Hachiman Shrine (Municipal Tangible Folk Cultural Property){{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/kurashi/1000021/1000119/1000847/1000915/1004125.html|title=民俗文化財 |publisher=高山市 |accessdate=2016-12-11}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Sakurayama-hachiman-gū}} * [http://www.sakurayamahachiman.jimdo.com/ Sakurayama Hachimangu] {{shinto-stub}} {{Hachiman Faith}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto In the [[Edo period]], the Kanamori clan, the local lords, protected the shrine as their {{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神|12=draft}} and made the residents north of the {{ill|Miyagawa (Gifu)|ja|宮川|12=draft}} their parishioners. In the Meiji period, it was renamed Hachiman Shrine and became a Prefectural Shrine in 1932. The name Sakurayama Hachimangu has been used since after World War II. == Facilities == The {{ill|Takayama festival Yatai Kaikan|ja|高山祭屋台会館|12=draft}} is located on the shrine grounds, where the festival floats used in the autumn Takayama festival are displayed throughout the year. The autumn festival, along with the spring festival of {{ill|Hie Shrine (Takayama)|ja|日枝神社 (高山市)|12=draft}}, is known as the {{ill|Takayama festival|ja|高山祭|12=draft}}, which is designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazai/17768/mukei-minzoku/takayamamaturi.html |title=高山祭の屋台行事 |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2016-12-11}}. The main deity is the Hachiman deity, {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|12=draft}}, and the enshrined deities include Atsuta Okami ({{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊命|12=draft}}) and Kashi Okami ({{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|12=draft}} and {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|12=draft}}). == History == It is said that in the 65th year of the reign of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇|12=draft}}, a bandit with two faces named {{ill|Ryomen Sukuna|ja|両面宿儺|12=draft}} appeared in Hida Province. He was defeated by {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|武振熊命|12=draft}}, the ancestor of the Wani clan, who prayed for victory to the former Emperor Ōjin. Another theory is that it was established as a protective Hachiman shrine by {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇|12=draft}} in various provinces. In the [[Edo period]], the Kanamori clan, the local lords, protected the shrine as their {{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神|12=draft}} and made the residents north of the {{ill|Miyagawa (Gifu)|ja|宮川|12=draft}} their parishioners. In the Meiji period, it was renamed Hachiman Shrine and became a Prefectural Shrine in 1932. The name Sakurayama Hachimangu has been used since after World War II. == Facilities == The {{ill|Takayama festival Yatai Kaikan|ja|高山祭屋台会館|12=draft}} is located on the shrine grounds, where the festival floats used in the autumn Takayama festival are displayed throughout the year. File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref10s3s4020.jpg|Shrine gate File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref09n4110.jpg|Kotohira Shrine File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref07n4140.jpg|Inari Shrine File:Takayama_torii.jpg|Great Torii File:Sakurayama-hachiman_ninotorii.jpeg|Second Torii == Shrines within the Grounds == * Akiba Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Kagutsuchi|ja|火産霊神|12=draft}} * Tenman Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|12=draft}} * Inari Shrine ** Deities: {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲霊神|12=draft}}, {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦神|12=draft}}, {{ill|Matsunoo Shrine|ja|松尾神|12=draft}} * Kotohira Shrine ** Deities: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|12=draft}}, {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴神|12=draft}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少名彦名神|12=draft}} * Terumae Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|浪速根子武振熊命|12=draft}} == Cultural Properties == * Takayama festival Floats Event (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * Mikoshi and Mikoshi Storehouse of Hachiman Shrine (Municipal Tangible Folk Cultural Property){{Cite Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E}}}} | location = [[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], [[Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], Sakuramachi 178 | coordinates = {{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E|scale:30000}} | deity = [[Emperor Ōjin]] | type = Former Prefectural Shrine, [[Beppyo Shrine|Beppyo Shrine]], Kanpei-sha | annual_festival = {{ill|October 9|ja|10月9日|12=draft}}, {{ill|October 10|ja|10月10日|12=draft}} (Takayama festival) | ceremonies = Shikinen Taisai }} '''Sakurayama Hachimangu''' (桜山八幡宮, officially '''櫻山八幡宮''') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], [[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], Japan. The autumn festival, along with the spring festival of {{ill|Hie Shrine (Takayama)|ja|日枝神社 (高山市)|12=draft}}, is known as the {{ill|Takayama festival|ja|高山祭|12=draft}}, which is designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazai/17768/mukei-minzoku/takayamamaturi.html |title=高山祭の屋台行事 |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2016-12-11}}. The main deity is the Hachiman deity, {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|12=draft}}, and the enshrined deities include Atsuta Okami ({{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊命|12=draft}}) and Kashi Okami ({{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|12=draft}} and {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|12=draft}}). == History == It is said that in the 65th year of the reign of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇|12=draft}}, a bandit with two faces named {{ill|Ryomen Sukuna|ja|両面宿儺|12=draft}} appeared in Hida Province. He was defeated by {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|武振熊命|12=draft}}, the ancestor of the Wani clan, who prayed for victory to the former Emperor Ōjin. Another theory is that it was established as a protective Hachiman shrine by {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇|12=draft}} in various provinces. In the [[Edo period]], the Kanamori clan, the local lords, protected the shrine as their {{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神|12=draft}} and made the residents north of the {{ill|Miyagawa (Gifu)|ja|宮川|12=draft}} their parishioners. In the Meiji period, it was renamed Hachiman Shrine and became a Prefectural Shrine in 1932. The name Sakurayama Hachimangu has been used since after World War II. == Facilities == The {{ill|Takayama festival Yatai Kaikan|ja|高山祭屋台会館|12=draft}} is located on the shrine grounds, where the festival floats used in the autumn Takayama festival are displayed throughout the year. File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref10s3s4020.jpg|Shrine gate File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref09n4110.jpg|Kotohira Shrine File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref07n4140.jpg|Inari Shrine File:Takayama_torii.jpg|Great Torii File:Sakurayama-hachiman_ninotorii.jpeg|Second Torii == Shrines within the Grounds == * Akiba Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Kagutsuchi|ja|火産霊神|12=draft}} * Tenman Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|12=draft}} * Inari Shrine ** Deities: {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲霊神|12=draft}}, {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦神|12=draft}}, {{ill|Matsunoo Shrine|ja|松尾神|12=draft}} * Kotohira Shrine ** Deities: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|12=draft}}, {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴神|12=draft}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少名彦名神|12=draft}} * Terumae Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|浪速根子武振熊命|12=draft}} == Cultural Properties == * Takayama festival Floats Event (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * Mikoshi and Mikoshi Storehouse of Hachiman Shrine (Municipal Tangible Folk Cultural Property){{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/kurashi/1000021/1000119/1000847/1000915/1004125.html|title=民俗文化財 |publisher=高山市 |accessdate=2016-12-11}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Sakurayama-hachiman-gū}} * [http://www.sakurayamahachiman.jimdo.com/ Sakurayama Hachimangu] {{shinto-stub}} {{Hachiman Faith}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sakurayama Hachimangu | image = [[File:Sakurayama-hachimangu Takayama Gifu pref04s3s3990.jpg|250px]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E}}}} | location = [[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], [[Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], Sakuramachi 178 | coordinates = {{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E|scale:30000}} | deity = [[Emperor Ōjin]] | type = Former Prefectural Shrine, [[Beppyo Shrine|Beppyo Shrine]], Kanpei-sha | annual_festival = {{ill|October 9|ja|10月9日|12=draft}}, {{ill|October 10|ja|10月10日|12=draft}} (Takayama festival) | ceremonies = Shikinen Taisai }} '''Sakurayama Hachimangu''' (桜山八幡宮, officially '''櫻山八幡宮''') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], [[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], Japan. The autumn festival, along with the spring festival of {{ill|Hie Shrine (Takayama)|ja|日枝神社 (高山市)|12=draft}}, is known as the {{ill|Takayama festival|ja|高山祭|12=draft}}, which is designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazai/17768/mukei-minzoku/takayamamaturi.html |title=高山祭の屋台行事 |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2016-12-11}}. The main deity is the Hachiman deity, {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|12=draft}}, and the enshrined deities include Atsuta Okami ({{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊命|12=draft}}) and Kashi Okami ({{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|12=draft}} and {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|12=draft}}). == History == It is said that in the 65th year of the reign of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇|12=draft}}, a bandit with two faces named {{ill|Ryomen Sukuna|ja|両面宿儺|12=draft}} appeared in Hida Province. He was defeated by {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|武振熊命|12=draft}}, the ancestor of the Wani clan, who prayed for victory to the former Emperor Ōjin. Another theory is that it was established as a protective Hachiman shrine by {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇|12=draft}} in various provinces. In the [[Edo period]], the Kanamori clan, the local lords, protected the shrine as their {{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神|12=draft}} and made the residents north of the {{ill|Miyagawa (Gifu)|ja|宮川|12=draft}} their parishioners. In the Meiji period, it was renamed Hachiman Shrine and became a Prefectural Shrine in 1932. The name Sakurayama Hachimangu has been used since after World War II. == Facilities == The {{ill|Takayama festival Yatai Kaikan|ja|高山祭屋台会館|12=draft}} is located on the shrine grounds, where the festival floats used in the autumn Takayama festival are displayed throughout the year. File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref10s3s4020.jpg|Shrine gate File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref09n4110.jpg|Kotohira Shrine File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref07n4140.jpg|Inari Shrine File:Takayama_torii.jpg|Great Torii File:Sakurayama-hachiman_ninotorii.jpeg|Second Torii == Shrines within the Grounds == * Akiba Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Kagutsuchi|ja|火産霊神|12=draft}} * Tenman Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|12=draft}} * Inari Shrine ** Deities: {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲霊神|12=draft}}, {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦神|12=draft}}, {{ill|Matsunoo Shrine|ja|松尾神|12=draft}} * Kotohira Shrine ** Deities: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|12=draft}}, {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴神|12=draft}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少名彦名神|12=draft}} * Terumae Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|浪速根子武振熊命|12=draft}} == Cultural Properties == * Takayama festival Floats Event (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * Mikoshi and Mikoshi Storehouse of Hachiman Shrine (Municipal Tangible Folk Cultural Property){{Cite {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sakurayama Hachimangu | image = [[File:Sakurayama-hachimangu Takayama Gifu pref04s3s3990.jpg|250px]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E}}}} | location = [[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], [[Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], Sakuramachi 178 | coordinates = {{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E|scale:30000}} | deity = [[Emperor Ōjin]] | type = Former Prefectural Shrine, [[Beppyo Shrine|Beppyo Shrine]], Kanpei-sha | annual_festival = {{ill|October 9|ja|10月9日|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|October 10|ja|10月10日|12=draft|12=draft}} (Takayama festival) | ceremonies = Shikinen Taisai }} '''Sakurayama Hachimangu''' (桜山八幡宮, officially '''櫻山八幡宮''') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], [[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], Japan. The autumn festival, along with the spring festival of {{ill|Hie Shrine (Takayama)|ja|日枝神社 (高山市)|12=draft|12=draft}}, is known as the {{ill|Takayama festival|ja|高山祭|12=draft|12=draft}}, which is designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazai/17768/mukei-minzoku/takayamamaturi.html |title=高山祭の屋台行事 |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2016-12-11}}. The main deity is the Hachiman deity, {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}, and the enshrined deities include Atsuta Okami ({{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊命|12=draft|12=draft}}) and Kashi Okami ({{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|12=draft|12=draft}}). == History == It is said that in the 65th year of the reign of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}, a bandit with two faces named {{ill|Ryomen Sukuna|ja|両面宿儺|12=draft|12=draft}} appeared in Hida Province. He was defeated by {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|武振熊命|12=draft|12=draft}}, the ancestor of the Wani clan, who prayed for victory to the former Emperor Ōjin. Another theory is that it was established as a protective Hachiman shrine by {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} in various provinces. In the [[Edo period]], the Kanamori clan, the local lords, protected the shrine as their {{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神|12=draft|12=draft}} and made the residents north of the {{ill|Miyagawa (Gifu)|ja|宮川|12=draft|12=draft}} their parishioners. In the Meiji period, it was renamed Hachiman Shrine and became a Prefectural Shrine in 1932. The name Sakurayama Hachimangu has been used since after World War II. == Facilities == The {{ill|Takayama festival Yatai Kaikan|ja|高山祭屋台会館|12=draft|12=draft}} is located on the shrine grounds, where the festival floats used in the autumn Takayama festival are displayed throughout the year. File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref10s3s4020.jpg|Shrine gate File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref09n4110.jpg|Kotohira Shrine File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref07n4140.jpg|Inari Shrine File:Takayama_torii.jpg|Great Torii File:Sakurayama-hachiman_ninotorii.jpeg|Second Torii == Shrines within the Grounds == * Akiba Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Kagutsuchi|ja|火産霊神|12=draft|12=draft}} * Tenman Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|12=draft|12=draft}} * Inari Shrine ** Deities: {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲霊神|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦神|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Matsunoo Shrine|ja|松尾神|12=draft|12=draft}} * Kotohira Shrine ** Deities: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴神|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少名彦名神|12=draft|12=draft}} * Terumae Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|浪速根子武振熊命|12=draft|12=draft}} == Cultural Properties == * Takayama festival Floats Event (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * Mikoshi and Mikoshi Storehouse of Hachiman Shrine (Municipal Tangible Folk Cultural Property){{Cite web|和書|date= Another theory is that it was established as a protective Hachiman shrine by {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} in various provinces. revision.text:
{{神社 | 名称=櫻山八幡宮 | 画像=[[ファイル:Sakurayama-hachimangu Takayama Gifu pref04s3s3990.jpg|250px]]
拝殿 | 所在地=[[岐阜県]][[高山市]]桜町178 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E|scale:30000}} | 祭神=[[応神天皇]] | 社格=旧[[県社]]・[[別表神社]]・[[金幣社]] | 例祭=[[10月9日]]・[[10月10日|10日]]（高山祭） | 神事=式年大祭 | 地図={{Location map|Japan Gifu|label=櫻山八幡宮|coordinates= {{coord2|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E}}|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E}}}} }} '''桜山八幡宮'''（さくらやまはちまんぐう）（正字は'''櫻山八幡宮'''）は、[[岐阜県]][[高山市]]にある[[神社]]である。秋の例祭は、春の[[日枝神社 (高山市)|日枝神社]]の例祭とともに[[高山祭]]として知られ、国の重要無形民俗文化財に指定されている{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazai/17768/mukei-minzoku/takayamamaturi.html |title=高山祭の屋台行事 |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2016-12-11}}。 [[八幡神|八幡大神]]（[[応神天皇]]）を主祭神とし、相殿に熱田大神（[[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊命]]）・香椎大神（[[仲哀天皇]]・[[神功皇后]]）を祀る。 ==歴史== [[仁徳天皇]]65年、飛騨国に二つの顔を持つ[[両面宿儺|宿儺]]（すくな）という賊が現れ、それを討伐するために派遣された[[和珥氏]]の祖・[[武振熊命|難波根子武振熊命]]が戦勝祈願のために先代の応神天皇を祀ったのに始まると伝える。また、[[聖武天皇]]が諸国に設けた護国八幡だという説もある。 [[江戸時代]]に[[金森氏]]が当地の領主となったとき[[氏神]]として当社を保護し、[[宮川 (岐阜県)|宮川]]以北の住民を氏子とした。 明治時代に八幡神社に改称し、昭和７年に県社になる。桜山八幡宮の名は戦後からという。 ==施設== 境内に[[高山祭屋台会館]]があり、秋の高山祭に実際に曳き出される屋台が通年展示されている。 ファイル:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref10s3s4020.jpg|神門前 ファイル:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref09n4110.jpg|琴平神社 ファイル:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref07n4140.jpg|稲荷神社 ファイル:Takayama_torii.jpg|大鳥居 ファイル:Sakurayama-hachiman_ninotorii.jpeg|一の鳥居 == 境内社 == *秋葉神社 *:祭神・[[火産霊神|火結神]] *天満神社 *:祭神・[[菅原道真公]] *稲荷神社 *:祭神・[[倉稲魂命|倉稲霊神]]、[[猿田彦神]]、[[松尾大社|松尾神]] *琴平神社 *:祭神・[[大物主神]]、[[崇徳天皇]]、[[大己貴神]]、[[少彦名命|少名彦名神]] *照前神社 *:祭神・[[武振熊命|浪速根子武振熊命]] == 文化財 == *高山祭の屋台行事（国の[[重要無形民俗文化財]]） *八幡宮御輿及び御輿庫（市指定有形民俗文化財）{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/kurashi/1000021/1000119/1000847/1000915/1004125.html|title=民俗文化財 |publisher=高山市 |accessdate=2016-12-11}} == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} ==外部リンク== {{Commonscat|Sakurayama-hachiman-gū}} *[http://www.sakurayamahachiman.jimdo.com/ 櫻山八幡宮] {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:さくらやまはちまんくう}} [[Category:岐阜県の神社]] [[Category:高山市の歴史]] [[Category:岐阜県の旧県社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:八幡宮]] [[Category:区市町村指定有形民俗文化財]] [[Category:高山市の建築物]]

(岐阜県)|宮川]]以北の住民を氏子とした。 明治時代に八幡神社に改称し、昭和７年に県社になる。桜山八幡宮の名は戦後からという。 ==施設== 境内に[[高山祭屋台会館]]があり、秋の高山祭に実際に曳き出される屋台が通年展示されている。 ファイル:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref10s3s4020.jpg|神門前 {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sakurayama Hachimangu | image = [[File:Sakurayama-hachimangu Takayama Gifu pref04s3s3990.jpg|250px]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E}}}} | location = [[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], [[Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], Sakuramachi 178 | coordinates = {{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E|scale:30000}} | deity = [[Emperor Ōjin]] | type = Former Prefectural Shrine, [[Beppyo Shrine|Beppyo Shrine]], Kanpei-sha | annual_festival = {{ill|October 9|ja|10月9日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|October 10|ja|10月10日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Takayama festival) | ceremonies = Shikinen Taisai }} '''Sakurayama Hachimangu''' (桜山八幡宮, officially '''櫻山八幡宮''') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], [[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], Japan. The autumn festival, along with the spring festival of {{ill|Hie Shrine (Takayama)|ja|日枝神社 (高山市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, is known as the {{ill|Takayama festival|ja|高山祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which is designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazai/17768/mukei-minzoku/takayamamaturi.html |title=高山祭の屋台行事 |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2016-12-11}}. The main deity is the Hachiman deity, {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and the enshrined deities include Atsuta Okami ({{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) and Kashi Okami ({{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). == History == It is said that in the 65th year of the reign of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a bandit with two faces named {{ill|Ryomen Sukuna|ja|両面宿儺|qq=draft|qq=draft}} appeared in Hida Province. He was defeated by {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|武振熊命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the ancestor of the Wani clan, who prayed for victory to the former Emperor Ōjin. Another theory is that it was established as a protective Hachiman shrine by {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in various provinces. In the [[Edo period]], the Kanamori clan, the local lords, protected the shrine as their {{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and made the residents north of the {{ill|Miyagawa (Gifu)|ja|宮川|qq=draft|qq=draft}} their parishioners. In the Meiji period, it was renamed Hachiman Shrine and became a Prefectural Shrine in 1932. The name Sakurayama Hachimangu has been used since after World War II. == Facilities == The {{ill|Takayama festival Yatai Kaikan|ja|高山祭屋台会館|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is located on the shrine grounds, where the festival floats used in the autumn Takayama festival are displayed throughout the year. File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref10s3s4020.jpg|Shrine gate File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref09n4110.jpg|Kotohira Shrine File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref07n4140.jpg|Inari Shrine File:Takayama_torii.jpg|Great Torii File:Sakurayama-hachiman_ninotorii.jpeg|Second Torii == Shrines within the Grounds == * Akiba Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Kagutsuchi|ja|火産霊神|1=Kagutsuchi|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Tenman Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Inari Shrine ** Deities: {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲霊神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Matsunoo Shrine|ja|松尾神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Kotohira Shrine ** Deities: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少名彦名神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Terumae Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|浪速根子武振熊命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == Cultural Properties == * Takayama festival Floats Event (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * Mikoshi and Mikoshi Storehouse of Hachiman Shrine (Municipal Tangible Folk Cultural Property){{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/kurashi/1000021/1000119/1000847/1000915/1004125.html|title=民俗文化財 |publisher=高山市 |accessdate=2016-12-11}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Sakurayama-hachiman-gū}} * [http://www.sakurayamahachiman.jimdo.com/ Sakurayama Hachimangu] {{shinto-stub}} {{Hachiman Faith}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Takayama, Gifu]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sakurayama Hachimangu | image = [[File:Sakurayama-hachimangu Takayama Gifu pref04s3s3990.jpg|250px]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E}}}} | location = [[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], [[Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], Sakuramachi 178 | coordinates = {{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E|scale:30000}} | deity = [[Emperor Ōjin]] | type = Former Prefectural Shrine, [[Beppyo Shrine|Beppyo Shrine]], Kanpei-sha | annual_festival = {{ill|October 9|ja|10月9日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|October 10|ja|10月10日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Takayama festival) | ceremonies = Shikinen Taisai }} '''Sakurayama Hachimangu''' (桜山八幡宮, officially '''櫻山八幡宮''') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], [[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], Japan. The autumn festival, along with the spring festival of {{ill|Hie Shrine (Takayama)|ja|日枝神社 (高山市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, is known as the {{ill|Takayama festival|ja|高山祭|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, which is designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazai/17768/mukei-minzoku/takayamamaturi.html |title=高山祭の屋台行事 |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2016-12-11}}. The main deity is the Hachiman deity, {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, and the enshrined deities include Atsuta Okami ({{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) and Kashi Okami ({{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|qq=draft|qq=draft}}). == History == It is said that in the 65th year of the reign of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, a bandit with two faces named {{ill|Ryomen Sukuna|ja|両面宿儺|qq=draft|qq=draft}} appeared in Hida Province. He was defeated by {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|武振熊命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the ancestor of the Wani clan, who prayed for victory to the former Emperor Ōjin. Another theory is that it was established as a protective Hachiman shrine by {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in various provinces. In the [[Edo period]], the Kanamori clan, the local lords, protected the shrine as their {{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and made the residents north of the {{ill|Miyagawa (Gifu)|ja|宮川|qq=draft|qq=draft}} their parishioners. In the Meiji period, it was renamed Hachiman Shrine and became a Prefectural Shrine in 1932. The name Sakurayama Hachimangu has been used since after World War II. == Facilities == The {{ill|Takayama festival Yatai Kaikan|ja|高山祭屋台会館|qq=draft|qq=draft}} is located on the shrine grounds, where the festival floats used in the autumn Takayama festival are displayed throughout the year. File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref10s3s4020.jpg|Shrine gate File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref09n4110.jpg|Kotohira Shrine File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref07n4140.jpg|Inari Shrine File:Takayama_torii.jpg|Great Torii File:Sakurayama-hachiman_ninotorii.jpeg|Second Torii == Shrines within the Grounds == * Akiba Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Kagutsuchi|ja|火産霊神|1=Kagutsuchi|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Tenman Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Inari Shrine ** Deities: {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲霊神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Matsunoo Shrine|ja|松尾神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Kotohira Shrine ** Deities: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少名彦名神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * Terumae Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|浪速根子武振熊命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == Cultural Properties == * Takayama festival Floats Event (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * Mikoshi and Mikoshi Storehouse of Hachiman Shrine (Municipal Tangible Folk Cultural Property){{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/kurashi/1000021/1000119/1000847/1000915/1004125.html|title=民俗文化財 |publisher=高山市 |accessdate=2016-12-11}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sakurayama Hachimangu | image = [[File:Sakurayama-hachimangu Takayama Gifu pref04s3s3990.jpg|250px]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E}}}} | location = [[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], [[Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], Sakuramachi 178 | coordinates = {{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E|scale:30000}} | deity = [[Emperor Ōjin]] | type = Former Prefectural Shrine, [[Beppyo Shrine|Beppyo Shrine]], Kanpei-sha | annual_festival = {{ill|October 9|ja|10月9日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/October 9|qq=}}, {{ill|October 10|ja|10月10日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/October 10|qq=}} (Takayama festival) | ceremonies = Shikinen Taisai }} '''Sakurayama Hachimangu''' (桜山八幡宮, officially '''櫻山八幡宮''') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], [[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], Japan. The autumn festival, along with the spring festival of {{ill|Hie Shrine (Takayama)|ja|日枝神社 (高山市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hie Shrine (Takayama)|qq=}}, is known as the {{ill|Takayama festival|ja|高山祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takayama festival|qq=}}, which is designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazai/17768/mukei-minzoku/takayamamaturi.html |title=高山祭の屋台行事 |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2016-12-11}}. The main deity is the Hachiman deity, {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=}}, and the enshrined deities include Atsuta Okami ({{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}}) and Kashi Okami ({{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Chūai|qq=}} and {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jingū|qq=}}). == History == It is said that in the 65th year of the reign of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Nintoku|qq=}}, a bandit with two faces named {{ill|Ryomen Sukuna|ja|両面宿儺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryomen Sukuna|qq=}} appeared in Hida Province. He was defeated by {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|武振熊命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takefurukuma no Mikoto|qq=}}, the ancestor of the Wani clan, who prayed for victory to the former Emperor Ōjin. Another theory is that it was established as a protective Hachiman shrine by {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Shōmu|qq=}} in various provinces. In the [[Edo period]], the Kanamori clan, the local lords, protected the shrine as their {{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujigami|qq=}} and made the residents north of the {{ill|Miyagawa (Gifu)|ja|宮川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyagawa (Gifu)|qq=}} their parishioners. In the Meiji period, it was renamed Hachiman Shrine and became a Prefectural Shrine in 1932. The name Sakurayama Hachimangu has been used since after World War II. == Facilities == The {{ill|Takayama festival Yatai Kaikan|ja|高山祭屋台会館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takayama festival Yatai Kaikan|qq=}} is located on the shrine grounds, where the festival floats used in the autumn Takayama festival are displayed throughout the year. File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref10s3s4020.jpg|Shrine gate File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref09n4110.jpg|Kotohira Shrine File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref07n4140.jpg|Inari Shrine File:Takayama_torii.jpg|Great Torii File:Sakurayama-hachiman_ninotorii.jpeg|Second Torii == Shrines within the Grounds == * Akiba Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Kagutsuchi|ja|火産霊神|1=Kagutsuchi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|qq=}} * Tenman Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=}} * Inari Shrine ** Deities: {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲霊神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=}}, {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko|qq=}}, {{ill|Matsunoo Shrine|ja|松尾神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsunoo Shrine|qq=}} * Kotohira Shrine ** Deities: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|qq=}}, {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Sutoku|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少名彦名神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=}} * Terumae Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|浪速根子武振熊命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takefurukuma no Mikoto|qq=}} == Cultural Properties == * Takayama festival Floats Event (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * Mikoshi and Mikoshi Storehouse of Hachiman Shrine (Municipal Tangible Folk Cultural Property){{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/kurashi/1000021/1000119/1000847/1000915/1004125.html|title=民俗文化財 |publisher=高山市 |accessdate=2016-12-11}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sakurayama Hachimangu | image = [[File:Sakurayama-hachimangu Takayama Gifu pref04s3s3990.jpg|250px]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E}}}} | location = [[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], [[Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], Sakuramachi 178 | coordinates = {{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E|scale:30000}} | deity = [[Emperor Ōjin]] | type = Former Prefectural Shrine, [[Beppyo Shrine|Beppyo Shrine]], Kanpei-sha | annual_festival = {{ill|October 9|ja|10月9日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/October 9|qq=}}, {{ill|October 10|ja|10月10日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/October 10|qq=}} (Takayama festival) | ceremonies = Shikinen Taisai }} '''Sakurayama Hachimangu''' (桜山八幡宮, officially '''櫻山八幡宮''') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], [[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], Japan. The autumn festival, along with the spring festival of {{ill|Hie Shrine (Takayama)|ja|日枝神社 (高山市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hie Shrine (Takayama)|qq=}}, is known as the {{ill|Takayama festival|ja|高山祭|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takayama festival|qq=}}, which is designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazai/17768/mukei-minzoku/takayamamaturi.html |title=高山祭の屋台行事 |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2016-12-11}}. The main deity is the Hachiman deity, {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|ja|応神天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Ōjin|qq=}}, and the enshrined deities include Atsuta Okami ({{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}}) and Kashi Okami ({{ill|Emperor Chūai|ja|仲哀天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Chūai|qq=}} and {{ill|Empress Jingū|ja|神功皇后|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Jingū|qq=}}). == History == It is said that in the 65th year of the reign of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|ja|仁徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Nintoku|qq=}}, a bandit with two faces named {{ill|Ryomen Sukuna|ja|両面宿儺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryomen Sukuna|qq=}} appeared in Hida Province. He was defeated by {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|武振熊命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takefurukuma no Mikoto|qq=}}, the ancestor of the Wani clan, who prayed for victory to the former Emperor Ōjin. Another theory is that it was established as a protective Hachiman shrine by {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|ja|聖武天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Shōmu|qq=}} in various provinces. In the [[Edo period]], the Kanamori clan, the local lords, protected the shrine as their {{ill|Ujigami|ja|氏神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujigami|qq=}} and made the residents north of the {{ill|Miyagawa (Gifu)|ja|宮川|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyagawa (Gifu)|qq=}} their parishioners. In the Meiji period, it was renamed Hachiman Shrine and became a Prefectural Shrine in 1932. The name Sakurayama Hachimangu has been used since after World War II. == Facilities == The {{ill|Takayama festival Yatai Kaikan|ja|高山祭屋台会館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takayama festival Yatai Kaikan|qq=}} is located on the shrine grounds, where the festival floats used in the autumn Takayama festival are displayed throughout the year. File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref10s3s4020.jpg|Shrine gate File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref09n4110.jpg|Kotohira Shrine File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref07n4140.jpg|Inari Shrine File:Takayama_torii.jpg|Great Torii File:Sakurayama-hachiman_ninotorii.jpeg|Second Torii == Shrines within the Grounds == * Akiba Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|ja|火産霊神|1=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|qq=}} * Tenman Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=}} * Inari Shrine ** Deities: {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲霊神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=}}, {{ill|Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko|qq=}}, {{ill|Matsunoo Shrine|ja|松尾神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsunoo Shrine|qq=}} * Kotohira Shrine ** Deities: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|qq=}}, {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|ja|崇徳天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Sutoku|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少名彦名神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=}} * Terumae Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|浪速根子武振熊命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takefurukuma no Mikoto|qq=}} == Cultural Properties == * Takayama festival Floats Event (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * Mikoshi and Mikoshi Storehouse of Hachiman Shrine (Municipal Tangible Folk Cultural Property){{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/kurashi/1000021/1000119/1000847/1000915/1004125.html|title=民俗文化財 |publisher=高山市 |accessdate=2016-12-11}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sakurayama Hachimangu | image = [[File:Sakurayama-hachimangu Takayama Gifu pref04s3s3990.jpg|250px]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E}}}} | location = [[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], [[Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], Sakuramachi 178 | coordinates = {{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E|scale:30000}} | deity = [[Emperor Ōjin]] | type = Former Prefectural Shrine, [[Beppyo Shrine|Beppyo Shrine]], Kanpei-sha | annual_festival = [[:en:October 9|October 9]], [[:en:October 10|October 10]] (Takayama festival) | ceremonies = Shikinen Taisai }} '''Sakurayama Hachimangu''' (桜山八幡宮, officially '''櫻山八幡宮''') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], [[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], Japan. The autumn festival, along with the spring festival of {{ill|Hie Shrine (Takayama)|ja|日枝神社 (高山市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hie Shrine (Takayama)|qq=}}, is known as the [[:en:Takayama Festival|Takayama festival]], which is designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazai/17768/mukei-minzoku/takayamamaturi.html |title=高山祭の屋台行事 |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2016-12-11}}. The main deity is the Hachiman deity, [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], and the enshrined deities include Atsuta Okami ({{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}}) and Kashi Okami ([[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]] and [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]]). == History == It is said that in the 65th year of the reign of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]], a bandit with two faces named {{ill|Ryomen Sukuna|ja|両面宿儺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryomen Sukuna|qq=}} appeared in Hida Province. He was defeated by {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|武振熊命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takefurukuma no Mikoto|qq=}}, the ancestor of the Wani clan, who prayed for victory to the former Emperor Ōjin. Another theory is that it was established as a protective Hachiman shrine by [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Emperor Shōmu]] in various provinces. In the [[Edo period]], the Kanamori clan, the local lords, protected the shrine as their [[:en:Ujigami|Ujigami]] and made the residents north of the [[:en:Miyagawa|Miyagawa (Gifu)]] their parishioners. In the Meiji period, it was renamed Hachiman Shrine and became a Prefectural Shrine in 1932. The name Sakurayama Hachimangu has been used since after World War II. == Facilities == The {{ill|Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall|lt=Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall|lt=Takayama festival Yatai Kaikan|ja|高山祭屋台会館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takayama festival Yatai Kaikan|qq=}} is located on the shrine grounds, where the festival floats used in the autumn Takayama festival are displayed throughout the year. File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref10s3s4020.jpg|Shrine gate File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref09n4110.jpg|Kotohira Shrine File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref07n4140.jpg|Inari Shrine File:Takayama_torii.jpg|Great Torii File:Sakurayama-hachiman_ninotorii.jpeg|Second Torii == Shrines within the Grounds == * Akiba Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|ja|火産霊神|1=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|qq=}} * Tenman Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=}} * Inari Shrine ** Deities: {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲霊神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=}}, {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko|qq=}}, {{ill|Matsunoo Shrine|ja|松尾神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsunoo Shrine|qq=}} * Kotohira Shrine ** Deities: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|qq=}}, [[:en:Emperor Sutoku|Emperor Sutoku]], {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少名彦名神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=}} * Terumae Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|浪速根子武振熊命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takefurukuma no Mikoto|qq=}} == Cultural Properties == * Takayama festival Floats Event (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * Mikoshi and Mikoshi Storehouse of Hachiman Shrine (Municipal Tangible Folk Cultural Property){{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/kurashi/1000021/1000119/1000847/1000915/1004125.html|title=民俗文化財 |publisher=高山市 |accessdate=2016-12-11}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Sakurayama-hachiman-gū}} * [http://www.sakurayamahachiman.jimdo.com/ Sakurayama Hachimangu] {{shinto-stub}} {{Hachiman Faith}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|桜山八幡宮|version=102411526|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Takayama, Gifu]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Takayama, Gifu]] [[Category:Shinto]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Sakurayama Hachimangu]] [[en:Sakurayama Hachimangu]] [[ja:桜山八幡宮]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sakurayama Hachimangu | image = [[File:Sakurayama-hachimangu Takayama Gifu pref04s3s3990.jpg|250px]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E}}}} | location = [[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], [[Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], Sakuramachi 178 | coordinates = {{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E|scale:30000}} | deity = [[Emperor Ōjin]] | type = Former Prefectural Shrine, [[Beppyo Shrine|Beppyo Shrine]], Kanpei-sha | annual_festival = [[:en:October 9|October 9]], [[:en:October 10|October 10]] (Takayama festival) | ceremonies = Shikinen Taisai }} '''Sakurayama Hachimangu''' (桜山八幡宮, officially '''櫻山八幡宮''') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], [[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], Japan. The autumn festival, along with the spring festival of {{ill|Hie Shrine (Takayama)|ja|日枝神社 (高山市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hie Shrine (Takayama)|qq=}}, is known as the [[:en:Takayama Festival|Takayama festival]], which is designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazai/17768/mukei-minzoku/takayamamaturi.html |title=高山祭の屋台行事 |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2016-12-11}}. The main deity is the Hachiman deity, [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], and the enshrined deities include Atsuta Okami ({{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=}}) and Kashi Okami ([[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]] and [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]]). == History == It is said that in the 65th year of the reign of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]], a bandit with two faces named {{ill|Ryomen Sukuna|ja|両面宿儺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryomen Sukuna|qq=}} appeared in Hida Province. He was defeated by {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|武振熊命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takefurukuma no Mikoto|qq=}}, the ancestor of the Wani clan, who prayed for victory to the former Emperor Ōjin. Another theory is that it was established as a protective Hachiman shrine by [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Emperor Shōmu]] in various provinces. In the [[Edo period]], the Kanamori clan, the local lords, protected the shrine as their [[:en:Ujigami|Ujigami]] and made the residents north of the [[:en:Miyagawa|Miyagawa (Gifu)]] their parishioners. In the Meiji period, it was renamed Hachiman Shrine and became a Prefectural Shrine in 1932. The name Sakurayama Hachimangu has been used since after World War II. == Facilities == The {{ill|Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall|lt=Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall|lt=Takayama festival Yatai Kaikan|ja|高山祭屋台会館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takayama festival Yatai Kaikan|qq=}} is located on the shrine grounds, where the festival floats used in the autumn Takayama festival are displayed throughout the year. File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref10s3s4020.jpg|Shrine gate File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref09n4110.jpg|Kotohira Shrine File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref07n4140.jpg|Inari Shrine File:Takayama_torii.jpg|Great Torii File:Sakurayama-hachiman_ninotorii.jpeg|Second Torii == Shrines within the Grounds == * Akiba Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|ja|火産霊神|1=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|qq=}} * Tenman Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=}} * Inari Shrine ** Deities: {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲霊神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=}}, {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko|qq=}}, {{ill|Matsunoo Shrine|ja|松尾神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsunoo Shrine|qq=}} * Kotohira Shrine ** Deities: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|qq=}}, [[:en:Emperor Sutoku|Emperor Sutoku]], {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少名彦名神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=}} * Terumae Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|浪速根子武振熊命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takefurukuma no Mikoto|qq=}} == Cultural Properties == * Takayama festival Floats Event (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * Mikoshi and Mikoshi Storehouse of Hachiman Shrine (Municipal Tangible Folk Cultural Property){{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/kurashi/1000021/1000119/1000847/1000915/1004125.html|title=民俗文化財 |publisher=高山市 |accessdate=2016-12-11}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sakurayama Hachimangu | image = [[File:Sakurayama-hachimangu Takayama Gifu pref04s3s3990.jpg|250px]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E}}}} | location = [[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], [[Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], Sakuramachi 178 | coordinates = {{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E|scale:30000}} | deity = [[Emperor Ōjin]] | type = Former Prefectural Shrine, [[Beppyo Shrine|Beppyo Shrine]], Kanpei-sha | annual_festival = [[:en:October 9|October 9]], [[:en:October 10|October 10]] (Takayama festival) | ceremonies = Shikinen Taisai }} '''Sakurayama Hachimangu''' (桜山八幡宮, officially '''櫻山八幡宮''') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], [[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], Japan. The autumn festival, along with the spring festival of {{ill|Hie Shrine (Takayama)|ja|日枝神社 (高山市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hie Shrine (Takayama)|qq=}}, is known as the [[:en:Takayama Festival|Takayama festival]], which is designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazai/17768/mukei-minzoku/takayamamaturi.html |title=高山祭の屋台行事 |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2016-12-11}}. The main deity is the Hachiman deity, [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], and the enshrined deities include Atsuta Okami ({{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル}}) and Kashi Okami ([[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]] and [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]]). == History == It is said that in the 65th year of the reign of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]], a bandit with two faces named {{ill|Ryomen Sukuna|ja|両面宿儺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryomen Sukuna|qq=}} appeared in Hida Province. He was defeated by {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|武振熊命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takefurukuma no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武振熊}}, the ancestor of the Wani clan, who prayed for victory to the former Emperor Ōjin. Another theory is that it was established as a protective Hachiman shrine by [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Emperor Shōmu]] in various provinces. In the [[Edo period]], the Kanamori clan, the local lords, protected the shrine as their [[:en:Ujigami|Ujigami]] and made the residents north of the [[:en:Miyagawa|Miyagawa (Gifu)]] their parishioners. In the Meiji period, it was renamed Hachiman Shrine and became a Prefectural Shrine in 1932. The name Sakurayama Hachimangu has been used since after World War II. == Facilities == The {{ill|Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall|lt=Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall|lt=Takayama festival Yatai Kaikan|ja|高山祭屋台会館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takayama festival Yatai Kaikan|qq=}} is located on the shrine grounds, where the festival floats used in the autumn Takayama festival are displayed throughout the year. File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref10s3s4020.jpg|Shrine gate File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref09n4110.jpg|Kotohira Shrine File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref07n4140.jpg|Inari Shrine File:Takayama_torii.jpg|Great Torii File:Sakurayama-hachiman_ninotorii.jpeg|Second Torii == Shrines within the Grounds == * Akiba Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|ja|火産霊神|1=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ}} * Tenman Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Inari Shrine ** Deities: {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲霊神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}}, {{ill|Matsunoo Shrine|ja|松尾神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsunoo Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 松尾大社}} * Kotohira Shrine ** Deities: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}, [[:en:Emperor Sutoku|Emperor Sutoku]], {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少名彦名神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Terumae Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|浪速根子武振熊命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takefurukuma no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Cultural Properties == * Takayama festival Floats Event (Important Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E}}}} | location = [[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], [[Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], Sakuramachi 178 | coordinates = {{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E|scale:30000}} | deity = [[Emperor Ōjin]] | type = Former Prefectural Shrine, [[Beppyo Shrine|Beppyo Shrine]], Kanpei-sha | annual_festival = [[:en:October 9|October 9]], [[:en:October 10|October 10]] (Takayama festival) | ceremonies = Shikinen Taisai }} '''Sakurayama Hachimangu''' (桜山八幡宮, officially '''櫻山八幡宮''') is a [[Shinto shrine]] located in [[Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], [[Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], Japan. The autumn festival, along with the spring festival of {{ill|Hie Shrine (Takayama)|ja|日枝神社 (高山市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hie Shrine (Takayama)|qq=}}, is known as the [[:en:Takayama Festival|Takayama festival]], which is designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazai/17768/mukei-minzoku/takayamamaturi.html |title=高山祭の屋台行事 |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2016-12-11}}. The main deity is the Hachiman deity, [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], and the enshrined deities include Atsuta Okami ({{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル}}) and Kashi Okami ([[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]] and [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]]). == History == It is said that in the 65th year of the reign of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]], a bandit with two faces named {{ill|Ryomen Sukuna|ja|両面宿儺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryomen Sukuna|qq=}} appeared in Hida Province. He was defeated by {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|武振熊命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takefurukuma no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武振熊}}, the ancestor of the Wani clan, who prayed for victory to the former Emperor Ōjin. Another theory is that it was established as a protective Hachiman shrine by [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Emperor Shōmu]] in various provinces. In the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the Kanamori clan, the local lords, protected the shrine as their [[:en:Ujigami|Ujigami]] and made the residents north of the [[:en:Miyagawa|Miyagawa (Gifu)]] their parishioners. In the Meiji period, it was renamed Hachiman Shrine and became a Prefectural Shrine in 1932. The name Sakurayama Hachimangu has been used since after World War II. == Facilities == The {{ill|Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall|lt=Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall|lt=Takayama festival Yatai Kaikan|ja|高山祭屋台会館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takayama festival Yatai Kaikan|qq=}} is located on the shrine grounds, where the festival floats used in the autumn Takayama festival are displayed throughout the year. File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref10s3s4020.jpg|Shrine gate File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref09n4110.jpg|Kotohira Shrine File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref07n4140.jpg|Inari Shrine File:Takayama_torii.jpg|Great Torii File:Sakurayama-hachiman_ninotorii.jpeg|Second Torii == Shrines within the Grounds == * Akiba Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|ja|火産霊神|1=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ}} * Tenman Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Inari Shrine ** Deities: {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲霊神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}}, {{ill|Matsunoo Shrine|ja|松尾神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsunoo Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 松尾大社}} * Kotohira Shrine ** Deities: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}, [[:en:Emperor Sutoku|Emperor Sutoku]], {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少名彦名神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Terumae Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|浪速根子武振熊命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takefurukuma no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Cultural Properties == * Takayama festival Floats Event (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * Mikoshi and Mikoshi Storehouse of Hachiman Shrine (Municipal Tangible Folk Cultural Property){{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/kurashi/1000021/1000119/1000847/1000915/1004125.html|title=民俗文化財 |publisher=高山市 |accessdate=2016-12-11}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == The main deity is the Hachiman deity, [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], and the enshrined deities include Atsuta Okami ({{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル}}) and Kashi Okami ([[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]] and [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]]). == History == It is said that in the 65th year of the reign of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]], a bandit with two faces named {{ill|Ryomen Sukuna|ja|両面宿儺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryomen Sukuna|qq=}} appeared in Hida Province. He was defeated by {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|武振熊命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takefurukuma no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武振熊}}, the ancestor of the Wani clan, who prayed for victory to the former Emperor Ōjin. Another theory is that it was established as a protective Hachiman shrine by [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Emperor Shōmu]] in various provinces. In the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the Kanamori clan, the local lords, protected the shrine as their [[:en:Ujigami|Ujigami]] and made the residents north of the [[:en:Miyagawa|Miyagawa (Gifu)]] their parishioners. In the Meiji period, it was renamed Hachiman Shrine and became a Prefectural Shrine in 1932. The name Sakurayama Hachimangu has been used since after World War II. == Facilities == The {{ill|Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall|lt=Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall|lt=Takayama festival Yatai Kaikan|ja|高山祭屋台会館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takayama festival Yatai Kaikan|qq=}} is located on the shrine grounds, where the festival floats used in the autumn Takayama festival are displayed throughout the year. File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref10s3s4020.jpg|Shrine gate File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref09n4110.jpg|Kotohira Shrine File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref07n4140.jpg|Inari Shrine File:Takayama_torii.jpg|Great Torii File:Sakurayama-hachiman_ninotorii.jpeg|Second Torii == Shrines within the Grounds == * Akiba Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|ja|火産霊神|1=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ}} * Tenman Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Inari Shrine ** Deities: {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲霊神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}}, {{ill|Matsunoo Shrine|ja|松尾神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsunoo Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 松尾大社}} * Kotohira Shrine ** Deities: {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少名彦名神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Terumae Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|浪速根子武振熊命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takefurukuma no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Cultural Properties == * Takayama festival Floats Event (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * Mikoshi and Mikoshi Storehouse of Hachiman Shrine (Municipal Tangible Folk Cultural Property){{Cite web|和書|date= Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E}}}} | location = [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], [[:en:Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], Sakuramachi 178 | coordinates = {{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E|scale:30000}} | deity = [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] | type = Former Prefectural Shrine, [[Beppyo Shrine|Beppyo Shrine]], Kanpei-sha | annual_festival = [[:en:October 9|October 9]], [[:en:October 10|October 10]] (Takayama festival) | ceremonies = Shikinen Taisai }} '''Sakurayama Hachimangu''' (桜山八幡宮, officially '''櫻山八幡宮''') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], Japan. The autumn festival, along with the spring festival of {{ill|Hie Shrine (Takayama)|ja|日枝神社 (高山市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hie Shrine (Takayama)|qq=}}, is known as the [[:en:Takayama Festival|Takayama festival]], which is designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazai/17768/mukei-minzoku/takayamamaturi.html |title=高山祭の屋台行事 |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2016-12-11}}. The main deity is the Hachiman deity, [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], and the enshrined deities include Atsuta Okami ({{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル}}) and Kashi Okami ([[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]] and [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]]). == History == It is said that in the 65th year of the reign of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]], a bandit with two faces named {{ill|Ryomen Sukuna|ja|両面宿儺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryomen Sukuna|qq=}} appeared in Hida Province. He was defeated by {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|武振熊命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takefurukuma no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武振熊}}, the ancestor of the Wani clan, who prayed for victory to the former Emperor Ōjin. Another theory is that it was established as a protective Hachiman shrine by [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Emperor Shōmu]] in various provinces. In the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the Kanamori clan, the local lords, protected the shrine as their [[:en:Ujigami|Ujigami]] and made the residents north of the [[:en:Miyagawa|Miyagawa (Gifu)]] their parishioners. In the Meiji period, it was renamed Hachiman Shrine and became a Prefectural Shrine in 1932. The name Sakurayama Hachimangu has been used since after World War II. == Facilities == The {{ill|Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall|lt=Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall|lt=Takayama festival Yatai Kaikan|ja|高山祭屋台会館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takayama festival Yatai Kaikan|qq=}} is located on the shrine grounds, where the festival floats used in the autumn Takayama festival are displayed throughout the year. File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref10s3s4020.jpg|Shrine gate File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref09n4110.jpg|Kotohira Shrine File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref07n4140.jpg|Inari Shrine File:Takayama_torii.jpg|Great Torii File:Sakurayama-hachiman_ninotorii.jpeg|Second Torii == Shrines within the Grounds == * Akiba Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|ja|火産霊神|1=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ}} * Tenman Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Inari Shrine ** Deities: {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲霊神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}}, {{ill|Matsunoo Shrine|ja|松尾神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsunoo Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 松尾大社}} * Kotohira Shrine ** Deities: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}, [[:en:Emperor Sutoku|Emperor Sutoku]], {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少名彦名神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Terumae Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|浪速根子武振熊命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takefurukuma no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Cultural Properties == * Takayama festival Floats Event (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * Mikoshi and Mikoshi Storehouse of Hachiman Shrine (Municipal Tangible Folk Cultural Property){{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/kurashi/1000021/1000119/1000847/1000915/1004125.html|title=民俗文化財 |publisher=高山市 |accessdate=2016-12-11}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Shinto]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[sn:Sakurayama Hachimangu]] [[ja:桜山八幡宮]] [[Category:Municipality Designated Tangible Folk Cultural Properties]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11537980]] Wikidata name: Sakurayama Hachimangū [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:04, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sakurayama Hachimangu | image = [[File:Sakurayama-hachimangu Takayama Gifu pref04s3s3990.jpg|250px]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E}}}} | location = [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], [[:en:Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], Sakuramachi 178 | coordinates = {{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E|scale:30000}} | deity = [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] | type = Former Prefectural Shrine, [[Beppyo Shrine|Beppyo Shrine]], Kanpei-sha | annual_festival = [[:en:October 9|October 9]], [[:en:October 10|October 10]] (Takayama festival) | ceremonies = Shikinen Taisai }} '''Sakurayama Hachimangu''' (桜山八幡宮, officially '''櫻山八幡宮''') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], Japan. The autumn festival, along with the spring festival of {{ill|Hie Shrine (Takayama)|ja|日枝神社 (高山市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hie Shrine (Takayama)|qq=|1=Hie Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11509534}}, is known as the [[:en:Takayama Festival|Takayama festival]], which is designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazai/17768/mukei-minzoku/takayamamaturi.html |title=高山祭の屋台行事 |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2016-12-11}}. The main deity is the Hachiman deity, [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], and the enshrined deities include Atsuta Okami ({{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|1=Yamato Takeru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}}) and Kashi Okami ([[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]] and [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]]). == History == It is said that in the 65th year of the reign of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]], a bandit with two faces named {{ill|Ryomen Sukuna|ja|両面宿儺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryomen Sukuna|qq=|1=Ryōmen Sukuna|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11362694}} appeared in Hida Province. He was defeated by {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|武振熊命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takefurukuma no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武振熊|1=Takefurukuma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546000}}, the ancestor of the Wani clan, who prayed for victory to the former Emperor Ōjin. Another theory is that it was established as a protective Hachiman shrine by [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Emperor Shōmu]] in various provinces. In the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the Kanamori clan, the local lords, protected the shrine as their [[:en:Ujigami|Ujigami]] and made the residents north of the [[:en:Miyagawa|Miyagawa (Gifu)]] their parishioners. In the Meiji period, it was renamed Hachiman Shrine and became a Prefectural Shrine in 1932. The name Sakurayama Hachimangu has been used since after World War II. == Facilities == The {{ill|Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall|lt=Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall|lt=Takayama festival Yatai Kaikan|ja|高山祭屋台会館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takayama festival Yatai Kaikan|qq=}} is located on the shrine grounds, where the festival floats used in the autumn Takayama festival are displayed throughout the year. File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref10s3s4020.jpg|Shrine gate File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref09n4110.jpg|Kotohira Shrine File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref07n4140.jpg|Inari Shrine File:Takayama_torii.jpg|Great Torii File:Sakurayama-hachiman_ninotorii.jpeg|Second Torii == Shrines within the Grounds == * Akiba Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|ja|火産霊神|1=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ}} * Tenman Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Inari Shrine ** Deities: {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲霊神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}}, {{ill|Matsunoo Shrine|ja|松尾神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsunoo Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 松尾大社|1=Matsunoo Taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q710302}} * Kotohira Shrine ** Deities: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}, [[:en:Emperor Sutoku|Emperor Sutoku]], {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少名彦名神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Terumae Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|浪速根子武振熊命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takefurukuma no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Cultural Properties == * Takayama festival Floats Event (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * Mikoshi and Mikoshi Storehouse of Hachiman Shrine (Municipal Tangible Folk Cultural Property){{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/kurashi/1000021/1000119/1000847/1000915/1004125.html|title=民俗文化財 |publisher=高山市 |accessdate=2016-12-11}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == page|ja|桜山八幡宮|version=102411526|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]]
