revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11537980]] Wikidata name: Sakurayama Hachimangū [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:04, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sakurayama Hachimangu | image = [[File:Sakurayama-hachimangu Takayama Gifu pref04s3s3990.jpg|250px]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E}}}} | location = [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], [[:en:Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], Sakuramachi 178 | coordinates = {{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E|scale:30000}} | deity = [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] | type = Former Prefectural Shrine, [[Beppyo Shrine|Beppyo Shrine]], Kanpei-sha | annual_festival = [[:en:October 9|October 9]], [[:en:October 10|October 10]] (Takayama festival) | ceremonies = Shikinen Taisai }} '''Sakurayama Hachimangu''' (桜山八幡宮, officially '''櫻山八幡宮''') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], Japan. The autumn festival, along with the spring festival of {{ill|Hie Shrine (Takayama)|ja|日枝神社 (高山市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hie Shrine (Takayama)|qq=|1=Hie Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11509534}}, is known as the [[:en:Takayama Festival|Takayama festival]], which is designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazai/17768/mukei-minzoku/takayamamaturi.html |title=高山祭の屋台行事 |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2016-12-11}}. The main deity is the Hachiman deity, [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], and the enshrined deities include Atsuta Okami ({{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|1=Yamato Takeru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}}) and Kashi Okami ([[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]] and [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]]). == History == It is said that in the 65th year of the reign of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]], a bandit with two faces named {{ill|Ryomen Sukuna|ja|両面宿儺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryomen Sukuna|qq=|1=Ryōmen Sukuna|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11362694}} appeared in Hida Province. He was defeated by {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|武振熊命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takefurukuma no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武振熊|1=Takefurukuma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546000}}, the ancestor of the Wani clan, who prayed for victory to the former Emperor Ōjin. Another theory is that it was established as a protective Hachiman shrine by [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Emperor Shōmu]] in various provinces. In the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the Kanamori clan, the local lords, protected the shrine as their [[:en:Ujigami|Ujigami]] and made the residents north of the [[:en:Miyagawa|Miyagawa (Gifu)]] their parishioners. In the Meiji period, it was renamed Hachiman Shrine and became a Prefectural Shrine in 1932. The name Sakurayama Hachimangu has been used since after World War II. == Facilities == The {{ill|Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall|lt=Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall|lt=Takayama festival Yatai Kaikan|ja|高山祭屋台会館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takayama festival Yatai Kaikan|qq=}} is located on the shrine grounds, where the festival floats used in the autumn Takayama festival are displayed throughout the year. File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref10s3s4020.jpg|Shrine gate File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref09n4110.jpg|Kotohira Shrine File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref07n4140.jpg|Inari Shrine File:Takayama_torii.jpg|Great Torii File:Sakurayama-hachiman_ninotorii.jpeg|Second Torii == Shrines within the Grounds == * Akiba Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|ja|火産霊神|1=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ}} * Tenman Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} * Inari Shrine ** Deities: {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲霊神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}}, {{ill|Matsunoo Shrine|ja|松尾神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsunoo Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 松尾大社|1=Matsunoo Taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q710302}} * Kotohira Shrine ** Deities: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}, [[:en:Emperor Sutoku|Emperor Sutoku]], {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少名彦名神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Terumae Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|浪速根子武振熊命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takefurukuma no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Cultural Properties == * Takayama festival Floats Event (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * Mikoshi and Mikoshi Storehouse of Hachiman Shrine (Municipal Tangible Folk Cultural Property){{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/kurashi/1000021/1000119/1000847/1000915/1004125.html|title=民俗文化財 |publisher=高山市 |accessdate=2016-12-11}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Sakurayama-hachiman-gū}} * [http://www.sakurayamahachiman.jimdo.com/ Sakurayama Hachimangu] {{shinto-stub}} {{Hachiman Faith}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|桜山八幡宮|version=102411526|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Shinto]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] }} [[ja:桜山八幡宮]] [[Category:Municipality Designated Tangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Takayama, Gifu]] {{Commons category|Sakurayama-hachiman-gū}} {{wikidata link|Q11537980}}

revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11537980]] Wikidata name: Sakurayama Hachimangū [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:04, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sakurayama Hachimangu | image = [[File:Sakurayama-hachimangu Takayama Gifu pref04s3s3990.jpg|250px]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E}}}} | location = [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], [[:en:Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], Sakuramachi 178 | coordinates = {{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E|scale:30000}} | deity = [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] | type = Former Prefectural Shrine, [[Beppyo Shrine|Beppyo Shrine]], Kanpei-sha | annual_festival = [[:en:October 9|October 9]], [[:en:October 10|October 10]] (Takayama festival) | ceremonies = Shikinen Taisai }} '''Sakurayama Hachimangu''' (桜山八幡宮, officially '''櫻山八幡宮''') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], Japan. The autumn festival, along with the spring festival of {{ill|Hie Shrine (Takayama)|ja|日枝神社 (高山市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hie Shrine (Takayama)|qq=|1=Hie Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11509534}}, is known as the [[:en:Takayama Festival|Takayama festival]], which is designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazai/17768/mukei-minzoku/takayamamaturi.html |title=高山祭の屋台行事 |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2016-12-11}}. The main deity is the Hachiman deity, [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], and the enshrined deities include Atsuta Okami ({{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|1=Yamato Takeru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}}) and Kashi Okami ([[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]] and [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]]). == History == It is said that in the 65th year of the reign of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]], a bandit with two faces named {{ill|Ryomen Sukuna|ja|両面宿儺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryomen Sukuna|qq=|1=Ryōmen Sukuna|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11362694}} appeared in Hida Province. He was defeated by {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|武振熊命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takefurukuma no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武振熊|1=Takefurukuma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546000}}, the ancestor of the Wani clan, who prayed for victory to the former Emperor Ōjin. Another theory is that it was established as a protective Hachiman shrine by [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Emperor Shōmu]] in various provinces. In the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the Kanamori clan, the local lords, protected the shrine as their [[:en:Ujigami|Ujigami]] and made the residents north of the [[:en:Miyagawa|Miyagawa (Gifu)]] their parishioners. In the Meiji period, it was renamed Hachiman Shrine and became a Prefectural Shrine in 1932. The name Sakurayama Hachimangu has been used since after World War II. == Facilities == The {{ill|Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall|lt=Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall|lt=Takayama festival Yatai Kaikan|ja|高山祭屋台会館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takayama festival Yatai Kaikan|qq=}} is located on the shrine grounds, where the festival floats used in the autumn Takayama festival are displayed throughout the year. File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref10s3s4020.jpg|Shrine gate File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref09n4110.jpg|Kotohira Shrine File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref07n4140.jpg|Inari Shrine File:Takayama_torii.jpg|Great Torii File:Sakurayama-hachiman_ninotorii.jpeg|Second Torii == Shrines within the Grounds == * Akiba Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|ja|火産霊神|1=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ}} * Tenman Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} * Inari Shrine ** Deities: {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲霊神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}}, {{ill|Matsunoo Shrine|ja|松尾神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsunoo Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 松尾大社|1=Matsunoo Taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q710302}} * Kotohira Shrine ** Deities: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}, [[:en:Emperor Sutoku|Emperor Sutoku]], {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少名彦名神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Terumae Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|浪速根子武振熊命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takefurukuma no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Cultural Properties == * Takayama festival Floats Event (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * Mikoshi and Mikoshi Storehouse of Hachiman Shrine (Municipal Tangible Folk Cultural Property){{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/kurashi/1000021/1000119/1000847/1000915/1004125.html|title=民俗文化財 |publisher=高山市 |accessdate=2016-12-11}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == [[sn:commons:Category:Sakurayama-hachiman-gū]][[ja:桜山八幡宮]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11537980]] Wikidata name: Sakurayama Hachimangū [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:04, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sakurayama Hachimangu | image = [[File:Sakurayama-hachimangu Takayama Gifu pref04s3s3990.jpg|250px]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E}}}} | location = [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], [[:en:Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], Sakuramachi 178 | coordinates = {{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E|scale:30000}} | deity = [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] | type = Former Prefectural Shrine, [[Beppyo Shrine|Beppyo Shrine]], Kanpei-sha | annual_festival = [[:en:October 9|October 9]], [[:en:October 10|October 10]] (Takayama festival) | ceremonies = Shikinen Taisai }} '''Sakurayama Hachimangu''' (桜山八幡宮, officially '''櫻山八幡宮''') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], Japan. The autumn festival, along with the spring festival of {{ill|Hie Shrine (Takayama)|ja|日枝神社 (高山市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hie Shrine (Takayama)|qq=|1=Hie Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11509534}}, is known as the [[:en:Takayama Festival|Takayama festival]], which is designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazai/17768/mukei-minzoku/takayamamaturi.html |title=高山祭の屋台行事 |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2016-12-11}}. The main deity is the Hachiman deity, [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], and the enshrined deities include Atsuta Okami ({{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|1=Yamato Takeru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}}) and Kashi Okami ([[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]] and [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]]). == History == It is said that in the 65th year of the reign of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]], a bandit with two faces named {{ill|Ryomen Sukuna|ja|両面宿儺|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryomen Sukuna|qq=|1=Ryōmen Sukuna|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11362694}} appeared in Hida Province. He was defeated by {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|武振熊命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takefurukuma no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武振熊|1=Takefurukuma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546000}}, the ancestor of the Wani clan, who prayed for victory to the former Emperor Ōjin. Another theory is that it was established as a protective Hachiman shrine by [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Emperor Shōmu]] in various provinces. In the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the Kanamori clan, the local lords, protected the shrine as their [[:en:Ujigami|Ujigami]] and made the residents north of the [[:en:Miyagawa|Miyagawa (Gifu)]] their parishioners. In the Meiji period, it was renamed Hachiman Shrine and became a Prefectural Shrine in 1932. The name Sakurayama Hachimangu has been used since after World War II. == Facilities == The {{ill|Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall|lt=Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall|lt=Takayama festival Yatai Kaikan|ja|高山祭屋台会館|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takayama festival Yatai Kaikan|qq=}} is located on the shrine grounds, where the festival floats used in the autumn Takayama festival are displayed throughout the year. [[sn:commons:Category:Sakurayama-hachiman-gū]][[ja:桜山八幡宮]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11537980]] Wikidata name: Sakurayama Hachimangū [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:04, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sakurayama Hachimangu | image = [[File:Sakurayama-hachimangu Takayama Gifu pref04s3s3990.jpg|250px]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E}}}} | location = [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], [[:en:Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], Sakuramachi 178 | coordinates = {{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E|scale:30000}} | deity = [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] | type = Former Prefectural Shrine, [[Beppyo Shrine|Beppyo Shrine]], Kanpei-sha | annual_festival = [[:en:October 9|October 9]], [[:en:October 10|October 10]] (Takayama festival) | ceremonies = Shikinen Taisai }} '''Sakurayama Hachimangu''' (桜山八幡宮, officially '''櫻山八幡宮''') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], Japan. The autumn festival, along with the spring festival of {{ill|Hie Shrine (Takayama)|ja|日枝神社 (高山市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hie Shrine (Takayama)|1=Hie Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11509534}}, is known as the [[:en:Takayama Festival|Takayama festival]], which is designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazai/17768/mukei-minzoku/takayamamaturi.html |title=高山祭の屋台行事 |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2016-12-11}}. The main deity is the Hachiman deity, [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], and the enshrined deities include Atsuta Okami ({{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|1=Yamato Takeru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}}) and Kashi Okami ([[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]] and [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]]). == History == It is said that in the 65th year of the reign of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]], a bandit with two faces named {{ill|Ryomen Sukuna|ja|両面宿儺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryomen Sukuna|1=Ryōmen Sukuna|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11362694}} appeared in Hida Province. He was defeated by {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|武振熊命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武振熊|1=Takefurukuma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546000}}, the ancestor of the Wani clan, who prayed for victory to the former Emperor Ōjin. Another theory is that it was established as a protective Hachiman shrine by [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Emperor Shōmu]] in various provinces. In the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the Kanamori clan, the local lords, protected the shrine as their [[:en:Ujigami|Ujigami]] and made the residents north of the [[:en:Miyagawa|Miyagawa (Gifu)]] their parishioners. In the Meiji period, it was renamed Hachiman Shrine and became a Prefectural Shrine in 1932. The name Sakurayama Hachimangu has been used since after World War II. == Facilities == The {{ill|Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall|lt=Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall|lt=Takayama festival Yatai Kaikan|ja|高山祭屋台会館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takayama festival Yatai Kaikan|qq=}} is located on the shrine grounds, where the festival floats used in the autumn Takayama festival are displayed throughout the year. File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref10s3s4020.jpg|Shrine gate File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref09n4110.jpg|Kotohira Shrine File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref07n4140.jpg|Inari Shrine File:Takayama_torii.jpg|Great Torii File:Sakurayama-hachiman_ninotorii.jpeg|Second Torii == Shrines within the Grounds == * Akiba Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|ja|火産霊神|1=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ}} * Tenman Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} * Inari Shrine ** Deities: {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲霊神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}}, {{ill|Matsunoo Shrine|ja|松尾神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsunoo Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 松尾大社|1=Matsunoo Taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q710302}} * Kotohira Shrine ** Deities: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}, [[:en:Emperor Sutoku|Emperor Sutoku]], {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少名彦名神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Terumae Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|浪速根子武振熊命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Cultural Properties == * Takayama The name Sakurayama Hachimangu has been used since after World War II. == Facilities == The {{ill|Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall|lt=Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall|lt=Takayama festival Yatai Kaikan|ja|高山祭屋台会館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takayama festival Yatai Kaikan|qq=}} is located on the shrine grounds, where the festival floats used in the autumn Takayama festival are displayed throughout the year. revision.comment: Bot: Add Wikidata has short description - based on Wikidata English short description
revision.text:
[[sn:commons:Category:Sakurayama-hachiman-gū]][[ja:桜山八幡宮]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11537980]] Wikidata name: Sakurayama Hachimangū [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:04, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sakurayama Hachimangu | image = [[File:Sakurayama-hachimangu Takayama Gifu pref04s3s3990.jpg|250px]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E}}}} | location = [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], [[:en:Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], Sakuramachi 178 | coordinates = {{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E|scale:30000}} | deity = [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] | type = Former Prefectural Shrine, [[Beppyo Shrine|Beppyo Shrine]], Kanpei-sha | annual_festival = [[:en:October 9|October 9]], [[:en:October 10|October 10]] (Takayama festival) | ceremonies = Shikinen Taisai }} '''Sakurayama Hachimangu''' (桜山八幡宮, officially '''櫻山八幡宮''') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], Japan. The autumn festival, along with the spring festival of {{ill|Hie Shrine (Takayama)|ja|日枝神社 (高山市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hie Shrine (Takayama)|1=Hie Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11509534}}, is known as the [[:en:Takayama Festival|Takayama festival]], which is designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazai/17768/mukei-minzoku/takayamamaturi.html |title=高山祭の屋台行事 |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2016-12-11}}. The main deity is the Hachiman deity, [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], and the enshrined deities include Atsuta Okami ({{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|1=Yamato Takeru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}}) and Kashi Okami ([[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]] and [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]]). == History == It is said that in the 65th year of the reign of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]], a bandit with two faces named {{ill|Ryomen Sukuna|ja|両面宿儺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryomen Sukuna|1=Ryōmen Sukuna|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11362694}} appeared in Hida Province. He was defeated by {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|武振熊命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武振熊|1=Takefurukuma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546000}}, the ancestor of the Wani clan, who prayed for victory to the former Emperor Ōjin. Another theory is that it was established as a protective Hachiman shrine by [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Emperor Shōmu]] in various provinces. In the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the Kanamori clan, the local lords, protected the shrine as their [[:en:Ujigami|Ujigami]] and made the residents north of the [[:en:Miyagawa|Miyagawa (Gifu)]] their parishioners. In the Meiji period, it was renamed Hachiman Shrine and became a Prefectural Shrine in 1932. The name Sakurayama Hachimangu has been used since after World War II. == Facilities == The {{ill|Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall|lt=Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall|lt=Takayama festival Yatai Kaikan|ja|高山祭屋台会館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takayama festival Yatai Kaikan|qq=}} is located on the shrine grounds, where the festival floats used in the autumn Takayama festival are displayed throughout the year. File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref10s3s4020.jpg|Shrine gate File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref09n4110.jpg|Kotohira Shrine File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref07n4140.jpg|Inari Shrine File:Takayama_torii.jpg|Great Torii File:Sakurayama-hachiman_ninotorii.jpeg|Second Torii == Shrines within the Grounds == * Akiba Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|ja|火産霊神|1=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ}} * Tenman Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} * Inari Shrine ** Deities: {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲霊神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}}, {{ill|Matsunoo Shrine|ja|松尾神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsunoo Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 松尾大社|1=Matsunoo Taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q710302}} * Kotohira Shrine ** Deities: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}, [[:en:Emperor Sutoku|Emperor Sutoku]], {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少名彦名神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Terumae Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|浪速根子武振熊命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Cultural Properties == * Takayama The name Sakurayama Hachimangu has been used since after World War II. == Facilities == The {{ill|Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall|lt=Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall|lt=Takayama festival Yatai Kaikan|ja|高山祭屋台会館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takayama festival Yatai Kaikan|qq=}} is located on the shrine grounds, where the festival floats used in the autumn Takayama festival are displayed throughout the year. [[sn:commons:Category:Sakurayama-hachiman-gū]][[ja:桜山八幡宮]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11537980]] Wikidata name: Sakurayama Hachimangū [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:04, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sakurayama Hachimangu | image = [[File:Sakurayama-hachimangu Takayama Gifu pref04s3s3990.jpg|250px]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E}}}} | location = [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], [[:en:Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], Sakuramachi 178 | coordinates = {{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E|scale:30000}} | deity = [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] | type = Former Prefectural Shrine, [[Beppyo Shrine|Beppyo Shrine]], Kanpei-sha | annual_festival = [[:en:October 9|October 9]], [[:en:October 10|October 10]] (Takayama festival) | ceremonies = Shikinen Taisai }} '''Sakurayama Hachimangu''' (桜山八幡宮, officially '''櫻山八幡宮''') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], Japan. The autumn festival, along with the spring festival of {{ill|Hie Shrine (Takayama)|ja|日枝神社 (高山市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hie Shrine (Takayama)|1=Hie Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11509534}}, is known as the [[:en:Takayama Festival|Takayama festival]], which is designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazai/17768/mukei-minzoku/takayamamaturi.html |title=高山祭の屋台行事 |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2016-12-11}}. The main deity is the Hachiman deity, [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], and the enshrined deities include Atsuta Okami ({{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|1=Yamato Takeru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}}) and Kashi Okami ([[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]] and [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]]). == History == It is said that in the 65th year of the reign of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]], a bandit with two faces named {{ill|Ryomen Sukuna|ja|両面宿儺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryomen Sukuna|1=Ryōmen Sukuna|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11362694}} appeared in Hida Province. He was defeated by {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|武振熊命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武振熊|1=Takefurukuma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546000}}, the ancestor of the Wani clan, who prayed for victory to the former Emperor Ōjin. Another theory is that it was established as a protective Hachiman shrine by [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Emperor Shōmu]] in various provinces. In the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the Kanamori clan, the local lords, protected the shrine as their [[:en:Ujigami|Ujigami]] and made the residents north of the [[:en:Miyagawa|Miyagawa (Gifu)]] their parishioners. In the Meiji period, it was renamed Hachiman Shrine and became a Prefectural Shrine in 1932. The name Sakurayama Hachimangu has been used since after World War II. == Facilities == The {{ill|Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall|lt=Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall|lt=Takayama festival Yatai Kaikan|ja|高山祭屋台会館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takayama festival Yatai Kaikan|qq=}} is located on the shrine grounds, where the festival floats used in the autumn Takayama festival are displayed throughout the year. Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|ja|火産霊神|1=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to The name Sakurayama Hachimangu has been used since after World War II. == Facilities == The {{ill|Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall|lt=Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall|lt=Takayama festival Yatai Kaikan|ja|高山祭屋台会館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takayama festival Yatai Kaikan|qq=}} is located on the shrine grounds, where the festival floats used in the autumn Takayama festival are displayed throughout the year. カグツチ}} * Tenman Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} * Inari Shrine ** Deities: Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}}, {{ill|Matsunoo Shrine|ja|松尾神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsunoo Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki invalid}} * Terumae Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|浪速根子武振熊命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Cultural Properties == * Takayama [[sn:commons:Category:Sakurayama-hachiman-gū]][[ja:桜山八幡宮]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11537980]] Wikidata name: Sakurayama Hachimangū [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:04, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sakurayama Hachimangu | image = [[File:Sakurayama-hachimangu Takayama Gifu pref04s3s3990.jpg|250px]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E}}}} | location = [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], [[:en:Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], Sakuramachi 178 | coordinates = {{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E|scale:30000}} | deity = [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] | type = Former Prefectural Shrine, [[Beppyo Shrine|Beppyo Shrine]], Kanpei-sha | annual_festival = [[:en:October 9|October 9]], [[:en:October 10|October 10]] (Takayama festival) | ceremonies = Shikinen Taisai }} '''Sakurayama Hachimangu''' (桜山八幡宮, officially '''櫻山八幡宮''') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], Japan. The autumn festival, along with the spring festival of {{ill|Hie Shrine (Takayama)|ja|日枝神社 (高山市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hie Shrine (Takayama)|1=Hie Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11509534}}, is known as the [[:en:Takayama Festival|Takayama festival]], which is designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazai/17768/mukei-minzoku/takayamamaturi.html |title=高山祭の屋台行事 |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2016-12-11}}. The main deity is the Hachiman deity, [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], and the enshrined deities include Atsuta Okami ({{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|1=Yamato Takeru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}}) and Kashi Okami ([[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]] and [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]]). == History == It is said that in the 65th year of the reign of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]], a bandit with two faces named {{ill|Ryomen Sukuna|ja|両面宿儺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryomen Sukuna|1=Ryōmen Sukuna|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11362694}} appeared in Hida Province. He was defeated by {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|武振熊命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武振熊|1=Takefurukuma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546000}}, the ancestor of the Wani clan, who prayed for victory to the former Emperor Ōjin. Another theory is that it was established as a protective Hachiman shrine by [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Emperor Shōmu]] in various provinces. In the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the Kanamori clan, the local lords, protected the shrine as their [[:en:Ujigami|Ujigami]] and made the residents north of the [[:en:Miyagawa|Miyagawa (Gifu)]] their parishioners. In the Meiji period, it was renamed Hachiman Shrine and became a Prefectural Shrine in 1932. The name Sakurayama Hachimangu has been used since after World War II. == Facilities == The {{ill|Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall|lt=Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall|lt=Takayama festival Yatai Kaikan|ja|高山祭屋台会館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takayama festival Yatai Kaikan|qq=}} is located on the shrine grounds, where the festival floats used in the autumn Takayama festival are displayed throughout the year. File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref10s3s4020.jpg|Shrine gate File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref09n4110.jpg|Kotohira Shrine File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref07n4140.jpg|Inari Shrine File:Takayama_torii.jpg|Great Torii File:Sakurayama-hachiman_ninotorii.jpeg|Second Torii == Shrines within the Grounds == * Akiba Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|ja|火産霊神|1=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ}} * Tenman Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} * Inari Shrine ** Deities: {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲霊神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}}, {{ill|Matsunoo Shrine|ja|松尾神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsunoo Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 松尾大社|1=Matsunoo Taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q710302}} * Kotohira Shrine ** Deities: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}, [[:en:Emperor Sutoku|Emperor Sutoku]], {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少名彦名神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Terumae Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|浪速根子武振熊命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Cultural Properties == * Takayama festival Floats Event (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * Mikoshi and Mikoshi Storehouse of Hachiman Shrine (Municipal Tangible Folk Cultural Property){{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/kurashi/1000021/1000119/1000847/1000915/1004125.html|title=民俗文化財 |publisher=高山市 |accessdate=2016-12-11}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Sakurayama-hachiman-gū}} * [http://www.sakurayamahachiman.jimdo.com/ Sakurayama Hachimangu] {{shinto-stub}} {{Hachiman Faith}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated In the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the Kanamori clan, the local lords, protected the shrine as their [[:en:Ujigami|Ujigami]] and made the residents north of the [[:en:Miyagawa|Miyagawa (Gifu)]] their parishioners. In the Meiji period, it was renamed Hachiman Shrine and became a Prefectural Shrine in 1932. The name Sakurayama Hachimangu has been used since after World War II. == Facilities == The {{ill|Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall|lt=Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall|lt=Takayama festival Yatai Kaikan|ja|高山祭屋台会館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takayama festival Yatai Kaikan|qq=}} is located on the shrine grounds, where the festival floats used in the autumn Takayama festival are displayed throughout the year. [[commons:Category:Sakurayama-hachiman-gū]][[ja:桜山八幡宮]] [[commons:Category:Sakurayama-hachiman-gū]][[ja:桜山八幡宮]] [[sn:commons:Category:Sakurayama-hachiman-gū]][[ja:桜山八幡宮]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11537980]] Wikidata name: Sakurayama Hachimangū [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:04, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sakurayama Hachimangu | image = [[File:Sakurayama-hachimangu Takayama Gifu pref04s3s3990.jpg|250px]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E}}}} | location = [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], [[:en:Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], Sakuramachi 178 | coordinates = {{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E|scale:30000}} | deity = [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] | type = Former Prefectural Shrine, [[Beppyo Shrine|Beppyo Shrine]], Kanpei-sha | annual_festival = [[:en:October 9|October 9]], [[:en:October 10|October 10]] (Takayama festival) | ceremonies = Shikinen Taisai }} '''Sakurayama Hachimangu''' (桜山八幡宮, officially '''櫻山八幡宮''') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], Japan. The autumn festival, along with the spring festival of {{ill|Hie Shrine (Takayama)|ja|日枝神社 (高山市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hie Shrine (Takayama)|1=Hie Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11509534}}, is known as the [[:en:Takayama Festival|Takayama festival]], which is designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazai/17768/mukei-minzoku/takayamamaturi.html |title=高山祭の屋台行事 |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2016-12-11}}. The main deity is the Hachiman deity, [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], and the enshrined deities include Atsuta Okami ({{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|1=Yamato Takeru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}}) and Kashi Okami ([[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]] and [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]]). == History == It is said that in the 65th year of the reign of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]], a bandit with two faces named {{ill|Ryomen Sukuna|ja|両面宿儺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryomen Sukuna|1=Ryōmen Sukuna|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11362694}} appeared in Hida Province. He was defeated by {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|武振熊命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武振熊|1=Takefurukuma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546000}}, the ancestor of the Wani clan, who prayed for victory to the former Emperor Ōjin. Another theory is that it was established as a protective Hachiman shrine by [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Emperor Shōmu]] in various provinces. In the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the Kanamori clan, the local lords, protected the shrine as their [[:en:Ujigami|Ujigami]] and made the residents north of the [[:en:Miyagawa|Miyagawa (Gifu)]] their parishioners. In the Meiji period, it was renamed Hachiman Shrine and became a Prefectural Shrine in 1932. The name Sakurayama Hachimangu has been used since after World War II. == Facilities == The {{ill|Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall|lt=Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall|lt=Takayama festival Yatai Kaikan|ja|高山祭屋台会館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takayama festival Yatai Kaikan|qq=}} is located on the shrine grounds, where the festival floats used in the autumn Takayama festival are displayed throughout the year. File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref07n4140.jpg|Inari Shrine File:Takayama_torii.jpg|Great Torii File:Sakurayama-hachiman_ninotorii.jpeg|Second Torii == Shrines within the Grounds == * Akiba Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|ja|火産霊神|1=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} * Inari Shrine ** Deities: Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}}, {{ill|Matsunoo Shrine|ja|松尾神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsunoo Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 松尾大社|1=Matsunoo Taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q710302}} * Kotohira Shrine ** Deities: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}, [[:en:Emperor Sutoku|Emperor Sutoku]], Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E}}}} | location = [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], [[:en:Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], Sakuramachi 178 | coordinates = {{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E|scale:30000}} | deity = [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]] | type = Former Prefectural Shrine, [[Beppyo Shrine|Beppyo Shrine]], Kanpei-sha | annual_festival = [[:en:October 9|October 9]], [[:en:October 10|October 10]] (Takayama festival) | ceremonies = Shikinen Taisai }} '''Sakurayama Hachimangu''' (桜山八幡宮, officially '''櫻山八幡宮''') is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Takayama, Gifu|Takayama]], [[:en:Gifu Prefecture|Gifu]], Japan. The autumn festival, along with the spring festival of {{ill|Hie Shrine (Takayama)|ja|日枝神社 (高山市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hie Shrine (Takayama)|1=Hie Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11509534}}, is known as the [[:en:Takayama Festival|Takayama festival]], which is designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazai/17768/mukei-minzoku/takayamamaturi.html |title=高山祭の屋台行事 |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2016-12-11}}. The main deity is the Hachiman deity, [[:en:Emperor Ōjin|Emperor Ōjin]], and the enshrined deities include Atsuta Okami ({{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|1=Yamato Takeru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}}) and Kashi Okami ([[:en:Emperor Chūai|Emperor Chūai]] and [[:en:Empress Jingū|Empress Jingū]]). == History == It is said that in the 65th year of the reign of [[:en:Emperor Nintoku|Emperor Nintoku]], a bandit with two faces named {{ill|Ryomen Sukuna|ja|両面宿儺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryomen Sukuna|1=Ryōmen Sukuna|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11362694}} appeared in Hida Province. He was defeated by {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|武振熊命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武振熊|1=Takefurukuma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546000}}, the ancestor of the Wani clan, who prayed for victory to the former Emperor Ōjin. Another theory is that it was established as a protective Hachiman shrine by [[:en:Emperor Shōmu|Emperor Shōmu]] in various provinces. In the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the Kanamori clan, the local lords, protected the shrine as their [[:en:Ujigami|Ujigami]] and made the residents north of the [[:en:Miyagawa|Miyagawa (Gifu)]] their parishioners. In the Meiji period, it was renamed Hachiman Shrine and became a Prefectural Shrine in 1932. The name Sakurayama Hachimangu has been used since after World War II. == Facilities == The {{ill|Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall|lt=Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall|lt=Takayama festival Yatai Kaikan|ja|高山祭屋台会館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takayama festival Yatai Kaikan|qq=}} is located on the shrine grounds, where the festival floats used in the autumn Takayama festival are displayed throughout the year. File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref07n4140.jpg|Inari Shrine File:Takayama_torii.jpg|Great Torii File:Sakurayama-hachiman_ninotorii.jpeg|Second Torii == Shrines within the Grounds == * Akiba Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|ja|火産霊神|1=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ}} * Tenman Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} * Inari Shrine ** Deities: Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}}, {{ill|Matsunoo Shrine|ja|松尾神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsunoo Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少名彦名神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Terumae Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|浪速根子武振熊命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Cultural Properties == * Takayama festival Floats Event (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * Mikoshi and Mikoshi Storehouse of Hachiman Shrine (Municipal Tangible Folk Cultural Property){{Cite web|和書|date= revision.comment: v25: Convert interwiki links [[:en:...]] to {{ill}} templates
revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Sakurayama-hachiman-gū]][[ja:桜山八幡宮]] [[co:commons:Category:Sakurayama-hachiman-gū]][[ja:桜山八幡宮]] [[sn:commons:Category:Sakurayama-hachiman-gū]][[ja:桜山八幡宮]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11537980]] Wikidata name: Sakurayama Hachimangū [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:04, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sakurayama Hachimangu | image = [[File:Sakurayama-hachimangu Takayama Gifu pref04s3s3990.jpg|250px]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu}}, {{ill|Takayama, Gifu|en|Takayama, Gifu|WD=Q622594|lt=Takayama}}, Sakuramachi 178 | coordinates = {{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E|scale:30000}} | deity = {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin}} | type = Former Prefectural Shrine, [[Beppyo Shrine|Beppyo Shrine]], Kanpei-sha | annual_festival = {{ill|October 9|en|October 9|WD=Q2923|lt=October 9}}, {{ill|October 10|en|October 10|WD=Q2921|lt=October 10}} (Takayama festival) | ceremonies = Shikinen Taisai }} '''Sakurayama Hachimangu''' (桜山八幡宮, officially '''櫻山八幡宮''') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Takayama, Gifu|en|Takayama, Gifu|WD=Q622594|lt=Takayama}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu}}, Japan. The autumn festival, along with the spring festival of {{ill|Hie Shrine (Takayama)|ja|日枝神社 (高山市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hie Shrine (Takayama)|1=Hie Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11509534}}, is known as the {{ill|Takayama Festival|en|Takayama Festival|WD=Q7677467|lt=Takayama festival}}, which is designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazai/17768/mukei-minzoku/takayamamaturi.html |title=高山祭の屋台行事 |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2016-12-11}}. The main deity is the Hachiman deity, {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin}}, and the enshrined deities include Atsuta Okami ({{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|1=Yamato Takeru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}}) and Kashi Okami ({{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|WD=Q179971|lt=Emperor Chūai}} and {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū}}). == History == It is said that in the 65th year of the reign of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku}}, a bandit with two faces named {{ill|Ryomen Sukuna|ja|両面宿儺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryomen Sukuna|1=Ryōmen Sukuna|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11362694}} appeared in Hida Province. He was defeated by {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|武振熊命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武振熊|1=Takefurukuma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546000}}, the ancestor of the Wani clan, who prayed for victory to the former Emperor Ōjin. Another theory is that it was established as a protective Hachiman shrine by {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|en|Emperor Shōmu|WD=Q349263|lt=Emperor Shōmu}} in various provinces. In the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, the Kanamori clan, the local lords, protected the shrine as their {{ill|Ujigami|en|Ujigami|WD=Q767655|lt=Ujigami}} and made the residents north of the {{ill|Miyagawa|en|Miyagawa|WD=Q1665818|lt=Miyagawa (Gifu)}} their parishioners. In the Meiji period, it was renamed Hachiman Shrine and became a Prefectural Shrine in 1932. The name Sakurayama Hachimangu has been used since after World War II. == Facilities == The {{ill|Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall|lt=Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall|lt=Takayama festival Yatai Kaikan|ja|高山祭屋台会館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takayama festival Yatai Kaikan|qq=}} is located on the shrine grounds, where the festival floats used in the autumn Takayama festival are displayed throughout the year. File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref10s3s4020.jpg|Shrine gate File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref09n4110.jpg|Kotohira Shrine File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref07n4140.jpg|Inari Shrine File:Takayama_torii.jpg|Great Torii File:Sakurayama-hachiman_ninotorii.jpeg|Second Torii == Shrines within the Grounds == * Akiba Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|ja|火産霊神|1=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ}} * Tenman Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} * Inari Shrine ** Deities: {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲霊神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}}, {{ill|Matsunoo Shrine|ja|松尾神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsunoo Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 松尾大社|1=Matsunoo Taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q710302}} * Kotohira Shrine ** Deities: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}, {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|en|Emperor Sutoku|WD=Q349287|lt=Emperor Sutoku}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少名彦名神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Terumae Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|浪速根子武振熊命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Cultural Properties == * Takayama festival Floats Event (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * Mikoshi and Mikoshi Storehouse of Hachiman Shrine (Municipal Tangible Folk Cultural Property){{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/kurashi/1000021/1000119/1000847/1000915/1004125.html|title=民俗文化財 |publisher=高山市 |accessdate=2016-12-11}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Sakurayama-hachiman-gū}} * [http://www.sakurayamahachiman.jimdo.com/ Sakurayama Hachimangu] {{shinto-stub}} {{Hachiman Faith}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|桜山八幡宮|version=102411526|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:桜山八幡宮]] {{Commons category|Sakurayama-hachiman-gū}} {{wikidata link|Q11537980}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 2]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Hachiman shrines]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Municipality Designated Tangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Shinto]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Takayama, Gifu]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

revision.comment: imported only latest revision from https://archive.org/details/miraheze-wiki-shintowiki-20251218 to avoid excessive disk usage, comment on the revision was Removing {{afc comment}} templates
revision.text:
[[co:commons:Category:Sakurayama-hachiman-gū]][[ja:桜山八幡宮]] [[co:commons:Category:Sakurayama-hachiman-gū]][[ja:桜山八幡宮]] [[sn:commons:Category:Sakurayama-hachiman-gū]][[ja:桜山八幡宮]] {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sakurayama Hachimangu | image = [[File:Sakurayama-hachimangu Takayama Gifu pref04s3s3990.jpg|250px]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu}}, {{ill|Takayama, Gifu|en|Takayama, Gifu|WD=Q622594|lt=Takayama}}, Sakuramachi 178 | coordinates = {{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E|scale:30000}} | deity = {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin}} | type = Former Prefectural Shrine, [[Beppyo Shrine|Beppyo Shrine]], Kanpei-sha | annual_festival = {{ill|October 9|en|October 9|WD=Q2923|lt=October 9}}, {{ill|October 10|en|October 10|WD=Q2921|lt=October 10}} (Takayama festival) | ceremonies = Shikinen Taisai }} '''Sakurayama Hachimangu''' (桜山八幡宮, officially '''櫻山八幡宮''') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Takayama, Gifu|en|Takayama, Gifu|WD=Q622594|lt=Takayama}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu}}, Japan. The autumn festival, along with the spring festival of {{ill|Hie Shrine (Takayama)|ja|日枝神社 (高山市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hie Shrine (Takayama)|1=Hie Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11509534}}, is known as the {{ill|Takayama Festival|en|Takayama Festival|WD=Q7677467|lt=Takayama festival}}, which is designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazai/17768/mukei-minzoku/takayamamaturi.html |title=高山祭の屋台行事 |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2016-12-11}}. The main deity is the Hachiman deity, {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin}}, and the enshrined deities include Atsuta Okami ({{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|1=Yamato Takeru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}}) and Kashi Okami ({{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|WD=Q179971|lt=Emperor Chūai}} and {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū}}). == History == It is said that in the 65th year of the reign of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku}}, a bandit with two faces named {{ill|Ryomen Sukuna|ja|両面宿儺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryomen Sukuna|1=Ryōmen Sukuna|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11362694}} appeared in Hida Province. He was defeated by {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|武振熊命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武振熊|1=Takefurukuma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546000}}, the ancestor of the Wani clan, who prayed for victory to the former Emperor Ōjin. Another theory is that it was established as a protective Hachiman shrine by {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|en|Emperor Shōmu|WD=Q349263|lt=Emperor Shōmu}} in various provinces. In the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, the Kanamori clan, the local lords, protected the shrine as their {{ill|Ujigami|en|Ujigami|WD=Q767655|lt=Ujigami}} and made the residents north of the {{ill|Miyagawa|en|Miyagawa|WD=Q1665818|lt=Miyagawa (Gifu)}} their parishioners. In the Meiji period, it was renamed Hachiman Shrine and became a Prefectural Shrine in 1932. The name Sakurayama Hachimangu has been used since after World War II. == Facilities == The {{ill|Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall|lt=Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall|lt=Takayama festival Yatai Kaikan|ja|高山祭屋台会館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takayama festival Yatai Kaikan|qq=}} is located on the shrine grounds, where the festival floats used in the autumn Takayama festival are displayed throughout the year. File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref10s3s4020.jpg|Shrine gate File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref09n4110.jpg|Kotohira Shrine File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref07n4140.jpg|Inari Shrine File:Takayama_torii.jpg|Great Torii File:Sakurayama-hachiman_ninotorii.jpeg|Second Torii == Shrines within the Grounds == * Akiba Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|ja|火産霊神|1=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to カグツチ}} * Tenman Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|ja|Sugawara no Michizane|1=Sugawara no Michizane|2=ja|3=菅原道真|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sugawara no Michizane|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q382005}} * Inari Shrine ** Deities: {{ill|Ukanomitama|ja|倉稲霊神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ukanomitama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko Ōkami|lt=Sarutahiko|ja|猿田彦神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}}, {{ill|Matsunoo Shrine|ja|松尾神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsunoo Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 松尾大社|1=Matsunoo Taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q710302}} * Kotohira Shrine ** Deities: {{ill|Ōmononushi|ja|大物主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmononushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大物主}}, {{ill|Emperor Sutoku|en|Emperor Sutoku|WD=Q349287|lt=Emperor Sutoku}}, {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大己貴神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}, {{ill|Sukunabikona|ja|少名彦名神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunabikona|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * Terumae Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|浪速根子武振熊命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Cultural Properties == * Takayama festival Floats Event (Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property) * Mikoshi and Mikoshi Storehouse of Hachiman Shrine (Municipal Tangible Folk Cultural Property){{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.city.takayama.lg.jp/kurashi/1000021/1000119/1000847/1000915/1004125.html|title=民俗文化財 |publisher=高山市 |accessdate=2016-12-11}} == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Sakurayama-hachiman-gū}} * [http://www.sakurayamahachiman.jimdo.com/ Sakurayama Hachimangu] {{shinto-stub}} {{Hachiman Faith}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{translated page|ja|桜山八幡宮|version=102411526|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:桜山八幡宮]] {{Commons category|Sakurayama-hachiman-gū}} {{wikidata link|Q11537980}} [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] The autumn festival, along with the spring festival of {{ill|Hie Shrine (Takayama)|ja|日枝神社 (高山市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hie Shrine (Takayama)|1=Hie Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11509534}}, is known as the {{ill|Takayama Festival|en|Takayama Festival|WD=Q7677467|lt=Takayama festival|1=Takayama Festival}}, which is designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazai/17768/mukei-minzoku/takayamamaturi.html |title=高山祭の屋台行事 |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2016-12-11}}. The main deity is the Hachiman deity, {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}}, and the enshrined deities include Atsuta Okami ({{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|1=Yamato Takeru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}}) and Kashi Okami ({{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|WD=Q179971|lt=Emperor Chūai|1=Emperor Chūai}} and {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}}). == History == It is said that in the 65th year of the reign of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku|1=Emperor Nintoku}}, a bandit with two faces named {{ill|Ryomen Sukuna|ja|両面宿儺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryomen Sukuna|1=Ryōmen Sukuna|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11362694}} appeared in Hida Province. He was defeated by {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|武振熊命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武振熊|1=Takefurukuma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546000}}, the ancestor of the Wani clan, who prayed for victory to the former Emperor Ōjin. Another theory is that it was established as a protective Hachiman shrine by {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|en|Emperor Shōmu|WD=Q349263|lt=Emperor Shōmu|1=Emperor Shōmu}} in various provinces. In the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, the Kanamori clan, the local lords, protected the shrine as their {{ill|Ujigami|en|Ujigami|WD=Q767655|lt=Ujigami|1=Ujigami}} and made the residents north of the {{ill|Miyagawa|en|Miyagawa|WD=Q1665818|lt=Miyagawa (Gifu)|1=Miyagawa}} their parishioners. In the Meiji period, it was renamed Hachiman Shrine and became a Prefectural Shrine in 1932. The name Sakurayama Hachimangu has been used since after World War II. == Facilities == The {{ill|Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall|lt=Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall|lt=Takayama festival Yatai Kaikan|ja|高山祭屋台会館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takayama festival Yatai Kaikan|qq=}} is located on the shrine grounds, where the festival floats used in the autumn Takayama festival are displayed throughout the year. [[co:commons:Category:Sakurayama-hachiman-gū]][[ja:桜山八幡宮]] [[co:commons:Category:Sakurayama-hachiman-gū]][[ja:桜山八幡宮]] [[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Sakurayama-hachiman-gū]][[ja:桜山八幡宮]] {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Sakurayama Hachimangu | image = [[File:Sakurayama-hachimangu Takayama Gifu pref04s3s3990.jpg|250px]]
Haiden {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E}}}} | location = {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu|1=Gifu Prefecture}}, {{ill|Takayama, Gifu|en|Takayama, Gifu|WD=Q622594|lt=Takayama|1=Takayama, Gifu}}, Sakuramachi 178 | coordinates = {{coord|36|8|53.5|N|137|15|39.2|E|scale:30000}} | deity = {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}} | type = Former Prefectural Shrine, [[Beppyo Shrine|Beppyo Shrine]], Kanpei-sha | annual_festival = {{ill|October 9|en|October 9|WD=Q2923|lt=October 9|1=October 9}}, {{ill|October 10|en|October 10|WD=Q2921|lt=October 10|1=October 10}} (Takayama festival) | ceremonies = Shikinen Taisai }} '''Sakurayama Hachimangu''' (桜山八幡宮, officially '''櫻山八幡宮''') is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Takayama, Gifu|en|Takayama, Gifu|WD=Q622594|lt=Takayama|1=Takayama, Gifu}}, {{ill|Gifu Prefecture|en|Gifu Prefecture|WD=Q131277|lt=Gifu|1=Gifu Prefecture}}, Japan. The autumn festival, along with the spring festival of {{ill|Hie Shrine (Takayama)|ja|日枝神社 (高山市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hie Shrine (Takayama)|1=Hie Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11509534}}, is known as the {{ill|Takayama Festival|en|Takayama Festival|WD=Q7677467|lt=Takayama festival|1=Takayama Festival}}, which is designated as an Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property of Japan{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.pref.gifu.lg.jp/kyoiku/bunka/bunkazai/17768/mukei-minzoku/takayamamaturi.html |title=高山祭の屋台行事 |publisher=岐阜県 |accessdate=2016-12-11}}. The main deity is the Hachiman deity, {{ill|Emperor Ōjin|en|Emperor Ōjin|WD=Q317997|lt=Emperor Ōjin|1=Emperor Ōjin}}, and the enshrined deities include Atsuta Okami ({{ill|Yamato Takeru|ja|日本武尊命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|1=Yamato Takeru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q461258}}) and Kashi Okami ({{ill|Emperor Chūai|en|Emperor Chūai|WD=Q179971|lt=Emperor Chūai|1=Emperor Chūai}} and {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|WD=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}}). == History == It is said that in the 65th year of the reign of {{ill|Emperor Nintoku|en|Emperor Nintoku|WD=Q313119|lt=Emperor Nintoku|1=Emperor Nintoku}}, a bandit with two faces named {{ill|Ryomen Sukuna|ja|両面宿儺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryomen Sukuna|1=Ryōmen Sukuna|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11362694}} appeared in Hida Province. He was defeated by {{ill|Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja|武振熊命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takefurukuma no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 武振熊|1=Takefurukuma|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546000}}, the ancestor of the Wani clan, who prayed for victory to the former Emperor Ōjin. Another theory is that it was established as a protective Hachiman shrine by {{ill|Emperor Shōmu|en|Emperor Shōmu|WD=Q349263|lt=Emperor Shōmu|1=Emperor Shōmu}} in various provinces. In the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, the Kanamori clan, the local lords, protected the shrine as their {{ill|Ujigami|en|Ujigami|WD=Q767655|lt=Ujigami|1=Ujigami}} and made the residents north of the {{ill|Miyagawa|en|Miyagawa|WD=Q1665818|lt=Miyagawa (Gifu)|1=Miyagawa}} their parishioners. In the Meiji period, it was renamed Hachiman Shrine and became a Prefectural Shrine in 1932. The name Sakurayama Hachimangu has been used since after World War II. == Facilities == The {{ill|Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall|lt=Takayama Festival Float Exhibition Hall|lt=Takayama festival Yatai Kaikan|ja|高山祭屋台会館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takayama festival Yatai Kaikan|qq=}} is located on the shrine grounds, where the festival floats used in the autumn Takayama festival are displayed throughout the year. File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref10s3s4020.jpg|Shrine gate File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref09n4110.jpg|Kotohira Shrine File:Sakurayama-hachimangu_Takayama_Gifu_pref07n4140.jpg|Inari Shrine File:Takayama_torii.jpg|Great Torii File:Sakurayama-hachiman_ninotorii.jpeg|Second Torii == Shrines within the Grounds == * Akiba Shrine ** deity: {{ill|Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|ja|火産霊神|1=Kagu-tsuchi|lt=Kagutsuchi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|13=User:Immanuelle/Kagutsuchi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to redirects to 松尾大社|1=Matsunoo Taisha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q710302}} * Kotohira Shrine ** Deities:
